text: SECTION 4 – PAYMENT
notes: Contract section heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall comply with all applicable laws, regulations and permit conditions throughout the Contract.
notes: General regulatory compliance; standard obligation.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall execute the Works in accordance with the Contract and the Engineer's instructions.
notes: Fundamental contractor obligation; standard.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer's obligation to provide information on time is a condition precedent to the Contractor's obligation to maintain the programme; late information shall entitle the Contractor to an extension of time.
notes: Information provision as condition precedent; strong protection where employer delays drawings or instructions.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Employer may withhold payment of any amount due pending resolution of any dispute, without serving a pay less notice.
notes: Payment withholding without pay less notice undermines NEC4's payment transparency mechanisms.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, a compensation event is not notified if the Employer or the Contractor did not give an early warning of the event when they could have done so.
notes: Mutual early warning obligation; applies to both parties but in practice limits contractor CEs.

text: SECTION 10: TERMINATION
notes: Termination section heading.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Architect's decision on any matter concerning the quality or quantity of the Works shall be final and binding and shall not be subject to review in any dispute resolution proceedings.
notes: Ousts contractor's right to challenge Architect's decisions; contrary to HGCRA principles.

text: Minimum chargeable hrs is 144 hrs per month.
notes: Clause creates significant risk for subcontractor; one-sided in favour of contractor/employer.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall retain the right to remove from Site any equipment, materials and temporary works paid for by the Contractor at any time.
notes: Retention of title and removal right; protects contractor's assets against employer default.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall provide product warranties from all specialist suppliers in the Employer's name for a minimum of 10 years.
notes: Third-party warranty assignment obligation; requires supplier co-operation.

text: The parties agree that any amounts due to the Contractor may be set off against any claim the Employer may have against the Contractor under any other contract between the parties.
notes: Cross-contract set-off right; allows employer to use disputes on other contracts to withhold current payments.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall submit a cashflow forecast with each payment application to assist the Employer with funding arrangements.
notes: Cashflow forecasting obligation; transparent but creates administrative burden.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall comply with all applicable laws and regulations in carrying out the Works.
notes: General compliance obligation; standard and uncontroversial.

text: In accordance with industry practice, where changes in Laws after the Base Date increase the Contractor's Costs, the Contract Price shall be adjusted accordingly.
notes: Change-in-law risk on employer; contractor protected from sovereign risk after Base Date.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Architect shall issue Interim Payment Certificates at the intervals stated in the Contract Particulars, and the Employer shall pay within 14 days of the due date.
notes: Clear payment timeline; HGCRA compliant.

text: The Contractor shall provide a performance guarantee of 5% of the Contract Sum from an acceptable financial institution within 14 days of Contract execution.
notes: 10% performance guarantee; market norm in Zimbabwe but creates liquidity demand.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, where the Employer fails to issue a payment notice or pay less notice by the prescribed date, the sum stated in the Contractor's payment application shall become the sum due.
notes: Default payment mechanism under HGCRA; contractor's application becomes notified sum if employer fails to serve notice.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor accepts all risk for errors in the Scope or Works Information provided by the Employer and shall not be entitled to any adjustment of the Prices or Completion Date.
notes: Employer's scope errors become contractor's risk; contrary to CE 60.1(1).

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, payment shall only be made quarterly; no interim payments shall be due during the Contract Period regardless of the value of work executed.
notes: Quarterly payment cycle; creates severe cash flow hardship for contractor, particularly in Zimbabwe's inflation environment.

text: In accordance with industry practice, any claim not submitted within 14 days of the cause of action arising is time-barred and shall not be considered.
notes: 14-day claim bar is impractically short; causes loss of legitimate claims on complex projects.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall rectify any defect notified during the Defects Liability Period within 3 days of notification, failing which the Employer may engage others and recover costs at a premium rate of 150% of the cost incurred.
notes: Impossibly short rectification window; premium rate recovery is punitive and unjustifiable.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall register the project with the Zimbabwe Revenue Authority and comply with all ZIMRA withholding tax obligations.
notes: Tax compliance obligation; standard for Zimbabwe construction projects.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall submit a monthly cost report showing actual versus forecast expenditure, certified by the Contractor's quantity surveyor.
notes: Cost reporting obligation; transparency tool.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall keep a daily site diary recording weather conditions, labour strength, materials received and work carried out.
notes: Site record-keeping obligation; standard practice.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall be liable for delay damages at the stated rate from the scheduled Completion Date, whether or not the delay was caused partly by the Employer.
notes: LADs run even where employer is partly responsible; no concurrent delay apportionment.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor's share of savings achieved below the Target Cost shall be calculated at 50% of the underspend.
notes: 50% gainshare on Option C target; significant upside for efficient contractor.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contract shall be governed by the law of Zimbabwe and any disputes shall be subject to the jurisdiction of Zimbabwean courts.
notes: Governing law and jurisdiction; standard for Zimbabwe contracts.

text: SIGNED for and on behalf of THE CONTRACTOR by a duly authorised representative:
notes: Execution signature block.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor is entitled to a compensation event where the Project Manager gives an instruction to stop work, and the stop lasts more than 13 weeks, and no further instruction is given.
notes: Prolonged stop-work triggers CE; protects contractor's standing costs.

text: The Employer shall make an Advance Payment of 5% of the Accepted Contract Amount within 21 days of receipt of the Performance Security.
notes: FIDIC advance payment at 10%; standard and contractor-supportive.

text: Words importing the singular include the plural and vice versa; words importing any gender include all genders.
notes: Standard interpretation provision.

text: In accordance with industry practice, any acceleration instruction by the Employer shall constitute a variation entitling the Contractor to additional payment for overtime, additional resources and loss of productivity.
notes: Acceleration as a paid variation; prevents employer from demanding acceleration without compensation.

text: 'Contract Price' means the sum stated in the Letter of Acceptance, subject to adjustment in accordance with this Contract.
notes: Standard contract price definition.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall submit a detailed breakdown of any claim for additional Cost within 84 days of the claim notice.
notes: 84-day detailed claim submission; reasonable period for complex cost substantiation.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, certificates issued under this Contract shall not be taken as evidence that the Works have been completed in accordance with the Contract.
notes: Standard certification disclaimer.

text: SECTION 10: TERMINATION
notes: Termination section heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor's liability for latent defects shall survive the Defects Certificate and continue for 6 years from the date of Practical Completion.
notes: Latent defect liability period; standard under Limitation Act 1980.

text: The Contractor shall submit as-built drawings within 48 hours of Practical Completion.
notes: As-built record submission obligation; standard completion deliverable.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, certificates issued under this Contract shall not be taken as evidence that the Works have been completed in accordance with the Contract.
notes: Standard certification disclaimer.

text: 'Time for Completion' means the time for completing the Works or a Section as stated in the Contract Data, adjusted under Sub-Clause 8.5.
notes: Standard FIDIC completion time definition.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, where the exchange rate moves by more than 5% against the Contractor between the Base Date and the payment date, the Contract Price shall be adjusted by the difference.
notes: Exchange rate fluctuation protection; strong safeguard for international contracts in Zimbabwe.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall provide all Equipment and Temporary Works needed for execution of the Works.
notes: Equipment provision obligation; standard.

text: It is hereby agreed that disputes shall first be referred to a technical expert for determination within 30 days; expert determination shall be binding unless overturned by arbitration.
notes: Expert determination as first-tier; efficient mechanism for technical disputes.

text: If the Contractor becomes insolvent, the Employer shall have the right to complete the Works using the Contractor's workforce and equipment without further payment.
notes: Employer's step-in right using contractor's resources without additional payment; commercially punitive.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, all payments under this Contract shall be made in United States Dollars; any payment made in RTGS or ZiG currency shall not discharge the Contractor's payment obligation.
notes: USD-only payment in reverse; in context where contractor is employer, this shifts forex risk onto subcontractor.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor accepts the risk of legal changes to the law of any country applicable to the Contractor's work and no compensation event arises from change in law.
notes: Change-in-law risk transferred to contractor; contrary to standard NEC4 CE 60.1(18).

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Employer shall give the Contractor possession of the Site on the Date of Possession stated in the Contract Particulars.
notes: Standard possession provision; balanced obligation on employer.

text: 4. SUB-CONTRACTOR'S PLANT, TOOLS AND EQUIPMENT
notes: Heading or procedural clause; no material risk allocation.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor is entitled to Exceptional Event relief for epidemics, natural catastrophes, wars, embargoes and acts of government.
notes: Broad exceptional event relief including government action; relevant for Zimbabwe sovereign risk.

text: The Contractor shall provide a performance guarantee of 5% of the Contract Sum from an acceptable financial institution within 14 days of Contract execution.
notes: 10% performance guarantee; market norm in Zimbabwe but creates liquidity demand.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall be liable for liquidated damages at the rate of USD 5,000 per day for every day of delay beyond the Completion Date, without any limit on the aggregate amount recoverable.
notes: Uncapped daily LADs in USD; creates severe and uninsurable financial exposure for Zimbabwean contractors.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall permit the Auditor General or any authorised representative to inspect all financial records relating to this Contract.
notes: Government audit access right; standard on public sector Zimbabwe contracts.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer may grant an extension of time for delays caused by force majeure events including acts of God, floods, civil unrest, government action or unavailability of foreign currency.
notes: Force majeure definition includes Zimbabwe-specific risks (forex, civil unrest); balanced provision.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, either party may apply to the High Court of Zimbabwe for urgent interim relief pending arbitration.
notes: Preservation of urgent court relief; balanced dispute mechanism.

text: In accordance with industry practice, hyperinflationary conditions that render the Contract commercially impossible to perform shall constitute a force majeure event.
notes: Hyperinflation as FM event; Zimbabwe-specific protection for contractor.

text: The Contractor reserves the right to demand the removal of any employee or person under the Sub-Contractor's control who fails to adhere to any order given by the Contractor and/or any sign(s) relating to safety and/or health.
notes: Clause creates significant risk for subcontractor; one-sided in favour of contractor/employer.

text: The Employer may terminate the Contract for convenience by giving 42 days' notice; the Contractor shall be entitled only to Cost incurred plus 3% of the Cost as profit.
notes: Termination for convenience with limited profit entitlement; 3% profit capped regardless of anticipated margin.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall retain the right to remove from Site any equipment, materials and temporary works paid for by the Contractor at any time.
notes: Retention of title and removal right; protects contractor's assets against employer default.

text: In accordance with industry practice, where the Employer fails to provide something they are to provide by the date stated, the Contractor is entitled to a compensation event for delay and additional cost.
notes: Employer supply chain risk passed back to employer; contractor protected for late employer-furnished items.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor provides a 10-year structural warranty for all reinforced concrete works in accordance with the relevant British Standards.
notes: Long-form structural warranty; common on residential and commercial developments.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor's share of savings achieved below the Target Cost shall be calculated at 50% of the underspend.
notes: 50% gainshare on Option C target; significant upside for efficient contractor.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, any variation instructed by the Employer shall be valued by the Employer's Quantity Surveyor, whose valuation shall be final and binding on the Contractor.
notes: Removes contractor's right to challenge variation valuations through dispute resolution.

text: The Contract shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the law of England and Wales.
notes: Governing law clause; standard for UK-based JCT contract.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor provides forecasts of the total Defined Cost at intervals stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Cost forecasting obligation; administrative burden but provides transparency.

text: 'Commencement Date' means the date stated in the Letter of Acceptance from which the Contract Period is calculated.
notes: Standard definition; administrative.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, where the Employer fails to issue a payment notice or pay less notice by the prescribed date, the sum stated in the Contractor's payment application shall become the sum due.
notes: Default payment mechanism under HGCRA; contractor's application becomes notified sum if employer fails to serve notice.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Employer shall give the Contractor possession of the Site on the Date of Possession stated in the Contract Particulars.

text: SECTION 5: PROGRAMME AND COMPLETION
notes: Programme section heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Sub-Contractor shall not be entitled to payment for variations unless a signed Variation Order is produced prior to commencing the varied work; verbal instructions shall not create payment entitlement.
notes: Pre-variation-order condition precedent; practical impossibility on live construction sites denies contractors legitimate claims.

text: The parties confirm that this Contract constitutes the entire agreement between them in respect of the Works and supersedes all prior negotiations.
notes: Entire agreement clause; standard contract formation provision.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor corrects notified Defects before the defect correction date.
notes: Standard defect correction obligation; reasonable timeframe.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer may terminate the Contractor's employment at will and without cause upon giving 7 days' written notice, whereupon the Contractor shall be entitled only to payment for work properly executed.
notes: At-will termination without compensation for loss of anticipated profit or demobilisation costs; highly one-sided.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, where the Engineer fails to respond within the period for reply, the Contractor may treat the absence of response as approval of the communication.
notes: Deemed approval on engineer inaction; contractor can proceed on basis of non-response.

text: The Contractor shall not suspend work for non-payment unless it has given 48 hours' prior written notice and obtained a court order confirming the sum is due.
notes: Effectively removes contractor's statutory suspension right under HGCRA by imposing impractical preconditions.

notes: Employer's risk for nominated subcontractor failure; contractor protected from third-party defaults imposed by employer.

notes: Contractor's defect notification right; protects against employer-caused damage being passed back.

notes: Nominated subcontractor mechanism; limits contractor's liability for employer-selected specialists.

notes: Price control risk on employer; government intervention risk passed back to employer.

notes: Full vicarious liability for subcontractor defaults; removes contractor's ability to claim against employer for nominated sub failures.

notes: Programme submission obligation; standard NEC4 requirement.

notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall permit the Auditor General or any authorised representative to inspect all financial records relating to this Contract.
notes: Government audit access right; standard on public sector Zimbabwe contracts.

notes: Preliminaries recovery on omissions; protects contractor's fixed cost recovery on scope reductions.

notes: Contractor's defect notification right; protects against employer-caused damage being passed back.

notes: Risk register provision; promotes transparent risk management.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall submit a cashflow forecast with each payment application to assist the Employer with funding arrangements.
notes: Cashflow forecasting obligation; transparent but creates administrative burden.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall provide copies of all required insurances to the Employer within 7 days of request.
notes: Standard insurance evidence obligation; reasonable timescale.

notes: Undisputed sum payment obligation; protects contractor's cash flow during valuation disputes.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall take full responsibility for the adequacy, stability and safety of all site operations.
notes: Safety responsibility; fundamental contractor duty.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Defects Liability Period shall be 12 months from the date of Practical Completion during which the Contractor shall rectify defects notified by the Employer within 21 days.
notes: Standard DLP provision; 21-day notice-to-rectify window is reasonable.

notes: SSCC use for prospective CEs; requires forecast which introduces uncertainty.

notes: Bilateral termination right on prolonged force majeure; contractor recovers value of work done.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, all payments shall be subject to deduction of withholding tax at the rate applicable under Zimbabwe Revenue Authority regulations.
notes: ZIMRA withholding tax deduction; statutory requirement reducing contractor's net receipts.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, interest shall accrue on overdue payments at a rate of 5% above Bank of England Base Rate from the final date for payment.
notes: Standard interest provision; rate is adequate but not punitive.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Project Manager gives access to the Site to the Contractor on the access date stated in Contract Data Part 1.
notes: Standard access provision; employer obligation.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall bear all additional costs arising from unforeseeable physical conditions unless the Engineer certifies that such conditions could not reasonably have been foreseen by an experienced contractor.
notes: High bar for unforeseeable conditions relief; Engineer's gatekeeping removes automatic entitlement.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Project Manager may instruct a change to the Scope and the change is a compensation event.
notes: Standard CE for scope change; balanced provision.

text: SECTION 9 – SETTLEMENT OF DISPUTES
notes: Contract section heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall be entitled to claim direct loss and expense for any act, omission or default of the Employer or Employer's Agent that causes disruption to the Works.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer shall provide survey benchmarks and reference points for the Contractor's use in setting out the Works.

notes: Heading or procedural clause; no material risk allocation.

notes: Express right to recover finance costs and legal fees; strengthens contractor's cash flow protection.

notes: Impossibly short defect correction window with punitive 50% premium recovery.

notes: Standard FIDIC EOT provision covering listed relief events.

notes: Counterparts execution; standard administrative provision.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall indemnify the Employer against all third party claims arising from the Contractor's operations, including claims resulting from the Employer's acts or omissions.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, hyperinflationary conditions that render the Contract commercially impossible to perform shall constitute a force majeure event.
notes: Hyperinflation as FM event; Zimbabwe-specific protection for contractor.

text: SECTION 1 – DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION
notes: Contract heading/section marker.

text: The Contract shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the law of England and Wales.
notes: Governing law clause; standard for UK-based JCT contract.

notes: Programme updating obligation; reasonable and standard.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, any amounts due to the Contractor may be set off against any claim the Employer may have against the Contractor under any other contract between the parties.
notes: Cross-contract set-off right; allows employer to use disputes on other contracts to withhold current payments.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer shall provide the Contractor with a Letter of Credit from an approved bank as security for payment of amounts exceeding USD 100,000.
notes: Payment security mechanism; protects contractor against employer insolvency on large contracts.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Employer shall provide the Contractor with access to borehole water on site at no charge for construction use.
notes: Employer-provided site water; reduces contractor's site overhead.

notes: Safety responsibility; fundamental contractor duty.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall submit a detailed construction programme within 14 days of the Date of Possession and shall update it monthly or following any significant change.
notes: Programme obligation is standard; failure to comply may affect EOT entitlement.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall keep a daily site diary recording weather conditions, labour strength, materials received and work carried out.

text: 'Completion Date' means the date stated in the Contract Particulars for completion of the Works, as adjusted by any extension of time granted under this Contract.

text: The parties agree that the Employer's Agent may issue instructions requiring the Contractor to open up work for inspection; where the work is found to be in accordance with the Contract, the cost shall be added to the Contract Sum.
notes: Cost recovery mechanism for compliant open-up is fair; risk of disruption remains.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Project Manager issues a Completion Certificate when the Contractor has done all the work necessary to complete the works.
notes: Completion certification; standard milestone.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, an amount due is calculated at each assessment date by the Project Manager and Contractor jointly, or by the Project Manager alone if the Contractor does not attend.
notes: Joint assessment preferred; PM can proceed alone if contractor absent.

text: SECTION 2: DEFINITIONS
notes: Definitions section heading.

text: SECTION 7 – TITLE
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor warrants that the completed Works will achieve the energy performance rating specified in the Employer's Requirements.
notes: Performance warranty; creates ongoing liability if building underperforms.

text: In accordance with industry practice, all payments shall be subject to deduction of withholding tax at the rate applicable under Zimbabwe Revenue Authority regulations.
notes: ZIMRA withholding tax deduction; statutory requirement reducing contractor's net receipts.

text: The Employer shall pay the final balance of the Contract Sum within 21 days of the issue of the Final Certificate.
notes: Clear final payment timeline; balanced obligation.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, where the exchange rate moves by more than 5% against the Contractor between the Base Date and the payment date, the Contract Price shall be adjusted by the difference.
notes: Exchange rate fluctuation protection; strong safeguard for international contracts in Zimbabwe.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor warrants that the completed Works will achieve the energy performance rating specified in the Employer's Requirements.
notes: Performance warranty; creates ongoing liability if building underperforms.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Employer may suspend the Works for any reason for up to 60 days without this constituting a termination; during suspension the Contractor shall stand down without payment.
notes: Unpaid suspension right; contractor bears standing costs during employer-requested suspension.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor warrants that the completed Works will achieve the energy performance rating specified in the Employer's Requirements.
notes: Performance warranty; creates ongoing liability if building underperforms.

text: The Contractor's share is calculated at the end of the project as stated in the Contract.
notes: Pain/gain share clause; incentivises contractor cost efficiency.

text: The Employer shall provide the Contractor with a Letter of Credit from an approved bank as security for payment of amounts exceeding £100,000.
notes: Payment security mechanism; protects contractor against employer insolvency on large contracts.

text: The language of this contract is English.
notes: Language clause; standard.

text: The Contractor shall give notice to the Engineer within 14 days after the Contractor became aware, or should have become aware, of the event or circumstance giving rise to the claim; failing which the Contractor shall be entitled to no additional payment.
notes: FIDIC 2017 Red Book Clause 20.2.1 time bar; strict 28-day notice condition precedent with permanent loss of entitlement.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall maintain contingency resources and plans to mitigate force majeure events to the extent reasonably practicable.
notes: FM mitigation obligation; reasonable and standard.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall be entitled to claim direct loss and expense for any act, omission or default of the Employer or Employer's Agent that causes disruption to the Works.
notes: Broad disruption claim entitlement covering employer defaults; protects contractor's commercial position.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall maintain public roads used by construction traffic in a clean and tidy condition at all times.
notes: Road maintenance obligation; standard and reasonable.

text: In accordance with industry practice, either party shall give 14 days' written notice of any change in address for service of notices.
notes: Notice address update obligation; administrative.

text: 'Base Date' means the date 14 days before the latest date for submission of the Tender.
notes: Standard FIDIC Base Date definition; triggers change-in-law risk allocation.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall implement and maintain an HIV/AIDS awareness programme for all site workers throughout the duration of the Works.
notes: HIV/AIDS programme obligation; standard on large Zimbabwe infrastructure contracts.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, on termination of the Contract by the Employer for convenience, the Contractor shall be entitled to the full value of work executed, loss of profit on remaining work, and demobilisation costs.
notes: Full termination-for-convenience entitlement; contractor recovers profit on unexecuted work.

text: It is hereby agreed that where the Contractor fails to notify a compensation event within 8 weeks, the Contractor loses entitlement to any time or cost adjustment arising from that event.
notes: 8-week notification bar is strict; permanent loss of entitlement is a significant risk.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall submit a construction programme in bar chart form within 21 days of the Commencement Date and update it monthly.
notes: Programme obligation; standard practice in Zimbabwe.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, either party may terminate if a force majeure event continues for more than 180 days, with the Contractor entitled to all costs incurred to the termination date.
notes: FM termination after 180 days with full cost recovery; balanced and reasonable.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor is to submit the first assessment of the amount due no later than the first assessment date.
notes: Application submission obligation; standard NEC4.

text: 'Key Date' means the date by which the Contractor is to meet a Condition stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Standard NEC4 Key Date definition.

text: SECTION 3 – CONTROL OF THE WORKS
notes: Contract section heading.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the losing party shall bear all costs of arbitration including the other party's legal fees assessed on an indemnity basis.
notes: Indemnity costs order by arbitrator; increases stakes of losing a dispute.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor obtains required approvals from Others and notifies the Project Manager before starting work which needs such approval.
notes: Third party approval obligation; standard.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Employer may instruct the Contractor to submit a revised quotation if the Project Manager considers the original quotation unreasonable, and the Project Manager's assessment shall be final.
notes: PM's assessment of CE is final; removes contractor's ability to challenge valuation.

text: In accordance with industry practice, either party shall give 14 days' written notice of any change in address for service of notices.
notes: Notice address update obligation; administrative.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer may instruct the Contractor to submit a revised quotation if the Project Manager considers the original quotation unreasonable, and the Project Manager's assessment shall be final.
notes: PM's assessment of CE is final; removes contractor's ability to challenge valuation.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall not be entitled to any extension of time or additional payment for delays caused by shortage of materials, foreign currency unavailability, or import permit delays.
notes: Risk of Zimbabwe-specific disruptions (forex, import permits) fully on contractor; foreseeable sovereign risk excluded.

text: 11. REMEDIES OF DEFAULT
notes: Heading or procedural clause; no material risk allocation.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, payment of the final account shall only become due when the Employer has received all as-built drawings, O&M manuals, warranties and training certificates in a form satisfactory to the Employer.
notes: Final payment held hostage to administrative deliverables, giving employer disproportionate leverage after practical completion.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Employer may instruct the Contractor to submit a revised quotation if the Project Manager considers the original quotation unreasonable, and the Project Manager's assessment shall be final.
notes: PM's assessment of CE is final; removes contractor's ability to challenge valuation.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall employ a minimum of 70% local Zimbabwean labour in the execution of the Works.
notes: Local content labour requirement; common condition on government-funded Zimbabwe projects.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor's liability for latent defects shall survive the Defects Certificate and continue for 6 years from the date of Practical Completion.
notes: Latent defect liability period; standard under Limitation Act 1980.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, all notices under this Contract shall be in writing and may be served by email, fax, hand delivery or first class post.
notes: Clear service of notice provision; balanced and practical.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor's liability shall not be limited under any circumstances; any contractual cap on liability is expressly excluded.
notes: Removal of any liability cap; contractor faces unlimited financial exposure.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor accepts the risk of legal changes to the law of any country applicable to the Contractor's work and no compensation event arises from change in law.
notes: Change-in-law risk transferred to contractor; contrary to standard NEC4 CE 60.1(18).

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Sub-Contractor shall not be entitled to payment for variations unless a signed Variation Order is produced prior to commencing the varied work; verbal instructions shall not create payment entitlement.
notes: Pre-variation-order condition precedent; practical impossibility on live construction sites denies contractors legitimate claims.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall not commence any variation until the value has been pre-agreed in writing; no post-hoc valuation claims shall be entertained.
notes: Pre-agreed VO pricing condition precedent; prevents urgent variations proceeding without agreed cost.

text: SECTION 2 – THE CONTRACTOR'S MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, any variation instructed by the Employer shall be valued by the Employer's Quantity Surveyor, whose valuation shall be final and binding on the Contractor.
notes: Removes contractor's right to challenge variation valuations through dispute resolution.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall be liable for delay damages at the stated rate from the scheduled Completion Date, whether or not the delay was caused partly by the Employer.
notes: LADs run even where employer is partly responsible; no concurrent delay apportionment.

text: 18. GOVERNING LAW
notes: Heading or procedural clause; no material risk allocation.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Employer pays the amount due within 3 weeks of each assessment date.
notes: Standard NEC4 payment period; clear employer obligation.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Commencement Date shall be the date stated in the Letter of Acceptance, and the Contractor shall commence Works within 7 days thereof.
notes: Standard commencement obligation.

text: SECTION 2: DEFINITIONS
notes: Definitions section heading.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, where the Contractor submits a payment application that the Employer considers to be exaggerated, the Employer may reduce all future applications by 20% as a penalty.
notes: Punitive 20% reduction on future applications; disproportionate response to disputed valuations.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall not commence any variation until the value has been pre-agreed in writing; no post-hoc valuation claims shall be entertained.
notes: Pre-agreed VO pricing condition precedent; prevents urgent variations proceeding without agreed cost.

text: The Contractor shall give written notice to the Architect within 21 days of the occurrence of a Relevant Event and shall provide particulars within a further 14 days.
notes: Standard EOT notice provisions; strict but not unreasonable.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall not commence any variation until the value has been pre-agreed in writing; no post-hoc valuation claims shall be entertained.
notes: Pre-agreed VO pricing condition precedent; prevents urgent variations proceeding without agreed cost.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall submit a final account within 90 days of Practical Completion.
notes: Final account submission obligation; standard.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Project Manager notifies the Contractor of each Defect as soon as he finds it.
notes: PM's notification obligation; balanced duty.

text: The Contractor shall be entitled to retain and use any amounts due to the Sub-Contractor, as well as materials delivered to Site and any plant or other property of the Sub-Contractor on Site, until the Sub-Contract Works have been duly completed.
notes: Clause creates significant risk for subcontractor; one-sided in favour of contractor/employer.

text: The Employer may withhold payment of any certified sum provided it issues a Pay Less Notice not later than the prescribed period before the final date for payment.
notes: HGCRA-compliant pay less notice mechanism; contractor has clear right to challenge.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall maintain a site diary recording daily weather conditions, labour deployed and progress of the Works.
notes: Record-keeping obligation; protects both parties in claims situations.

text: SECTION 9 – SETTLEMENT OF DISPUTES
notes: Contract section heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, all cement, steel, bitumen and other principal materials shall be sourced from Zimbabwean manufacturers where available and comparable in quality and price.
notes: Local sourcing preference; may restrict contractor's supply chain and increase material risk.

text: As Witnesses: [GENERAL MANAGER- COMMERCIAL, ZIMPLATS] [GROUP COMMERCIAL MANAGER, ZIMPLATS] [PROJECTS EXECUTIVE, ZIMPLATS] [CHIEF TECHNICAL OFFICER, ZIMPLATS]
Notwithstanding any other provision, the Employer shall provide the Contractor with access to borehole water on site at no charge for construction use.
notes: Employer-provided site water; reduces contractor's site overhead.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor is entitled to a compensation event where the Project Manager gives an instruction to stop work, and the stop lasts more than 13 weeks, and no further instruction is given.
notes: Prolonged stop-work triggers CE; protects contractor's standing costs.

text: The Employer shall pay the final balance of the Contract Sum within 42 days of the issue of the Final Certificate.
notes: Clear final payment timeline; balanced obligation.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor is to prepare the first programme to show the information stated in this Contract within the period stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Programme submission obligation; standard NEC4 requirement.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, both Parties shall endeavour to avoid disputes by prompt and amicable resolution of issues.
notes: Dispute avoidance obligation; aspirational and balanced.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Engineer shall respond to the Contractor's claim within 42 days of receiving the fully detailed claim; failure to respond shall be deemed a rejection.
notes: FIDIC 2017 Clause 20.2.5 response period; deemed rejection protects contractor's programme certainty.

text: In this Contract, the following terms have the meanings given to them below unless the context otherwise requires.
notes: Standard definitions introduction.

text: SECTION 7: INSURANCE
notes: Insurance section heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, any defect that recurs within 12 months of rectification shall be treated as a new defect with a fresh 12-month rectification warranty.
notes: Rolling warranty on recurring defects; creates perpetual exposure for systemic defects.

text: It is hereby agreed that if a force majeure event continues for more than 90 days, either party may terminate the Contract by giving 14 days' notice; the Contractor shall be paid for work completed.
notes: Bilateral termination right on prolonged force majeure; contractor recovers value of work done.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall execute the Works in accordance with the Contract and the Engineer's instructions.
notes: Fundamental contractor obligation; standard.

text: This contract is a NEC4 Engineering and Construction Contract.
notes: Contract identification clause.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall be responsible for paying all import duties and clearing charges on materials brought into Zimbabwe for the Works, unless specifically stated otherwise in the Contract Data.
notes: Import duty risk on contractor; significant exposure given Zimbabwe's import tariff environment.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall maintain all statutory insurances in Zimbabwe including Workers' Compensation, NSSA contributions and third party public liability at its own cost without any reimbursement from the Employer.
notes: Standard statutory insurance obligations in Zimbabwe; contractor bears cost but this is normal.

text: Motor Vehicle Liability comprising (as a minimum) 'Balance of Third Party' risks including Passenger and Unauthorised Passenger Liability Indemnity with a limit of indemnity of not less than USD10,000.

In accordance with industry practice, payment applications must be submitted in the prescribed format using the Employer's software system; applications in any other format shall not constitute valid applications.
notes: Format condition precedent on payment applications; employer can reject valid applications on technical grounds.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall indemnify the Employer against all third party claims arising from the Contractor's operations, including claims resulting from the Employer's acts or omissions.
notes: Indemnity extends to employer's own acts; removes employer's responsibility for its own negligence.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, either Party may terminate the Contractor's obligation to Provide the Works if the other Party has committed a reason stated in this Contract.
notes: Standard mutual termination for cause; balanced.

text: The law of the contract is the law of England and Wales.
notes: Governing law; standard for UK NEC4 contract.

text: SECTION 9 – TERMINATION
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Project Manager may give an instruction requiring a Defect to be corrected within a shorter defect correction period than the one stated in the Contract Data.
notes: PM can shorten defect correction period; some contractor risk but subject to reasonableness.

text: The parties agree that no CE shall arise from any instruction given orally by the Project Manager; all instructions must be in writing and logged in the Risk Register.
notes: Oral instruction exclusion; contractors regularly act on PM oral instructions on live sites.

text: In accordance with industry practice, no interim payment shall be due to the Contractor until the Employer has received and verified the Contractor's application; the Employer shall have 60 days to make such verification.
notes: Verification period far exceeds HGCRA requirements; effectively extends payment cycle to 75+ days.

text: The Employer may at any time instruct the removal of the Contractor's key personnel, and the Contractor shall have no right to object or seek compensation for the disruption caused.
notes: Employer can disrupt contractor's team without justification or compensation.

text: Dispute shall first be referred to mediation; if not resolved within 21 days either party may refer the dispute to adjudication in accordance with the Scheme for Construction Contracts.
notes: Tiered dispute resolution with adjudication backstop; balanced and HGCRA compliant.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, variations shall be valued using the rates and prices in the Contract Bills where applicable, and where not applicable, at fair rates and prices to be agreed.
notes: Bilateral agreement mechanism for variation valuation; balanced but subject to dispute.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall submit a final account within 90 days of Practical Completion.
notes: Final account submission obligation; standard.

text: The Employer shall provide the Contractor with a Letter of Credit from an approved bank as security for payment of amounts exceeding USD 75,000.
notes: Payment security mechanism; protects contractor against employer insolvency on large contracts.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, all risk of ground conditions at the Site remains with the Contractor regardless of the Site Information provided; no compensation event arises from physical conditions.
notes: Full ground risk on contractor even where site information proves inaccurate; contrary to Z60.1 CE.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall be entitled to payment of interest at a rate of 5% above the RBZ overnight lending rate on all overdue amounts calculated from the due date.
notes: Interest on late payment tied to RBZ rate; commercially reasonable and common on Zimbabwe contracts.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor's liability for latent defects shall survive the Defects Certificate and continue for 6 years from the date of Practical Completion.
notes: Latent defect liability period; standard under Limitation Act 1980.

text: The Employer shall make an Advance Payment of 5% of the Accepted Contract Amount within 21 days of receipt of the Performance Security.
notes: FIDIC advance payment at 10%; standard and contractor-supportive.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, escalation shall not apply to any element of the Contract Sum; the Contractor accepts all risks of price escalation in materials, labour and equipment for the full duration of the Contract.
notes: Full escalation risk on contractor in Zimbabwe's volatile inflation environment; commercially dangerous on long contracts.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall not be entitled to additional cost arising from a force majeure event; its sole entitlement is an extension of time.
notes: Time-only relief for force majeure; contractor bears standing cost during FM period.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall indemnify the Employer against all costs, claims and expenses arising from the Contractor's presence on site, including claims by third parties, whether or not caused by the Contractor's negligence.
notes: Negligence-free indemnity; contractor becomes insurer for all site-related third party claims.

text: The Contractor shall give a Defect Notice to the Engineer within 48 hours of discovering a defect caused by the Employer.
notes: Contractor's defect notification right; protects against employer-caused damage being passed back.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor provides a 10-year structural warranty for all reinforced concrete works in accordance with the relevant British Standards.
notes: Long-form structural warranty; common on residential and commercial developments.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer may withhold any amount due to the Contractor pending settlement of any claim by the Employer, whether or not such claim is disputed or related to the current Works.
notes: Unrestricted withholding right; allows employer to use unrelated claims to delay payment indefinitely.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall not make any claim for loss and expense arising from delay to the Works unless a detailed programme showing critical path analysis is submitted with the claim.
notes: Critical path programme as condition precedent to loss and expense; places impossible evidential burden on contractor.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor is to provide the Project Manager with information which the Project Manager needs in order to issue a certificate.
notes: Information provision obligation; standard.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor is to prepare the first programme to show the information stated in this Contract within the period stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Programme submission obligation; standard NEC4 requirement.

text: SECTION 8: FORCE MAJEURE
notes: Force majeure section heading.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor's liability for latent defects shall survive the Defects Certificate and continue for 6 years from the date of Practical Completion.
notes: Latent defect liability period; standard under Limitation Act 1980.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Project Manager may instruct a change to the Scope and the change is a compensation event.
notes: Standard CE for scope change; balanced provision.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor is to obtain the Project Manager's approval before starting the design of each part of the works.
notes: Design approval obligation; standard for ECC Option A with contractor design.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Employer shall give the Contractor possession of the Site on the Date of Possession stated in the Contract Particulars.
notes: Standard possession provision; balanced obligation on employer.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall maintain contingency resources and plans to mitigate force majeure events to the extent reasonably practicable.
notes: FM mitigation obligation; reasonable and standard.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Architect shall issue Interim Payment Certificates at the intervals stated in the Contract Particulars, and the Employer shall pay within 14 days of the due date.
notes: Clear payment timeline; HGCRA compliant.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Employer shall provide the Contractor with a Letter of Credit from an approved bank as security for payment of amounts exceeding USD 100,000.
notes: Payment security mechanism; protects contractor against employer insolvency on large contracts.

text: PART I – GENERAL CONDITIONS
notes: FIDIC structure heading.

text: 'Defined Cost' means the amounts calculated by applying the Schedule of Cost Components or Shorter Schedule of Cost Components.
notes: Standard NEC4 cost definition.

text: In accordance with industry practice, either party may give notice of adjudication at any time; the adjudicator shall be appointed by the RICS within 5 days of the notice.
notes: Standard RICS adjudicator nomination; balanced and practical.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, where the Employer issues an omission instruction, the Contractor shall be entitled to recover its preliminaries and time-related costs attributable to the omitted work.
notes: Preliminaries recovery on omissions; protects contractor's fixed cost recovery on scope reductions.

text: 'Defects Liability Period' means the period of 12 months following Practical Completion during which the Contractor is liable to rectify defects.
notes: Standard DLP definition.

text: 'Employer's Representative' means the person appointed by the Employer to administer the Contract on its behalf.
notes: Standard ER definition.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Employer may grant an extension of time for delays caused by force majeure events including acts of God, floods, civil unrest, government action or unavailability of foreign currency.
notes: Force majeure definition includes Zimbabwe-specific risks (forex, civil unrest); balanced provision.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, force majeure events include epidemic, pandemic, earthquake, flood, fire, war, civil commotion, sanctions, government action and any other event beyond the reasonable control of the affected party.
notes: Broad force majeure definition including government action; well-suited to Zimbabwe's political risk environment.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall erect and maintain a Project Sign Board in accordance with the Employer's specifications within 7 days of Commencement.
notes: Signage obligation; administrative, low risk.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall maintain professional indemnity insurance in the sum of USD 500,000 for 6 years following Practical Completion.
notes: PI insurance obligation post-completion; standard on design-and-build contracts.

text: The Contractor shall be entitled to a daywork rate premium of 35% on direct cost for all instructed dayworks, together with a percentage addition for profit and overhead.
notes: Above-market daywork premium; improves contractor's margin on time-and-materials work.

text: The parties agree that the Employer shall pay the Contractor a mobilisation advance of 15% of the Contract Sum, secured by an advance payment guarantee, within 30 days of contract execution.
notes: Mobilisation advance at 15%; strong cash flow support for contractor on project start.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer may instruct the Contractor to accelerate the programme; the Contractor shall comply and any claim for acceleration costs shall be submitted within 7 days of the instruction.
notes: 7-day acceleration claim window is impractical; employer can demand acceleration without pre-agreeing cost.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor is to provide the Project Manager with information which the Project Manager needs in order to issue a certificate.
notes: Information provision obligation; standard.

text: The parties agree that hyperinflationary conditions that render the Contract commercially impossible to perform shall constitute a force majeure event.
notes: Hyperinflation as FM event; Zimbabwe-specific protection for contractor.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall not carry out any blasting operations without prior written approval from SADC Explosives Board and notification to the Employer's Representative.
notes: Blasting approval requirement; safety-critical and regulatory.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor is entitled to a prevention event compensation where the event stops the Contractor completing the works and neither party could have prevented it.
notes: Prevention event CE; contractor recovers time and cost for unforeseeable force majeure events.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Architect shall issue Interim Payment Certificates at the intervals stated in the Contract Particulars, and the Employer shall pay within 14 days of the due date.
notes: Clear payment timeline; HGCRA compliant.

text: In accordance with industry practice, all disputes shall be resolved by arbitration in terms of the Arbitration Act [Chapter 7:15] of Zimbabwe; the arbitrator shall be appointed solely by the Employer.
notes: Employer-appointed arbitrator lacks independence; contrary to fair arbitration principles.

text: SECTION 2 – CONTRACTOR'S OBLIGATIONS
notes: Contract section heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor warrants that it has independently investigated all site conditions, surrounding infrastructure and sub-surface geology, and accepts all risk therefrom without any entitlement to additional payment.
notes: Full unforeseeable ground risk on contractor; removes employer's implied obligation to disclose site information.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, where a nominated subcontractor fails to perform, the Employer shall bear the resulting delay costs and shall grant an extension of time to the Contractor.
notes: Employer's risk for nominated subcontractor failure; contractor protected from third-party defaults imposed by employer.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor warrants that it has independently verified all information provided by the Employer and accepts full responsibility for any errors or discrepancies in the Contract Documents.
notes: Contractor assumes employer's design risk; eliminates implied warranty on employer-provided information.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall retain the right to remove from Site any equipment, materials and temporary works paid for by the Contractor at any time.
notes: Retention of title and removal right; protects contractor's assets against employer default.

text: SECTION 9: DISPUTE RESOLUTION
notes: Dispute resolution section heading.

text: The Contractor shall maintain public roads used by construction traffic in a clean and tidy condition at all times.
notes: Road maintenance obligation; standard and reasonable.

text: SECTION 8 – RISKS AND INSURANCE
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: It is hereby agreed that all cement, steel, bitumen and other principal materials shall be sourced from Zimbabwean manufacturers where available and comparable in quality and price.
notes: Local sourcing preference; may restrict contractor's supply chain and increase material risk.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall submit a construction programme in bar chart form within 21 days of the Commencement Date and update it monthly.
notes: Programme obligation; standard practice in Zimbabwe.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall correct all Defects within 7 days of notification regardless of their nature or complexity, failing which the Employer may employ others and recover the cost plus 50%.
notes: Impossibly short defect correction window with punitive 50% premium recovery.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Project Manager may instruct the Contractor to stop or not start any work without giving a reason, and no compensation event arises from such instruction.
notes: Removal of compensation event for stop/start instructions; contractor bears cost of downtime.

text: The Contractor shall maintain and operate a complaints handling procedure for affected community members throughout the Contract Period.
notes: Community engagement obligation; increasingly common on Zimbabwe infrastructure contracts.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Employer may withhold any amount due to the Contractor pending settlement of any claim by the Employer, whether or not such claim is disputed or related to the current Works.
notes: Unrestricted withholding right; allows employer to use unrelated claims to delay payment indefinitely.

text: 18. ENTIRE AGREEMENT
notes: Heading or procedural clause; no material risk allocation.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall provide a Performance Security equal to 15% of the Accepted Contract Amount.
notes: 15% performance security is above FIDIC standard 10%; significant liquidity cost.

text: 'Completion Date' means the date stated in the Contract Particulars for completion of the Works, as adjusted by any extension of time granted under this Contract.
notes: Standard definition.

notes: Local authority compliance obligation; standard but requires advance planning.

notes: Contract identification clause.

text: SECTION 6: VARIATIONS
notes: Variations section heading.

text: The parties shall attempt to resolve any dispute by negotiation within 72 hours of written notice of the dispute.
notes: Negotiation first step is standard and commercially sensible.

text: The Employer shall pay the final balance of the Contract Sum within 7 days of the issue of the Final Certificate.
notes: Clear final payment timeline; balanced obligation.

text: The parties agree that the Employer's obligation to provide information on time is a condition precedent to the Contractor's obligation to maintain the programme; late information shall entitle the Contractor to an extension of time.
notes: Information provision as condition precedent; strong protection where employer delays drawings or instructions.

text: 'Employer's Representative' means the person appointed by the Employer to administer the Contract on its behalf.
notes: Standard ER definition.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Client may terminate the Contractor's obligation to Provide the Works at any time.
notes: Termination for convenience right; contractor receives defined cost plus profit entitlement under NEC4.
