text: The Contractor shall give a Defect Notice to the Engineer within 7 days of discovering a defect caused by the Employer.
notes: Contractor's defect notification right; protects against employer-caused damage being passed back.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Employer may grant an extension of time for delays caused by force majeure events including acts of God, floods, civil unrest, government action or unavailability of foreign currency.
notes: Force majeure definition includes Zimbabwe-specific risks (forex, civil unrest); balanced provision.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Works shall be carried out in accordance with the drawings, specifications and Bill of Quantities forming part of this Contract.
notes: Fundamental obligation to comply with contract documents; standard.

text: The parties agree that if the Contractor becomes insolvent, the Employer shall have the right to complete the Works using the Contractor's workforce and equipment without further payment.
notes: Employer's step-in right using contractor's resources without additional payment; commercially punitive.

text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF the parties have signed this Contract on the date first written above.
notes: Execution clause.

text: The language of this contract is English.
notes: Language clause; standard.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall submit an Environmental Management Plan in accordance with EMA regulations within 30 days of Contract Date.
notes: EMA compliance requirement; mandatory for infrastructure projects in Zimbabwe.

text: The Employer shall make an Advance Payment of 12.5% of the Accepted Contract Amount within 21 days of receipt of the Performance Security.
notes: FIDIC advance payment at 10%; standard and contractor-supportive.

text: Words importing the singular include the plural and vice versa; words importing any gender include all genders.
notes: Standard interpretation provision.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall be liable for liquidated damages at the rate of USD 5,000 per day for every day of delay beyond the Completion Date, without any limit on the aggregate amount recoverable.
notes: Uncapped daily LADs in USD; creates severe and uninsurable financial exposure for Zimbabwean contractors.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor obtains required approvals from Others and notifies the Project Manager before starting work which needs such approval.
notes: Third party approval obligation; standard.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, either party may give notice of adjudication at any time; the adjudicator shall be appointed by the RICS within 5 days of the notice.
notes: Standard RICS adjudicator nomination; balanced and practical.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, where the exchange rate moves by more than 5% against the Contractor between the Base Date and the payment date, the Contract Price shall be adjusted by the difference.
notes: Exchange rate fluctuation protection; strong safeguard for international contracts in Zimbabwe.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall comply with the Employer's Health, Safety and Environment policy as issued and updated from time to time.
notes: HSE policy compliance obligation; standard.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor Provides the Works in accordance with the Scope and this Contract.
notes: Fundamental contractor obligation; standard.

text: SECTION 7 – ASSIGNMENT, THIRD PARTY RIGHTS AND COLLATERAL WARRANTIES
notes: Contract section heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall pay all employees a minimum wage not less than the applicable ZITS/ZCEA minimum wage for the construction sector.
notes: Labour law compliance; Zimbabwe Construction Industry Federation wage requirement.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, either party may terminate this Contract on the insolvency of the other party.
notes: Standard insolvency termination clause; mutual right is balanced.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall continue to work diligently pending resolution of any dispute and shall not suspend work merely because a dispute exists.
notes: Work continuation obligation during disputes; limits contractor's leverage but maintains project progress.

text: SECTION 8 – TERMINATION
notes: Contract section heading.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, any defect that recurs within 12 months of rectification shall be treated as a new defect with a fresh 12-month rectification warranty.
notes: Rolling warranty on recurring defects; creates perpetual exposure for systemic defects.

text: This contract is a NEC4 Engineering and Construction Contract.
notes: Contract identification clause.

text: PART I – GENERAL CONDITIONS
notes: FIDIC structure heading.

text: The parties agree that any variation instructed by the Employer shall be valued by the Employer's Quantity Surveyor, whose valuation shall be final and binding on the Contractor.
notes: Removes contractor's right to challenge variation valuations through dispute resolution.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Project Manager may instruct the Contractor to use a different method of working without it being a compensation event if the change is necessary for safety.
notes: Safety-based method change without CE; employer defines 'necessary' unilaterally.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor provides forecasts of the total Defined Cost at intervals stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Cost forecasting obligation; administrative burden but provides transparency.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor warrants that the completed Works will achieve the energy performance rating specified in the Employer's Requirements.
notes: Performance warranty; creates ongoing liability if building underperforms.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer may instruct the Contractor to submit a revised quotation if the Project Manager considers the original quotation unreasonable, and the Project Manager's assessment shall be final.
notes: PM's assessment of CE is final; removes contractor's ability to challenge valuation.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall not assign this Contract or any part of it without the prior written consent of the Employer, which shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed.
notes: Anti-assignment clause with reasonableness qualifier; balanced provision.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor is to refer a dispute to the Dispute Avoidance Board within 4 weeks of becoming aware of the dispute.
notes: DAB referral timeframe; standard NEC4 W3 procedure.

text: SECTION 5: PROGRAMME AND COMPLETION
notes: Programme section heading.

text: The parties agree that the Defects Liability Period shall be 12 months from the date of Practical Completion during which the Contractor shall rectify defects notified by the Employer within 21 days.
notes: Standard DLP provision; 21-day notice-to-rectify window is reasonable.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, escalation shall not apply to any element of the Contract Sum; the Contractor accepts all risks of price escalation in materials, labour and equipment for the full duration of the Contract.
notes: Full escalation risk on contractor in Zimbabwe's volatile inflation environment; commercially dangerous on long contracts.

text: In accordance with industry practice, variations shall be valued using the rates and prices in the Contract Bills where applicable, and where not applicable, at fair rates and prices to be agreed.
notes: Bilateral agreement mechanism for variation valuation; balanced but subject to dispute.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall bear all additional costs arising from unforeseeable physical conditions unless the Engineer certifies that such conditions could not reasonably have been foreseen by an experienced contractor.
notes: High bar for unforeseeable conditions relief; Engineer's gatekeeping removes automatic entitlement.

text: CLAUSE 1 – GENERAL PROVISIONS
notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall be responsible for obtaining all statutory approvals, permits and licences required for the Works at its own cost, and delays arising from the permitting process shall not entitle the Contractor to any extension of time.
notes: Contractor bears permitting risk in a jurisdiction with unpredictable regulatory timelines.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, all Contractor's Equipment brought to Site shall be deemed to be exclusively intended for use on the Works and shall not be removed without the Engineer's consent.
notes: Deemed vesting of contractor's equipment; exposes contractor to loss on employer insolvency.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor is entitled to a prevention event compensation where the event stops the Contractor completing the works and neither party could have prevented it.
notes: Prevention event CE; contractor recovers time and cost for unforeseeable force majeure events.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall keep and maintain proper books of account relating to the Works and shall make them available for inspection by the Employer's Quantity Surveyor on 48 hours' notice.
notes: Open-book accounting obligation; some administrative burden but not unusual.

text: The Employer may terminate the Contractor's employment for any reason and the Contractor shall be entitled only to an amount equal to the Defined Cost incurred to date less a 12.5% reduction.
notes: Termination for convenience with punitive 10% reduction; contractor has no entitlement to profit or pipeline contribution.

text: 'Architect' means the person named as such in the Contract Particulars or any replacement appointed by the Employer.
notes: Standard definition; identifies key contract administrator.

text: In accordance with industry practice, a compensation event is not notified if the Employer or the Contractor did not give an early warning of the event when they could have done so.
notes: Mutual early warning obligation; applies to both parties but in practice limits contractor CEs.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer may withhold payment of any amount due pending resolution of any dispute, without serving a pay less notice.
notes: Payment withholding without pay less notice undermines NEC4's payment transparency mechanisms.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall repay all amounts received under interim certificates if the final account shows the total certified was overpaid, with interest at 8% above base rate.
notes: Retroactive overpayment recovery with punitive interest; creates cash flow uncertainty throughout project.

text: 8.3 The Independent Contractor is expected to carry the company's name with honor; therefore, only general good behavior and respectful language should be used. No vulgar or foul language is tolerated.

Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall be responsible for the security of the entire Site, including all Employer-stored materials, and shall indemnify the Employer for any theft or loss on Site.
notes: Security indemnity for employer's materials; imposes insurance cost for employer's own property.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor is to obtain the Project Manager's approval before starting the design of each part of the works.
notes: Design approval obligation; standard for ECC Option A with contractor design.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, on termination by the Contractor for Employer default, the Contractor shall be entitled to recover its costs, loss of profit on the remaining Works, and reasonable demobilisation costs.
notes: Full termination entitlement for contractor on employer default; standard JCT 8.9 protection.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall indemnify the Employer against all third party claims arising from the Contractor's operations, including claims resulting from the Employer's acts or omissions.
notes: Indemnity extends to employer's own acts; removes employer's responsibility for its own negligence.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer shall provide the Site to the Contractor on or before the Commencement Date.
notes: Standard FIDIC site possession obligation; employer duty.

text: 'Relevant Event' means any of the events listed in clause 2.29 of these Conditions.
notes: Cross-reference definition; no risk allocation.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall give notice of a force majeure event within 14 days of its commencement; failure to give notice within this period shall disentitle the Contractor from any relief.
notes: 14-day FM notice as condition precedent; strict but proportionate.

text: In accordance with industry practice, variations shall be valued using the rates and prices in the Contract Bills where applicable, and where not applicable, at fair rates and prices to be agreed.
notes: Bilateral agreement mechanism for variation valuation; balanced but subject to dispute.

text: The parties agree that interest shall accrue on certified but unpaid sums at LIBOR plus 2% from the due date for payment.
notes: Interest on overdue sums; LIBOR-linked rate is market standard.

text: On termination for employer convenience, the Contractor shall be entitled to the value of work executed, materials on site, reasonable overheads and 15% of the remaining works as lost profit.
notes: Generous termination for convenience entitlement including lost profit; contractor-favourable.

text: ANNEXURE A: CONTRACT DATA
notes: Contract data annexure heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor corrects notified Defects before the defect correction date.
notes: Standard defect correction obligation; reasonable timeframe.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor's liability shall not be limited under any circumstances; any contractual cap on liability is expressly excluded.
notes: Removal of any liability cap; contractor faces unlimited financial exposure.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall take full responsibility for the adequacy, stability and safety of all site operations.
notes: Safety responsibility; fundamental contractor duty.

text: The parties agree that the Employer pays the amount due within 3 weeks of each assessment date.
notes: Standard NEC4 payment period; clear employer obligation.

text: On any termination, the Employer shall pay the Contractor within 72 hours for all work properly executed and materials delivered to site.
notes: Payment on termination obligation; standard and reasonable.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall complete the Works and all snagging within the Defects Liability Period without any entitlement to prolongation costs or disruption claims.
notes: Contractor bears full prolongation and disruption costs even where caused by employer actions.

text: 20.7 Notwithstanding any express or implied provision of this Agreement to the contrary, any latitude or extension of time which may be allowed by any Party to another Party in respect of any matter or thing that such Party is bound to perform or observe in terms hereof, shall not under any circumstances be deemed to be a waiver of the rights of the Party allowing the latitude or extension and such Party may at any time and without notice, require strict and punctual compliance with each and every provisions or term hereof.
Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor is to submit revised programmes whenever the Project Manager instructs, or when the Contractor chooses.
notes: Programme updating obligation; reasonable and standard.

text: The Engineer's determination of any claim shall be final and binding unless challenged by a DAAB decision within 7 days.
notes: Short DAAB challenge window; practical difficulty in convening DAAB within 28 days in Zimbabwe.

text: 12.6 The Parties irrevocably agree that the decision in these arbitration proceedings: (1) shall be binding on them and the Parties renounce any right of appeal from the arbitral award in so far as such renunciation can validly be made; (2) shall be carried into effect; and (3) may be made an order of any court of competent jurisdiction.
'Key Date' means the date by which the Contractor is to meet a Condition stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Standard NEC4 Key Date definition.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor warrants that it has independently investigated all site conditions, surrounding infrastructure and sub-surface geology, and accepts all risk therefrom without any entitlement to additional payment.
notes: Full unforeseeable ground risk on contractor; removes employer's implied obligation to disclose site information.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Engineer shall respond to the Contractor's claim within 42 days of receiving the fully detailed claim; failure to respond shall be deemed a rejection.
notes: FIDIC 2017 Clause 20.2.5 response period; deemed rejection protects contractor's programme certainty.

text: CONTRACT DATA PART 2 – DATA PROVIDED BY THE CONTRACTOR
notes: Standard NEC4 document heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall maintain all statutory insurances in Zimbabwe including Workers' Compensation, NSSA contributions and third party public liability at its own cost without any reimbursement from the Employer.
notes: Standard statutory insurance obligations in Zimbabwe; contractor bears cost but this is normal.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall complete the Works and all snagging within the Defects Liability Period without any entitlement to prolongation costs or disruption claims.
notes: Contractor bears full prolongation and disruption costs even where caused by employer actions.

text: 'Compensation Event' means an event which entitles the Contractor to changes to the Prices and Completion Date.
notes: Standard NEC4 compensation event definition.

text: It is hereby agreed that price escalation shall be calculated monthly using the CPIH index published by ZIMSTAT; all increases shall be paid in the same currency as the Contract Sum.
notes: Escalation clause with ZIMSTAT index; full price protection for contractor in inflationary environment.

text: It is hereby agreed that any amounts due to the Contractor may be set off against any claim the Employer may have against the Contractor under any other contract between the parties.
notes: Cross-contract set-off right; allows employer to use disputes on other contracts to withhold current payments.

text: In this Contract, the following terms have the meanings given to them below unless the context otherwise requires.
notes: Standard definitions introduction.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Engineer shall act neutrally between the parties in all matters under this Contract.
notes: FIDIC 2017 engineer neutrality obligation; fundamental balanced provision.

text: The Employer shall pay the final balance of the Contract Sum within 48 hours of the issue of the Final Certificate.
notes: Clear final payment timeline; balanced obligation.

text: The law of the contract is the law of England and Wales.
notes: Governing law; standard for UK NEC4 contract.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor is to assess the compensation event using the Shorter Schedule of Cost Components where the work has not been done.
notes: SSCC use for prospective CEs; requires forecast which introduces uncertainty.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall indemnify the Employer against all costs, claims and expenses arising from the Contractor's presence on site, including claims by third parties, whether or not caused by the Contractor's negligence.
notes: Negligence-free indemnity; contractor becomes insurer for all site-related third party claims.

text: It is hereby agreed that where a force majeure event affects both parties, each shall bear its own costs and no claim shall be made against the other.
notes: Bilateral cost-bearing on shared FM events; balanced loss allocation.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall not be entitled to additional cost arising from a force majeure event; its sole entitlement is an extension of time.
notes: Time-only relief for force majeure; contractor bears standing cost during FM period.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, payment shall only be made quarterly; no interim payments shall be due during the Contract Period regardless of the value of work executed.
notes: Quarterly payment cycle; creates severe cash flow hardship for contractor, particularly in Zimbabwe's inflation environment.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall be liable for consequential, indirect and economic loss suffered by the Employer arising out of any breach, delay or defect, without financial cap.
notes: Uncapped consequential loss liability is commercially unacceptable and uninsurable.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer shall pay the amounts certified by the Engineer in accordance with the Contract.
notes: Employer's fundamental payment obligation; standard.

text: In accordance with industry practice, payment applications must be submitted in the prescribed format using the Employer's software system; applications in any other format shall not constitute valid applications.
notes: Format condition precedent on payment applications; employer can reject valid applications on technical grounds.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, disputes shall first be referred to a technical expert for determination within 30 days; expert determination shall be binding unless overturned by arbitration.
notes: Expert determination as first-tier; efficient mechanism for technical disputes.

text: 'Completion Date' means the date stated in the Contract Particulars for completion of the Works, as adjusted by any extension of time granted under this Contract.
notes: Standard definition.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall continue to work diligently pending resolution of any dispute and shall not suspend work merely because a dispute exists.
notes: Work continuation obligation during disputes; limits contractor's leverage but maintains project progress.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall submit a construction programme in bar chart form within 21 days of the Commencement Date and update it monthly.
notes: Programme obligation; standard practice in Zimbabwe.

text: In accordance with industry practice, if the Contractor desires to make any variation to the design or specification, it shall submit a formal request to the Employer's Agent, who may consent or refuse at their reasonable discretion.
notes: Variation approval subject to reasonable discretion; some restriction on contractor flexibility.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor's share is calculated at the end of the project as stated in the Contract.
notes: Pain/gain share clause; incentivises contractor cost efficiency.

text: Retention shall be as set out in Annexure 'A' and shall reduce by half on the issue of a Practical Completion Certificate.
For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall not suspend work for non-payment unless it has given 28 days' prior written notice and obtained a court order confirming the sum is due.
notes: Effectively removes contractor's statutory suspension right under HGCRA by imposing impractical preconditions.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall be responsible for the security of the entire Site, including all Employer-stored materials, and shall indemnify the Employer for any theft or loss on Site.
notes: Security indemnity for employer's materials; imposes insurance cost for employer's own property.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor accepts no warranty liability for defects arising from the Employer's design or specification.
notes: Design warranty carve-out for employer-provided design; fair on contractor.

text: Extension of time shall be granted where the Contractor demonstrates on the balance of probabilities that a Relevant Event caused critical delay to Practical Completion.
notes: Causal link and balance of probabilities test is standard but requires robust programming evidence.

text: 'Completion Date' means the date stated in the Contract Particulars for completion of the Works, as adjusted by any extension of time granted under this Contract.
notes: Standard definition.

text: Either party may apply to the High Court of Zimbabwe for urgent interim relief pending arbitration.
notes: Preservation of urgent court relief; balanced dispute mechanism.

text: The parties agree that where changes in Laws after the Base Date increase the Contractor's Costs, the Contract Price shall be adjusted accordingly.
notes: Change-in-law risk on employer; contractor protected from sovereign risk after Base Date.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor is to obtain the Project Manager's approval before starting the design of each part of the works.
notes: Design approval obligation; standard for ECC Option A with contractor design.

text: The Contractor's share of any cost overrun shall be 100% for the first 2.5% of overrun and 50% thereafter; the Employer's share of savings is 70%.
notes: Asymmetric pain/gain share highly unfavourable to contractor; 100% pain on first 10% with only 30% gain.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall be entitled to apply for interim payment on a monthly basis throughout the Contract Period.
notes: Monthly interim payment right; standard cash flow provision.

text: In accordance with industry practice, either party may terminate if a force majeure event continues for more than 180 days, with the Contractor entitled to all costs incurred to the termination date.
notes: FM termination after 180 days with full cost recovery; balanced and reasonable.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor is to assess the compensation event using the Shorter Schedule of Cost Components where the work has not been done.
notes: SSCC use for prospective CEs; requires forecast which introduces uncertainty.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall complete the Works within the time stated even if access, materials or instructions are delayed; no extension of time shall be granted for any cause whatsoever.
notes: Absolute completion obligation with no EOT; imposes strict liability for delays not within contractor's control.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall be responsible for the security of the entire Site, including all Employer-stored materials, and shall indemnify the Employer for any theft or loss on Site.
notes: Security indemnity for employer's materials; imposes insurance cost for employer's own property.

text: The Project Manager certifies a payment in the amount due within one week of each assessment date.
notes: Payment certification timeline; standard NEC4.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, either party may terminate this Contract on the insolvency of the other party.
notes: Standard insolvency termination clause; mutual right is balanced.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, on termination by the Contractor for Employer default, the Contractor shall be entitled to recover its costs, loss of profit on the remaining Works, and reasonable demobilisation costs.
notes: Full termination entitlement for contractor on employer default; standard JCT 8.9 protection.

text: 2. SUB-CONTRACTOR'S RESPONSIBILITY
notes: Heading or procedural clause; no material risk allocation.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall bear all costs of independent testing regardless of the test results; no cost recovery applies where tests confirm compliance.
notes: All testing costs on contractor; creates financial disincentive to test and verify compliance.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor retains ownership of its Equipment and Plant brought onto Site until the Contract is terminated or the items are removed.
notes: Retention of title for contractor's equipment; protects assets on employer insolvency.

text: 'Base Date' means the date 72 hours before the latest date for submission of the Tender.
notes: Standard FIDIC Base Date definition; triggers change-in-law risk allocation.

text: It is hereby agreed that good faith negotiation shall be a condition precedent to arbitration; parties shall negotiate for 30 days before initiating formal proceedings.
notes: Negotiation condition precedent; promotes amicable resolution before formal proceedings.

text: It is hereby agreed that unavailability of foreign currency shall constitute a force majeure event under this Contract.
notes: Forex unavailability as FM event; critical protection for Zimbabwe contracts.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Project Manager notifies the Contractor of each Defect as soon as he finds it.
notes: PM's notification obligation; balanced duty.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall comply with all CDM 2015 requirements as Principal Contractor and shall bear the cost of such compliance unless expressly stated otherwise.
notes: CDM compliance is a statutory duty; cost allocation is standard practice.

text: SECTION 5 – VARIATIONS
notes: Contract section heading.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall be responsible for setting out the Works from the reference points provided and shall verify all dimensions before commencing construction.
notes: Contractor's setting-out responsibility; standard obligation.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor is to provide the Project Manager with a first programme showing the information required by this Contract.
notes: Programme provision obligation; standard.

text: 'Practical Completion' means the stage at which the Works are practically complete as certified by the Architect.
notes: Standard definition; pivotal trigger for retention release and LAD cessation.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor warrants that all subcontractors and suppliers will perform their obligations; the Contractor shall be liable for all defaults of subcontractors as if they were the Contractor's own defaults.
notes: Full vicarious liability for subcontractor defaults; removes contractor's ability to claim against employer for nominated sub failures.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, payment of the final account shall only become due when the Employer has received all as-built drawings, O&M manuals, warranties and training certificates in a form satisfactory to the Employer.
notes: Final payment held hostage to administrative deliverables, giving employer disproportionate leverage after practical completion.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the seat of arbitration shall be Harare, Zimbabwe; the arbitrator shall have authority to grant interim relief.
notes: Harare-seated arbitration; appropriate for Zimbabwe construction disputes.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall rectify all defects notified during the Defects Liability Period at its own cost, including defects caused by fair wear and tear, acts of God or third parties.
notes: Warranty extends to fair wear and tear and force majeure damage; far beyond standard contractor warranty obligations.

text: The Employer shall provide the Contractor with a Letter of Credit from an approved bank as security for payment of amounts exceeding ZAR 500,000.
notes: Payment security mechanism; protects contractor against employer insolvency on large contracts.

text: The parties shall attempt to resolve any dispute by negotiation within 72 hours of written notice of the dispute.
notes: Negotiation first step is standard and commercially sensible.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, payment applications shall be submitted by the Contractor on the 25th of each month; the Employer's Quantity Surveyor shall certify within 14 days and payment shall be made within 30 days of certification.
notes: Payment cycle of approximately 44 days; acceptable but on the longer side for Zimbabwe market.

text: 'Completion Date' means the date stated in the Contract Particulars for completion of the Works, as adjusted by any extension of time granted under this Contract.
notes: Standard definition.

text: In accordance with industry practice, a Defect is a part of the works which is not in accordance with the Scope.
notes: Standard definition of defect.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer may withhold any amount due to the Contractor pending settlement of any claim by the Employer, whether or not such claim is disputed or related to the current Works.
notes: Unrestricted withholding right; allows employer to use unrelated claims to delay payment indefinitely.

text: In accordance with industry practice, all variations must be supported by a Variation Order signed by the Employer's Representative before work commences, except in cases of urgency where verbal instruction may be confirmed in writing within 48 hours.
notes: VO requirement with urgency exception; balanced but requires contractor diligence.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall erect and maintain a Project Sign Board in accordance with the Employer's specifications within 7 days of Commencement.
notes: Signage obligation; administrative, low risk.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor retains ownership of its Equipment and Plant brought onto Site until the Contract is terminated or the items are removed.
notes: Retention of title for contractor's equipment; protects assets on employer insolvency.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall not be entitled to loss of profit on omitted works where variations reduce the scope of the Contract.
notes: Exclusion of loss of profit on omissions is common but limits contractor's commercial protection.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall bear the cost of all power, water, telecommunications and site services throughout the Contract Period; no allowance has been made in the Contract Sum.
notes: Undisclosed service costs to be borne by contractor; creates hidden financial risk.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall not be entitled to a compensation event for delay caused by any action or inaction of a subcontractor or supplier.
notes: Sub-contractor delay risk entirely on contractor with no pass-through to employer.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, all disputes shall be resolved by a sole arbitrator appointed by the Employer; the Employer's selection shall be final and not subject to challenge.
notes: Employer-appointed sole arbitrator; fundamentally compromises independence of arbitration.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall comply with all applicable laws, regulations and permit conditions throughout the Contract.
notes: General regulatory compliance; standard obligation.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, where the Employer fails to issue a payment notice or pay less notice by the prescribed date, the sum stated in the Contractor's payment application shall become the sum due.
notes: Default payment mechanism under HGCRA; contractor's application becomes notified sum if employer fails to serve notice.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor accepts all risk of currency fluctuation throughout the Contract Period; no adjustment mechanism applies to the Contract Sum in respect of exchange rate movements.
notes: Full currency risk transferred to contractor in long-duration international projects.

text: SECTION 5 – VARIATIONS
notes: Contract section heading.

text: 'Commencement Date' means the date stated in the Letter of Acceptance from which the Contract Period is calculated.
notes: Standard definition; administrative.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall permit the Auditor General or any authorised representative to inspect all financial records relating to this Contract.
notes: Government audit access right; standard on public sector Zimbabwe contracts.

text: SECTION 10: TERMINATION
notes: Termination section heading.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, where a Relevant Event also causes the Contractor to incur additional costs, the Contractor shall be entitled to both extension of time and reimbursement of loss and expense.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor warrants that all rates in the Schedule of Rates are adequate and shall not be entitled to any adjustment for unforeseeable increases in cost.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall give a Defect Notice to the Engineer within 28 days of discovering a defect caused by the Employer.

text: It is hereby agreed that payment applications must be submitted in the prescribed format using the Employer's software system; applications in any other format shall not constitute valid applications.

text: SECTION 3 – CONTROL OF THE WORKS
notes: Contract section heading.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall be entitled to claim direct loss and expense for any act, omission or default of the Employer or Employer's Agent that causes disruption to the Works.
notes: Broad disruption claim entitlement covering employer defaults; protects contractor's commercial position.

text: Should the Sub-Contractor be liable to the Contractor for the payment of any sum of money, whether arising out of or in connection with this Sub-Contract or not, the Contractor shall be entitled to deduct from or set off against any monies due to the Sub-Contractor any sum so payable.
notes: Clause creates significant risk for subcontractor; one-sided in favour of contractor/employer.

text: SECTION 3: SCOPE OF WORKS
notes: Scope section heading.

text: The parties agree that practical Completion shall not be certified unless all snagging items and outstanding works have been fully completed to the Employer's Agent's absolute satisfaction.
notes: Subjective practical completion test prevents contractor triggering LAD stop; unlimited retention period.

text: It is hereby agreed that if a force majeure event continues for more than 90 days, either party may terminate the Contract by giving 14 days' notice; the Contractor shall be paid for work completed.
notes: Bilateral termination right on prolonged force majeure; contractor recovers value of work done.

text: CLAUSE 8 – COMMENCEMENT, DELAYS AND SUSPENSION
notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, escalation shall not apply to any element of the Contract Sum; the Contractor accepts all risks of price escalation in materials, labour and equipment for the full duration of the Contract.
notes: Full escalation risk on contractor in Zimbabwe's volatile inflation environment; commercially dangerous on long contracts.

text: The Contractor's share of any cost overrun shall be 100% for the first 2.5% of overrun and 50% thereafter; the Employer's share of savings is 70%.
notes: Asymmetric pain/gain share highly unfavourable to contractor; 100% pain on first 10% with only 30% gain.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, any acceleration instruction by the Employer shall constitute a variation entitling the Contractor to additional payment for overtime, additional resources and loss of productivity.
notes: Acceleration as a paid variation; prevents employer from demanding acceleration without compensation.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor may terminate if the Employer fails to pay a certified sum within 28 days of the final date for payment after 14 days' notice.
notes: Contractor termination right for employer non-payment; standard protection.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor warrants that all rates in the Schedule of Rates are adequate and shall not be entitled to any adjustment for unforeseeable increases in cost.
notes: Fixed-rate warranty removes all cost risk adjustment mechanisms; dangerous in Zimbabwe's volatile material cost environment.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, where the Contract Documents are inconsistent, the order of precedence shall be: the Agreement, then the Conditions, then the Employer's Requirements, then the Contractor's Proposals.
notes: Standard precedence clause; provides clarity in case of conflict.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall rectify any defect notified during the Defects Liability Period within 3 days of notification, failing which the Employer may engage others and recover costs at a premium rate of 150% of the cost incurred.
notes: Impossibly short rectification window; premium rate recovery is punitive and unjustifiable.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall be liable for delay damages at the stated rate from the scheduled Completion Date, whether or not the delay was caused partly by the Employer.
notes: LADs run even where employer is partly responsible; no concurrent delay apportionment.

text: SECTION 7 – TITLE
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall notify the Employer within 14 days of becoming aware of any circumstance that may give rise to a compensation event under clause 60.
notes: Notice obligation is standard; failure may reduce entitlement in practice.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, where the Employer issues an omission instruction, the Contractor shall be entitled to recover its preliminaries and time-related costs attributable to the omitted work.
notes: Preliminaries recovery on omissions; protects contractor's fixed cost recovery on scope reductions.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall take all reasonable precautions to prevent nuisance, disturbance or inconvenience to occupiers of adjacent properties.
notes: Reasonable neighbour obligation; qualified by 'reasonable precautions'.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, payment applications shall be submitted by the Contractor on the 25th of each month; the Employer's Quantity Surveyor shall certify within 14 days and payment shall be made within 30 days of certification.
notes: Payment cycle of approximately 44 days; acceptable but on the longer side for Zimbabwe market.

text: The parties agree that either party may terminate if a force majeure event continues for more than 180 days, with the Contractor entitled to all costs incurred to the termination date.
notes: FM termination after 180 days with full cost recovery; balanced and reasonable.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall submit an Environmental Management Plan in accordance with EMA regulations within 30 days of Contract Date.
notes: EMA compliance requirement; mandatory for infrastructure projects in Zimbabwe.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the seat of arbitration shall be Harare, Zimbabwe; the arbitrator shall have authority to grant interim relief.
notes: Harare-seated arbitration; appropriate for Zimbabwe construction disputes.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Employer shall appoint an Employer's Agent to act on its behalf in administering the Contract.
notes: Standard appointment provision; defines contract administration structure.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall be entitled to recover finance charges and legal costs incurred in pursuing overdue payments as a debt due under this Contract.
notes: Express right to recover finance costs and legal fees; strengthens contractor's cash flow protection.

text: The Contractor's share of any cost overrun shall be 100% for the first 15% of overrun and 50% thereafter; the Employer's share of savings is 70%.
notes: Asymmetric pain/gain share highly unfavourable to contractor; 100% pain on first 10% with only 30% gain.

text: SECTION 9: DISPUTE RESOLUTION
notes: Dispute resolution section heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall erect and maintain a Project Sign Board in accordance with the Employer's specifications within 7 days of Commencement.
notes: Signage obligation; administrative, low risk.

text: SECTION 1: PARTIES TO THE CONTRACT
notes: Contract parties section.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor maintains the Site as free from rubbish and the Contractor's equipment as the Project Manager instructs.
notes: Site tidiness obligation; standard.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor accepts all risk of currency fluctuation throughout the Contract Period; no adjustment mechanism applies to the Contract Sum in respect of exchange rate movements.
notes: Full currency risk transferred to contractor in long-duration international projects.

text: The Sub-Contract shall remain in full force and effect until all claims of the Contractor against the Sub-Contractor have been determined and vice versa, alternatively until the contract has been terminated by either party, in accordance with its terms, in which case Clause 12, being in relation to the resolution of disputes, shall be deemed to have survived such termination.

It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall rectify any defect notified during the Defects Liability Period within 3 days of notification, failing which the Employer may engage others and recover costs at a premium rate of 150% of the cost incurred.
notes: Impossibly short rectification window; premium rate recovery is punitive and unjustifiable.

text: The Employer may terminate the Contract for convenience by giving 28 days' notice; the Contractor shall be entitled only to Cost incurred plus 3% of the Cost as profit.
notes: Termination for convenience with limited profit entitlement; 3% profit capped regardless of anticipated margin.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall submit a cashflow forecast with each payment application to assist the Employer with funding arrangements.
notes: Cashflow forecasting obligation; transparent but creates administrative burden.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor repays to the Employer the amount of any overpayment on the next payment date.
notes: Overpayment recovery; balanced and expected.

text: SECTION 1: PARTIES TO THE CONTRACT
notes: Contract parties section.

text: CLAUSE 8 – COMMENCEMENT, DELAYS AND SUSPENSION
notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: The Contractor shall maintain all statutory insurances in Zimbabwe including Workers' Compensation, NSSA contributions and third party public liability at its own cost without any reimbursement from the Employer.
notes: Standard statutory insurance obligations in Zimbabwe; contractor bears cost but this is normal.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall not carry out any blasting operations without prior written approval from SADC Explosives Board and notification to the Employer's Representative.
notes: Blasting approval requirement; safety-critical and regulatory.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall be responsible for setting out the Works from the reference points provided and shall verify all dimensions before commencing construction.
notes: Contractor's setting-out responsibility; standard obligation.

text: 'Defined Cost' means the amounts calculated by applying the Schedule of Cost Components or Shorter Schedule of Cost Components.
notes: Standard NEC4 cost definition.

text: The parties agree that the Client may terminate the Contractor's obligation to Provide the Works at any time.
notes: Termination for convenience right; contractor receives defined cost plus profit entitlement under NEC4.

text: The Contractor shall, unless otherwise agreed, subject to and in accordance with the Principal Contract, make monthly application for Certificates of Payment and for the inclusion therein of the amount which at the date thereof fairly represents the value of the Sub-Contract Works and any authorised variations thereof then executed and of the materials and goods delivered upon the Site for use in the Sub-Contract Works.
The parties agree that where changes in Laws after the Base Date increase the Contractor's Costs, the Contract Price shall be adjusted accordingly.
notes: Change-in-law risk on employer; contractor protected from sovereign risk after Base Date.

text: It is hereby agreed that retention shall be held by the Employer in its general account and shall not attract interest; no obligation to place retention in a separate trust account arises.
notes: Retention is not ring-fenced; contractor has no protection if employer becomes insolvent.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, either party may terminate if a force majeure event continues for more than 180 days, with the Contractor entitled to all costs incurred to the termination date.
notes: FM termination after 180 days with full cost recovery; balanced and reasonable.

text: The Contractor shall commence the Works on the Date of Possession and shall proceed regularly and diligently.
notes: Commencement obligation; standard.

text: CLAUSE 2 – THE EMPLOYER
notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor maintains the Site as free from rubbish and the Contractor's equipment as the Project Manager instructs.
notes: Site tidiness obligation; standard.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor warrants that all subcontractors and suppliers will perform their obligations; the Contractor shall be liable for all defaults of subcontractors as if they were the Contractor's own defaults.
notes: Full vicarious liability for subcontractor defaults; removes contractor's ability to claim against employer for nominated sub failures.

text: In accordance with industry practice, all variations must be supported by a Variation Order signed by the Employer's Representative before work commences, except in cases of urgency where verbal instruction may be confirmed in writing within 48 hours.
notes: VO requirement with urgency exception; balanced but requires contractor diligence.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall retain title to all temporary works and equipment on site until paid in full; the Employer acknowledges no lien or right of retention over such items.
notes: Romalpa-style retention of title clause; protects contractor's plant and equipment on employer insolvency.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, all Intellectual Property created during the Works shall vest exclusively in the Employer upon creation without further payment to the Contractor.
notes: IP assignment without compensation; contractor loses rights to proprietary methods and designs.

text: CLAUSE 3 – THE ENGINEER
notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: Compliance with the quality assurance system shall not relieve the Subcontractor of any of his duties, obligations, or responsibilities under the Contract.
notes: Clause creates significant risk for subcontractor; one-sided in favour of contractor/employer.

text: In accordance with industry practice, extension of time shall be granted where the Contractor demonstrates on the balance of probabilities that a Relevant Event caused critical delay to Practical Completion.
notes: Causal link and balance of probabilities test is standard but requires robust programming evidence.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, where the Employer fails to certify payment within the prescribed period, the Contractor shall be entitled to suspend the Works after giving 7 days' written notice.
notes: Suspension right on late certification; strengthens contractor's cash flow protection in Zimbabwe's difficult payment environment.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor is entitled to a prevention event compensation where the event stops the Contractor completing the works and neither party could have prevented it.
notes: Prevention event CE; contractor recovers time and cost for unforeseeable force majeure events.

text: The Contractor shall give written notice to the Architect within 28 days of the occurrence of a Relevant Event and shall provide particulars within a further 14 days.
notes: Standard EOT notice provisions; strict but not unreasonable.

text: The law of the contract is the law of England and Wales.
notes: Governing law; standard for UK NEC4 contract.

text: The Employer shall pay the amounts certified by the Engineer in accordance with the Contract.
notes: Employer's fundamental payment obligation; standard.

text: 'Time for Completion' means the time for completing the Works or a Section as stated in the Contract Data, adjusted under Sub-Clause 8.5.
notes: Standard FIDIC completion time definition.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall attend progress meetings at such intervals as the Employer's Agent may reasonably require.
notes: Standard meeting obligation; reasonable frequency qualifier protects contractor.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor is entitled to a compensation event where the Employer or Others do not work within the times shown in the Accepted Programme.
notes: CE for Others' delay; contractor protected where employer-managed parties cause delay.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer shall give the Contractor possession of the Site on the Date of Possession stated in the Contract Particulars.
notes: Standard possession provision; balanced obligation on employer.

text: SECTION 1 – DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION
notes: Contract heading/section marker.

text: The Employer shall make an Advance Payment of 12.5% of the Accepted Contract Amount within 21 days of receipt of the Performance Security.
notes: FIDIC advance payment at 10%; standard and contractor-supportive.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, a Defect is a part of the works which is not in accordance with the Scope.
notes: Standard definition of defect.

text: 'Accepted Contract Amount' means the amount accepted in the Letter of Acceptance for the execution and completion of the Works and the remedying of any defects.
notes: Standard FIDIC definition.

text: In accordance with industry practice, disputes shall be referred to a Dispute Adjudication Board constituted in terms of FIDIC where the Contract is administered under FIDIC, or to the AFCCA in other cases.
notes: Tiered dispute resolution; appropriate for Zimbabwe construction disputes.

text: It is hereby agreed that where the Project Manager gives an instruction changing the Scope after the Contract Date, all additional Defined Cost and time is recoverable as a compensation event.
notes: Full cost and time recovery on scope change CE; standard NEC4 protection.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall comply with the Employer's Health, Safety and Environment policy as issued and updated from time to time.
notes: HSE policy compliance obligation; standard.

text: The Contractor shall give notice to the Engineer within 48 hours after the Contractor became aware, or should have become aware, of the event or circumstance giving rise to the claim; failing which the Contractor shall be entitled to no additional payment.
notes: FIDIC 2017 Red Book Clause 20.2.1 time bar; strict 28-day notice condition precedent with permanent loss of entitlement.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall take all reasonable precautions to prevent nuisance, disturbance or inconvenience to occupiers of adjacent properties.
notes: Reasonable neighbour obligation; qualified by 'reasonable precautions'.

text: The Contractor is to assess the compensation event using the Shorter Schedule of Cost Components where the work has not been done.
notes: SSCC use for prospective CEs; requires forecast which introduces uncertainty.

text: In accordance with industry practice, where a nominated subcontractor fails to perform, the Employer shall bear the resulting delay costs and shall grant an extension of time to the Contractor.
notes: Employer's risk for nominated subcontractor failure; contractor protected from third-party defaults imposed by employer.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall be liable for liquidated damages at the rate of USD 5,000 per day for every day of delay beyond the Completion Date, without any limit on the aggregate amount recoverable.
notes: Uncapped daily LADs in USD; creates severe and uninsurable financial exposure for Zimbabwean contractors.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, a Defect is a part of the works which is not in accordance with the Scope.
notes: Standard definition of defect.

text: SECTION 4 – PAYMENT
notes: Contract section heading.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, where the Contract Documents are inconsistent, the order of precedence shall be: the Agreement, then the Conditions, then the Employer's Requirements, then the Contractor's Proposals.
notes: Standard precedence clause; provides clarity in case of conflict.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall be entitled to a pre-agreed sum for demobilisation costs payable on Practical Completion.
notes: Express demobilisation payment; reduces contractor's post-completion financial exposure.

text: SECTION 5 – VARIATIONS
notes: Contract section heading.

text: 'Practical Completion' means the stage at which the Works are practically complete as certified by the Architect.
notes: Standard definition; pivotal trigger for retention release and LAD cessation.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor is to refer a dispute to the Dispute Avoidance Board within 4 weeks of becoming aware of the dispute.
notes: DAB referral timeframe; standard NEC4 W3 procedure.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall not be entitled to any additional payment for compliance with any new law, regulation or standard enacted after the Contract Date.
notes: Full post-contract regulatory risk on contractor; particularly dangerous in Zimbabwe's evolving regulatory environment.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, either party shall give 14 days' written notice of any change in address for service of notices.
notes: Notice address update obligation; administrative.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall provide product warranties from all specialist suppliers in the Employer's name for a minimum of 10 years.
notes: Third-party warranty assignment obligation; requires supplier co-operation.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor accepts the risk of legal changes to the law of any country applicable to the Contractor's work and no compensation event arises from change in law.
notes: Change-in-law risk transferred to contractor; contrary to standard NEC4 CE 60.1(18).

text: SECTION 6 – INJURY, DAMAGE AND INSURANCE
notes: Contract section heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Architect shall issue Interim Payment Certificates at the intervals stated in the Contract Particulars, and the Employer shall pay within 14 days of the due date.
notes: Clear payment timeline; HGCRA compliant.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall attend progress meetings at such intervals as the Employer's Agent may reasonably require.
notes: Standard meeting obligation; reasonable frequency qualifier protects contractor.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor is to prepare the first programme to show the information stated in this Contract within the period stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Programme submission obligation; standard NEC4 requirement.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, all cement, steel, bitumen and other principal materials shall be sourced from Zimbabwean manufacturers where available and comparable in quality and price.
notes: Local sourcing preference; may restrict contractor's supply chain and increase material risk.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, where the Employer terminates for convenience, the Contractor is entitled to Defined Cost of work done, profit on work done, and a fee for breaking subcontracts.
notes: Termination for convenience with full cost recovery including profit; standard NEC4 R21 entitlement.

text: 16.1 The Contractor shall, at all times during the subsistence of this Agreement, maintain business ethics standards aimed at avoiding real or apparent impropriety or conflicts of interest. No substantial gifts, entertainment, payments, loans or other considerations beyond incidental shall be made to any employees or officials of the Principal, its authorized agents and representatives, or to family members. Should the Contractor come to know of a possible violation, it shall notify the Principal. The Principal may request a representation letter. The Contractor acknowledges the OECD Guidelines for responsible sourcing of PGMs and agrees to render Services ethically and provide information for auditing. Any breach of the Guidelines shall be considered a breach of a material term entitling the Principal to terminate the Agreement.
Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor warrants that the completed Works will achieve the energy performance rating specified in the Employer's Requirements.
notes: Performance warranty; creates ongoing liability if building underperforms.

text: The parties agree that any variation instructed by the Employer shall be valued by the Employer's Quantity Surveyor, whose valuation shall be final and binding on the Contractor.
notes: Removes contractor's right to challenge variation valuations through dispute resolution.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, all disputes shall be finally resolved by a panel of three arbitrators appointed by the ICC; the Contractor waives the right to challenge any ICC appointment.
notes: ICC arbitration with waiver of appointment challenge; reduces contractor's procedural rights.

text: The Sub-Contractor shall make available one of his staff who will be responsible to the Contractor's Safety and Security Officer for all safety, health and security rules and regulations governing the Site and the Works.

Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall not assign this Contract or any part of it without the prior written consent of the Employer, which shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed.
notes: Anti-assignment clause with reasonableness qualifier; balanced provision.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, sub-contracting of portions of the Works exceeding 25% of the Contract Sum requires prior written consent from the Employer's Agent.
notes: Sub-contracting threshold consent requirement is commercially acceptable.

text: The seat of arbitration shall be Harare, Zimbabwe; the arbitrator shall have authority to grant interim relief.
notes: Harare-seated arbitration; appropriate for Zimbabwe construction disputes.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall comply with the Employer's site rules and environmental management plan as updated from time to time during the Contract Period.
notes: Compliance with evolving site rules creates some uncertainty but is standard on managed sites.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, any claim not submitted within 14 days of the cause of action arising is time-barred and shall not be considered.
notes: 14-day claim bar is impractically short; causes loss of legitimate claims on complex projects.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, retention shall be held by the Employer in its general account and shall not attract interest; no obligation to place retention in a separate trust account arises.
notes: Retention is not ring-fenced; contractor has no protection if employer becomes insolvent.

text: The Employer shall provide the Site to the Contractor on or before the Commencement Date.
notes: Standard FIDIC site possession obligation; employer duty.

text: This Contract may be executed in counterparts, each of which when executed shall constitute an original.
notes: Counterparts execution; standard administrative provision.

text: This contract is a NEC4 Engineering and Construction Contract.
notes: Contract identification clause.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor's share of any cost overrun shall be 100% for the first 10% of overrun and 50% thereafter; the Employer's share of savings is 70%.
notes: Asymmetric pain/gain share highly unfavourable to contractor; 100% pain on first 10% with only 30% gain.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, if the Contractor becomes insolvent, the Employer shall have the right to complete the Works using the Contractor's workforce and equipment without further payment.
notes: Employer's step-in right using contractor's resources without additional payment; commercially punitive.

text: If a force majeure event continues for more than 90 days, either party may terminate the Contract by giving 14 days' notice; the Contractor shall be paid for work completed.
notes: Bilateral termination right on prolonged force majeure; contractor recovers value of work done.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall not be entitled to a compensation event where the Contractor failed to give early warning of the matter which caused the event.
notes: Loss of CE entitlement for failure to give early warning; strict application has harsh financial effect.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, any amounts due to the Contractor may be set off against any claim the Employer may have against the Contractor under any other contract between the parties.
notes: Cross-contract set-off right; allows employer to use disputes on other contracts to withhold current payments.

text: SPECIAL CONDITIONS OF CONTRACT
notes: Document heading; modifications to general conditions.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Project Manager may give an instruction requiring a Defect to be corrected within a shorter defect correction period than the one stated in the Contract Data.
notes: PM can shorten defect correction period; some contractor risk but subject to reasonableness.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall not be entitled to any additional payment for compliance with any new law, regulation or standard enacted after the Contract Date.
notes: Full post-contract regulatory risk on contractor; particularly dangerous in Zimbabwe's evolving regulatory environment.

text: It is hereby agreed that disputes shall be referred to a Dispute Adjudication Board constituted in terms of FIDIC where the Contract is administered under FIDIC, or to the AFCCA in other cases.
notes: Tiered dispute resolution; appropriate for Zimbabwe construction disputes.

text: 'Accepted Contract Amount' means the amount accepted in the Letter of Acceptance for the execution and completion of the Works and the remedying of any defects.
notes: Standard FIDIC definition.

notes: Information provision obligation; standard.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor is entitled to a compensation event where the Project Manager gives an instruction to stop work, and the stop lasts more than 13 weeks, and no further instruction is given.
notes: Prolonged stop-work triggers CE; protects contractor's standing costs.

notes: Anti-canon clause protecting contractor from employer using omission instructions to divert work to others.

text: 'Key Date' means the date by which the Contractor is to meet a Condition stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Standard NEC4 Key Date definition.

text: The parties agree that the Employer may at any time instruct the removal of the Contractor's key personnel, and the Contractor shall have no right to object or seek compensation for the disruption caused.
notes: Employer can disrupt contractor's team without justification or compensation.

text: In accordance with industry practice, payment of the Contract Sum shall be 50% in United States Dollars and 50% in Zimbabwe Gold (ZiG) at the official exchange rate published by RBZ on the payment date.
notes: Dual-currency payment mechanism; reflects Zimbabwe's multi-currency environment; ZiG portion carries exchange rate risk.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor is entitled to a compensation event where the Project Manager gives an instruction to stop work, and the stop lasts more than 13 weeks, and no further instruction is given.
notes: Prolonged stop-work triggers CE; protects contractor's standing costs.

text: In accordance with industry practice, where the Employer fails to certify payment within the prescribed period, the Contractor shall be entitled to suspend the Works after giving 7 days' written notice.

text: The parties agree that the Employer shall pay the amounts certified by the Engineer in accordance with the Contract.
notes: Employer's fundamental payment obligation; standard.

text: The parties agree that where the Contract Sum is denominated in USD, all payments shall be made in USD or at the parallel market rate, whichever is more favourable to the Contractor.
notes: Parallel market rate protection; significant financial benefit for contractor in multi-currency Zimbabwe environment.

text: It is hereby agreed that where a nominated subcontractor fails to perform, the Employer shall bear the resulting delay costs and shall grant an extension of time to the Contractor.
notes: Employer's risk for nominated subcontractor failure; contractor protected from third-party defaults imposed by employer.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall submit an Environmental Management Plan in accordance with EMA regulations within 30 days of Contract Date.

text: SECTION 9: DISPUTE RESOLUTION
notes: Dispute resolution section heading.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall ensure that all waste generated during the Works is disposed of in accordance with EMA regulations.
notes: Waste disposal obligation; EMA compliance standard.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall keep a daily site diary recording weather conditions, labour strength, materials received and work carried out.
notes: Site record-keeping obligation; standard practice.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the final account shall be settled within 180 days of the issue of the Defects Certificate; any disputed items shall be referred to adjudication.
notes: 180-day final account settlement with adjudication backstop; reasonable overall.

text: CLAUSE 4 – THE CONTRACTOR
notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Engineer shall act neutrally between the parties in all matters under this Contract.
notes: FIDIC 2017 engineer neutrality obligation; fundamental balanced provision.

text: The parties agree that the Defects Liability Period shall be 12 months from the date of Practical Completion during which the Contractor shall rectify defects notified by the Employer within 21 days.
notes: Standard DLP provision; 21-day notice-to-rectify window is reasonable.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall notify the Employer within 14 days of becoming aware of any circumstance that may give rise to a compensation event under clause 60.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, a Defect is a part of the works which is not in accordance with the Scope.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer may terminate the Contractor's employment at will and without cause upon giving 7 days' written notice, whereupon the Contractor shall be entitled only to payment for work properly executed.

text: It is hereby agreed that where the exchange rate moves by more than 5% against the Contractor between the Base Date and the payment date, the Contract Price shall be adjusted by the difference.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, extension of time shall be granted where the Contractor demonstrates on the balance of probabilities that a Relevant Event caused critical delay to Practical Completion.
