text: PART II – PARTICULAR CONDITIONS
notes: FIDIC particular conditions heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, where the Employer fails to provide the Site by the agreed date, the Contractor is entitled to an extension of time and Cost plus profit.
notes: Full recovery (time + cost + profit) for employer's failure to provide site; strong contractor protection.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall maintain a site diary recording daily weather conditions, labour deployed and progress of the Works.
notes: Record-keeping obligation; protects both parties in claims situations.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall be responsible for obtaining all statutory approvals, permits and licences required for the Works at its own cost, and delays arising from the permitting process shall not entitle the Contractor to any extension of time.
notes: Contractor bears permitting risk in a jurisdiction with unpredictable regulatory timelines.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Employer provides the Site and the means of access to and from the Site to the Contractor on time.
notes: Employer's access obligation; breach is a CE.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, either party may give notice of adjudication at any time; the adjudicator shall be appointed by the RICS within 5 days of the notice.
notes: Standard RICS adjudicator nomination; balanced and practical.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the risk register is issued to the Contractor at the starting date.
notes: Risk register provision; promotes transparent risk management.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Engineer may issue a Taking-Over Certificate when the Works are substantially complete.
notes: Taking-over certification; milestone trigger for risk transfer.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall be responsible for obtaining all statutory approvals, permits and licences required for the Works at its own cost, and delays arising from the permitting process shall not entitle the Contractor to any extension of time.
notes: Contractor bears permitting risk in a jurisdiction with unpredictable regulatory timelines.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer shall provide the Site to the Contractor on or before the Commencement Date.
notes: Standard FIDIC site possession obligation; employer duty.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, force majeure events include epidemic, pandemic, earthquake, flood, fire, war, civil commotion, sanctions, government action and any other event beyond the reasonable control of the affected party.
notes: Broad force majeure definition including government action; well-suited to Zimbabwe's political risk environment.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall provide a performance bond in the sum of 10% of the Contract Sum within 14 days of execution of the Contract.
notes: Performance bond at 10% is at the higher end; bond cost is a contractor risk.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor's liability shall not be limited under any circumstances; any contractual cap on liability is expressly excluded.
notes: Removal of any liability cap; contractor faces unlimited financial exposure.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor warrants that all rates in the Schedule of Rates are adequate and shall not be entitled to any adjustment for unforeseeable increases in cost.
notes: Fixed-rate warranty removes all cost risk adjustment mechanisms; dangerous in Zimbabwe's volatile material cost environment.

text: SECTION 2: DEFINITIONS
notes: Definitions section heading.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer shall pay the Contract Price in the currency or currencies stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Currency of payment clause; basic obligation.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall be entitled to an extension of time and additional cost for delays caused by unavailability of foreign currency for imported materials, provided such unavailability is documented by a bank.
notes: Forex-induced delay recognised as relief event; critical protection in Zimbabwe's USD-constrained environment.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall not be entitled to a compensation event where the Contractor failed to give early warning of the matter which caused the event.
notes: Loss of CE entitlement for failure to give early warning; strict application has harsh financial effect.

text: SECTION 7: INSURANCE
notes: Insurance section heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, practical Completion shall not be certified unless all snagging items and outstanding works have been fully completed to the Employer's Agent's absolute satisfaction.
notes: Subjective practical completion test prevents contractor triggering LAD stop; unlimited retention period.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall not be entitled to any uplift on the rates and prices in the Contract Bills for any variation, regardless of the nature, complexity or volume of the varied work.
notes: Fixed-rate variations with no uplift for changed conditions; erodes contractor margin on complex variations.

text: 'Relevant Event' means any of the events listed in clause 2.29 of these Conditions.
notes: Cross-reference definition; no risk allocation.

text: Where the Employer fails to issue a payment notice or pay less notice by the prescribed date, the sum stated in the Contractor's payment application shall become the sum due.
notes: Default payment mechanism under HGCRA; contractor's application becomes notified sum if employer fails to serve notice.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor is entitled to a compensation event where the Project Manager gives an instruction to stop work, and the stop lasts more than 13 weeks, and no further instruction is given.
notes: Prolonged stop-work triggers CE; protects contractor's standing costs.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, interest shall accrue on certified but unpaid sums at LIBOR plus 2% from the due date for payment.
notes: Interest on overdue sums; LIBOR-linked rate is market standard.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall submit all CE quotations within 21 days; late submission means the PM's own assessment is binding on the Contractor without review.
notes: PM's assessment binding on late quotation; removes contractor's ability to challenge PM's figures.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall be responsible for setting out the Works from the reference points provided and shall verify all dimensions before commencing construction.
notes: Contractor's setting-out responsibility; standard obligation.

text: The Contractor's liability shall not be limited under any circumstances; any contractual cap on liability is expressly excluded.
notes: Removal of any liability cap; contractor faces unlimited financial exposure.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall be liable for delay damages at the stated rate from the scheduled Completion Date, whether or not the delay was caused partly by the Employer.
notes: LADs run even where employer is partly responsible; no concurrent delay apportionment.

text: C-01 CONTRACT DOCUMENTS
notes: Heading or procedural clause; no material risk allocation.

text: The Engineer shall issue Payment Certificates within 21 days of receiving the Contractor's Statement.
notes: 28-day certification period; standard FIDIC payment cycle.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall bear all additional costs arising from unforeseeable physical conditions unless the Engineer certifies that such conditions could not reasonably have been foreseen by an experienced contractor.
notes: High bar for unforeseeable conditions relief; Engineer's gatekeeping removes automatic entitlement.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Employer shall provide the Site to the Contractor on or before the Commencement Date.
notes: Standard FIDIC site possession obligation; employer duty.

text: 'Accepted Contract Amount' means the amount accepted in the Letter of Acceptance for the execution and completion of the Works and the remedying of any defects.
notes: Standard FIDIC definition.

text: SECTION 9: DISPUTE RESOLUTION
notes: Dispute resolution section heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, where the final account value exceeds the Contract Sum by more than 15%, the Contractor shall be entitled to re-price preliminaries and time-related items.
notes: Preliminaries re-pricing right on substantial scope increase; protects contractor on poorly scoped projects.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall submit an Environmental Management Plan in accordance with EMA regulations within 30 days of Contract Date.
notes: EMA compliance requirement; mandatory for infrastructure projects in Zimbabwe.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall submit a detailed construction programme within 14 days of the Date of Possession and shall update it monthly or following any significant change.
notes: Programme obligation is standard; failure to comply may affect EOT entitlement.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor corrects notified Defects before the defect correction date.
notes: Standard defect correction obligation; reasonable timeframe.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, any amounts due to the Contractor may be set off against any claim the Employer may have against the Contractor under any other contract between the parties.
notes: Cross-contract set-off right; allows employer to use disputes on other contracts to withhold current payments.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, if the Contractor becomes insolvent, the Employer shall have the right to complete the Works using the Contractor's workforce and equipment without further payment.
notes: Employer's step-in right using contractor's resources without additional payment; commercially punitive.

text: It is hereby agreed that where the final account value exceeds the Contract Sum by more than 15%, the Contractor shall be entitled to re-price preliminaries and time-related items.
notes: Preliminaries re-pricing right on substantial scope increase; protects contractor on poorly scoped projects.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Project Manager may assess a compensation event at any time and the Contractor shall have no right to submit its own quotation.
notes: Removal of contractor's right to quote for CEs; PM-only assessment is contrary to NEC4 spirit.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer provides the Site and the means of access to and from the Site to the Contractor on time.
notes: Employer's access obligation; breach is a CE.

text: 'The Fee' means the tendered fee percentage applied to Defined Cost.
notes: Standard NEC4 fee definition.

text: Any loss or damage to the same or caused by the same shall, subject to the provisions of Clause 3.c) hereof, be the sole responsibility of the Sub-Contractor who indemnifies the Contractor against any loss, claim, or proceedings in respect thereof.
notes: Clause creates significant risk for subcontractor; one-sided in favour of contractor/employer.

text: SECTION 9 – SETTLEMENT OF DISPUTES
notes: Contract section heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor warrants that all subcontractors and suppliers will perform their obligations; the Contractor shall be liable for all defaults of subcontractors as if they were the Contractor's own defaults.
notes: Full vicarious liability for subcontractor defaults; removes contractor's ability to claim against employer for nominated sub failures.

text: GENERAL CONDITIONS OF CONTRACT
notes: Document heading.

text: SECTION 6 – COMPENSATION EVENTS
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall submit a cashflow forecast with each payment application to assist the Employer with funding arrangements.
notes: Cashflow forecasting obligation; transparent but creates administrative burden.

text: SECTION 4 – TESTING AND DEFECTS
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Employer shall provide such information as the Contractor may reasonably require to carry out the Works.
notes: Mutual information obligation; protects contractor's right to information.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, all risk of ground conditions at the Site remains with the Contractor regardless of the Site Information provided; no compensation event arises from physical conditions.
notes: Full ground risk on contractor even where site information proves inaccurate; contrary to Z60.1 CE.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, if a force majeure event continues for more than 90 days, either party may terminate the Contract by giving 14 days' notice; the Contractor shall be paid for work completed.
notes: Bilateral termination right on prolonged force majeure; contractor recovers value of work done.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, where the Contract Documents are inconsistent, the order of precedence shall be: the Agreement, then the Conditions, then the Employer's Requirements, then the Contractor's Proposals.
notes: Standard precedence clause; provides clarity in case of conflict.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer's Agent may issue instructions requiring the Contractor to open up work for inspection; where the work is found to be in accordance with the Contract, the cost shall be added to the Contract Sum.
notes: Cost recovery mechanism for compliant open-up is fair; risk of disruption remains.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, any acceleration instruction by the Employer shall constitute a variation entitling the Contractor to additional payment for overtime, additional resources and loss of productivity.
notes: Acceleration as a paid variation; prevents employer from demanding acceleration without compensation.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor provides services and facilities listed in the Scope at the times and for the periods stated.
notes: Service provision obligation; scope-dependent risk.

text: It is hereby agreed that either party may terminate if a force majeure event continues for more than 180 days, with the Contractor entitled to all costs incurred to the termination date.
notes: FM termination after 180 days with full cost recovery; balanced and reasonable.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, arbitration shall be conducted under the Zimbabwe Arbitration Act [Chapter 7:15] and the rules of the Arbitration Foundation of Southern Africa (AFSA).
notes: Zimbabwe arbitration framework; appropriate institutional rules for regional commercial disputes.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, sub-contracting of portions of the Works exceeding 25% of the Contract Sum requires prior written consent from the Employer's Agent.
notes: Sub-contracting threshold consent requirement is commercially acceptable.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor corrects notified Defects before the defect correction date.
notes: Standard defect correction obligation; reasonable timeframe.

text: 'Accepted Contract Amount' means the amount accepted in the Letter of Acceptance for the execution and completion of the Works and the remedying of any defects.
notes: Standard FIDIC definition.

text: OBLIGATIONS OF CONTRACTOR
notes: Heading or procedural clause; no material risk allocation.

text: The CONTRACTOR warrants that it has not given or promised to give any money or gift to any official or employee of the XYZ and any violation of this warranty shall be sufficient ground for XYZ to revoke or extra judicially cancel this Contract.
notes: Clause creates significant risk for subcontractor; one-sided in favour of contractor/employer.

text: The programme and the cash flow forecast shall be subject to review and, if so instructed by the Contractor, shall be adjusted by the Subcontractor on a regular monthly basis, or at such intervals as circumstances may require, or where a specific probable future event or circumstance may increase the Contract Price or delay the execution of the Works.
All payments shall be subject to deduction of withholding tax at the rate applicable under Zimbabwe Revenue Authority regulations.
notes: ZIMRA withholding tax deduction; statutory requirement reducing contractor's net receipts.

notes: SSCC use for prospective CEs; requires forecast which introduces uncertainty.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor accepts all risk of currency fluctuation throughout the Contract Period; no adjustment mechanism applies to the Contract Sum in respect of exchange rate movements.

text: SECTION 2: DEFINITIONS
notes: Definitions section heading.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor's share of any cost overrun shall be 100% for the first 10% of overrun and 50% thereafter; the Employer's share of savings is 70%.
notes: Asymmetric pain/gain share highly unfavourable to contractor; 100% pain on first 10% with only 30% gain.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall be entitled to a pre-agreed sum for demobilisation costs payable on Practical Completion.
notes: Express demobilisation payment; reduces contractor's post-completion financial exposure.

text: The parties agree that the Engineer shall act neutrally between the parties in all matters under this Contract.
notes: FIDIC 2017 engineer neutrality obligation; fundamental balanced provision.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, either party may give notice of adjudication at any time; the adjudicator shall be appointed by the RICS within 5 days of the notice.
notes: Standard RICS adjudicator nomination; balanced and practical.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Architect shall issue Interim Payment Certificates at the intervals stated in the Contract Particulars, and the Employer shall pay within 14 days of the due date.
notes: Clear payment timeline; HGCRA compliant.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall maintain a site diary recording daily weather conditions, labour deployed and progress of the Works.

notes: Clause creates significant risk for subcontractor; one-sided in favour of contractor/employer.

notes: Tiered dispute resolution; appropriate for Zimbabwe construction disputes.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor Provides the Works in accordance with the Scope and this Contract.
notes: Fundamental contractor obligation; standard.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall bear the cost of all power, water, telecommunications and site services throughout the Contract Period; no allowance has been made in the Contract Sum.
notes: Undisclosed service costs to be borne by contractor; creates hidden financial risk.

text: The parties agree that all cement, steel, bitumen and other principal materials shall be sourced from Zimbabwean manufacturers where available and comparable in quality and price.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Project Manager gives access to the Site to the Contractor on the access date stated in Contract Data Part 1.
notes: Standard access provision; employer obligation.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall give notice to the Engineer within 28 days after the Contractor became aware, or should have become aware, of the event or circumstance giving rise to the claim; failing which the Contractor shall be entitled to no additional payment.
notes: FIDIC 2017 Red Book Clause 20.2.1 time bar; strict 28-day notice condition precedent with permanent loss of entitlement.

text: The Contractor shall give written notice to the Architect within 21 days of the occurrence of a Relevant Event and shall provide particulars within a further 14 days.
notes: Standard EOT notice provisions; strict but not unreasonable.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, in the event of any dispute, the Employer's decision shall be final and binding; the Contractor shall continue to perform the Works pending any subsequent arbitration.
notes: Employer as sole arbiter of disputes; no independent decision-making; contractor must perform under adverse decisions.

text: Where the Employer fails to take possession of the completed Works within 14 days of Practical Completion, the Contractor shall be entitled to storage charges at a rate of 7.5% of the Contract Sum per week.
notes: Storage charges on employer's failure to take possession; monetises employer-caused delay post-completion.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor is to refer a dispute to the Dispute Avoidance Board within 4 weeks of becoming aware of the dispute.
notes: DAB referral timeframe; standard NEC4 W3 procedure.

notes: Sub-contracting threshold consent requirement is commercially acceptable.

text: The Contractor shall employ a minimum of 70% local Zimbabwean labour in the execution of the Works.
notes: Local content labour requirement; common condition on government-funded Zimbabwe projects.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall rectify all defects notified during the Defects Liability Period at its own cost, including defects caused by fair wear and tear, acts of God or third parties.
notes: Warranty extends to fair wear and tear and force majeure damage; far beyond standard contractor warranty obligations.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor is to prepare the first programme to show the information stated in this Contract within the period stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Programme submission obligation; standard NEC4 requirement.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, no payment shall be made for materials off-site unless the Contractor provides proof of insurance, vesting certificate and list of materials.
notes: Off-site materials payment conditions; reasonable certification requirements.

notes: Variation approval subject to reasonable discretion; some restriction on contractor flexibility.

text: KNOW ALL MEN BY THESE PRESENTS: This Contract is executed and entered into this 12th day of January, 2003 at Quezon City, Philippines, by and between: XYZ ADMINISTRATION, a government-owned and controlled corporation created and existing under Republic Act No. 3601, as amended by Presidential Decree Nos. 552 and 1702, with principal office address at XYZ Building, EDSA, Diliman, Quezon City, Metro Manila, represented herein by Administrator, Juan de la Cruz, who duly authorized to represent it in this Contract, hereinafter referred to as the XYZ; -and- A-B-C JOINT VENTURE, a joint venture which are duly organized and existing individual corporations under and by virtue of the laws of Japan, represented herein by the Authorized Representative, Kimono Kimono, General Manager of Philippine Branch, A-B-C who is duly authorized to represent said joint venture in this Contract, hereinafter referred to as the CONTRACTOR.
notes: Heading or procedural clause; no material risk allocation.

text: GENERAL CONDITIONS OF CONTRACT
notes: Document heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, retention shall be at the rate of 5% until Practical Completion, reducing to 2.5% thereafter, and shall be released at the end of the Defects Liability Period.
notes: Standard retention regime; rates are at the higher end of market practice.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor maintains the Site as free from rubbish and the Contractor's equipment as the Project Manager instructs.
notes: Site tidiness obligation; standard.

text: CLAUSE 3 – THE ENGINEER
notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer shall pay the Contract Price in the currency or currencies stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Currency of payment clause; basic obligation.

text: 'Defined Cost' means the amounts calculated by applying the Schedule of Cost Components or Shorter Schedule of Cost Components.
notes: Standard NEC4 cost definition.

text: The parties agree that the Defects Liability Period shall be extended by the period of any defect rectification; a single recurring defect may extend the DLP indefinitely.
notes: Rolling DLP extension creates perpetual warranty exposure; no limit on total DLP duration.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer may instruct the Contractor to accelerate the programme; the Contractor shall comply and any claim for acceleration costs shall be submitted within 7 days of the instruction.
notes: 7-day acceleration claim window is impractical; employer can demand acceleration without pre-agreeing cost.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall submit an Environmental Management Plan in accordance with EMA regulations within 30 days of Contract Date.
notes: EMA compliance requirement; mandatory for infrastructure projects in Zimbabwe.

text: The Contract shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the law of England and Wales.
notes: Governing law clause; standard for UK-based JCT contract.

text: The parties agree that the Project Manager certifies a payment in the amount due within one week of each assessment date.
notes: Payment certification timeline; standard NEC4.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall bear all costs of independent testing regardless of the test results; no cost recovery applies where tests confirm compliance.
notes: All testing costs on contractor; creates financial disincentive to test and verify compliance.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall submit a programme to the Project Manager within 21 days of Contract Date; failure to submit an accepted programme means that 25% of all sums otherwise due to the Contractor shall be retained.
notes: Punitive 25% payment withholding for programme failure; unusually aggressive sanction.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Architect's decision on any matter concerning the quality or quantity of the Works shall be final and binding and shall not be subject to review in any dispute resolution proceedings.
notes: Ousts contractor's right to challenge Architect's decisions; contrary to HGCRA principles.

text: SCHEDULE OF RATES AND QUANTITIES
notes: Pricing document heading.

text: 'Contract Sum' means the sum named in Article 2, as adjusted in accordance with the provisions of this Contract.
notes: Standard definition of contract price.

text: SECTION 4 – TESTING AND DEFECTS
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall maintain professional indemnity insurance in the sum of USD 500,000 for 6 years following Practical Completion.
notes: PI insurance obligation post-completion; standard on design-and-build contracts.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Engineer shall act neutrally between the parties in all matters under this Contract.
notes: FIDIC 2017 engineer neutrality obligation; fundamental balanced provision.

text: On termination for employer convenience, the Contractor shall be entitled to the value of work executed, materials on site, reasonable overheads and 7.5% of the remaining works as lost profit.
notes: Generous termination for convenience entitlement including lost profit; contractor-favourable.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor provides services and facilities listed in the Scope at the times and for the periods stated.
notes: Service provision obligation; scope-dependent risk.

text: 'Contract Price' means the sum stated in the Letter of Acceptance, subject to adjustment in accordance with this Contract.
notes: Standard contract price definition.

text: The Contractor shall give a Notice of Claim as soon as practicable and in any event within 14 days of the event or circumstance first arising.
notes: 28-day CE notification obligation; standard FIDIC time bar.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, all Contractor's equipment, materials and temporary works brought onto Site shall be deemed to be the property of the Employer for the duration of the Contract and shall not be removed without Employer's written consent.
notes: Deemed vesting of contractor's property; exposes contractor to loss if employer becomes insolvent or hostile.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer may withhold payment of any certified sum provided it issues a Pay Less Notice not later than the prescribed period before the final date for payment.
notes: HGCRA-compliant pay less notice mechanism; contractor has clear right to challenge.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor accepts no warranty liability for defects arising from the Employer's design or specification.
notes: Design warranty carve-out for employer-provided design; fair on contractor.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, where the Contractor fails to notify a compensation event within 8 weeks, the Contractor loses entitlement to any time or cost adjustment arising from that event.
notes: 8-week notification bar is strict; permanent loss of entitlement is a significant risk.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall retain title to all temporary works and equipment on site until paid in full; the Employer acknowledges no lien or right of retention over such items.
notes: Romalpa-style retention of title clause; protects contractor's plant and equipment on employer insolvency.

text: SECTION 9 – TERMINATION
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, where a force majeure event affects both parties, each shall bear its own costs and no claim shall be made against the other.
notes: Bilateral cost-bearing on shared FM events; balanced loss allocation.

text: It is hereby agreed that payment of the final account shall only become due when the Employer has received all as-built drawings, O&M manuals, warranties and training certificates in a form satisfactory to the Employer.
notes: Final payment held hostage to administrative deliverables, giving employer disproportionate leverage after practical completion.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall be liable for delay damages at the stated rate from the scheduled Completion Date, whether or not the delay was caused partly by the Employer.
notes: LADs run even where employer is partly responsible; no concurrent delay apportionment.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, all Intellectual Property created during the Works shall vest exclusively in the Employer upon creation without further payment to the Contractor.
notes: IP assignment without compensation; contractor loses rights to proprietary methods and designs.

text: 'Relevant Event' means any of the events listed in clause 2.29 of these Conditions.
notes: Cross-reference definition; no risk allocation.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall rectify any defect notified during the Defects Liability Period within 3 days of notification, failing which the Employer may engage others and recover costs at a premium rate of 150% of the cost incurred.
notes: Impossibly short rectification window; premium rate recovery is punitive and unjustifiable.

text: The Contractor is entitled to a prevention event compensation where the event stops the Contractor completing the works and neither party could have prevented it.
notes: Prevention event CE; contractor recovers time and cost for unforeseeable force majeure events.

text: CLAUSE 8 – COMMENCEMENT, DELAYS AND SUSPENSION
notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, where the final account value exceeds the Contract Sum by more than 15%, the Contractor shall be entitled to re-price preliminaries and time-related items.
notes: Preliminaries re-pricing right on substantial scope increase; protects contractor on poorly scoped projects.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall be liable for delay damages at the stated rate from the scheduled Completion Date, whether or not the delay was caused partly by the Employer.
notes: LADs run even where employer is partly responsible; no concurrent delay apportionment.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, where practical completion is delayed, the Employer may call upon the performance bond in full without proving loss.
notes: On-demand bond call without proof of loss; contractor's bond is vulnerable to abuse.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall complete the Works within the time stated even if access, materials or instructions are delayed; no extension of time shall be granted for any cause whatsoever.
notes: Absolute completion obligation with no EOT; imposes strict liability for delays not within contractor's control.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer shall nominate and pay directly any nominated subcontractors; the Contractor shall be responsible for coordinating nominated subcontractors but not for their defaults.
notes: Nominated subcontractor mechanism; limits contractor's liability for employer-selected specialists.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, payment of the Contract Sum shall be 50% in United States Dollars and 50% in Zimbabwe Gold (ZiG) at the official exchange rate published by RBZ on the payment date.
notes: Dual-currency payment mechanism; reflects Zimbabwe's multi-currency environment; ZiG portion carries exchange rate risk.

text: SECTION 8 – RISKS AND INSURANCE
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: 'Compensation Event' means an event which entitles the Contractor to changes to the Prices and Completion Date.
notes: Standard NEC4 compensation event definition.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall not be entitled to a compensation event where the Contractor failed to give early warning of the matter which caused the event.
notes: Loss of CE entitlement for failure to give early warning; strict application has harsh financial effect.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Employer's right to terminate shall not arise where the alleged breach is caused by the Employer's own act or omission.
notes: Employer's concurrent breach prevents termination; protects contractor where employer contributes to the breach.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, sub-contracting of portions of the Works exceeding 25% of the Contract Sum requires prior written consent from the Employer's Agent.
notes: Sub-contracting threshold consent requirement is commercially acceptable.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall ensure that all waste generated during the Works is disposed of in accordance with EMA regulations.
notes: Waste disposal obligation; EMA compliance standard.

notes: Contract section heading.

notes: Standard NEC4 document heading.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Employer may grant an extension of time for delays caused by force majeure events including acts of God, floods, civil unrest, government action or unavailability of foreign currency.
notes: Force majeure definition includes Zimbabwe-specific risks (forex, civil unrest); balanced provision.

notes: Standard NEC4 document heading.

For the avoidance of doubt, the Employer shall pay the Contractor a mobilisation advance of 15% of the Contract Sum, secured by an advance payment guarantee, within 30 days of contract execution.
notes: Mobilisation advance at 15%; strong cash flow support for contractor on project start.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Project Manager issues a Completion Certificate when the Contractor has done all the work necessary to complete the works.

text: ANNEXURE A: CONTRACT DATA
notes: Contract data annexure heading.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, where the exchange rate moves by more than 5% against the Contractor between the Base Date and the payment date, the Contract Price shall be adjusted by the difference.
notes: Exchange rate fluctuation protection; strong safeguard for international contracts in Zimbabwe.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor is to obtain the Project Manager's approval before starting the design of each part of the works.
notes: Design approval obligation; standard for ECC Option A with contractor design.

text: 14. PERSONAL INFORMATION PROTECTION
notes: Heading or procedural clause; no material risk allocation.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall be responsible for obtaining all statutory approvals, permits and licences required for the Works at its own cost, and delays arising from the permitting process shall not entitle the Contractor to any extension of time.
notes: Contractor bears permitting risk in a jurisdiction with unpredictable regulatory timelines.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall employ a minimum of 70% local Zimbabwean labour in the execution of the Works.
notes: Local content labour requirement; common condition on government-funded Zimbabwe projects.

text: It is hereby agreed that either party shall give 14 days' written notice of any change in address for service of notices.
notes: Notice address update obligation; administrative.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer shall provide such information as the Contractor may reasonably require to carry out the Works.
notes: Mutual information obligation; protects contractor's right to information.

text: 17.1 This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts and by the Parties hereto on separate counterparts, each of which, when executed and delivered, shall be an original and all the counterparts shall together constitute one and the same instrument.

In accordance with industry practice, dispute shall first be referred to mediation; if not resolved within 28 days either party may refer the dispute to adjudication in accordance with the Scheme for Construction Contracts.
notes: Tiered dispute resolution with adjudication backstop; balanced and HGCRA compliant.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, disputes shall first be referred to a technical expert for determination within 30 days; expert determination shall be binding unless overturned by arbitration.
notes: Expert determination as first-tier; efficient mechanism for technical disputes.

text: It is hereby agreed that extensions of Time shall be granted for Exceptional Events and other matters set out in Sub-Clause 8.5.
notes: Standard FIDIC EOT provision covering listed relief events.

text: The Parties shall make every effort to resolve any dispute or difference of opinion between them concerning their rights and obligations under this Agreement amicably by direct informal negotiation and discussion. If, after 30 days from the commencement of such informal negotiations, the Parties fail to resolve the dispute or difference amicably, either party may refer the dispute for resolution to the Commercial Arbitration Centre, Harare. Such arbitration proceedings shall be governed by provisions of the Arbitration Act. The Parties agree that the venue shall be Harare, Zimbabwe and that all proceedings shall be conducted in the English language. Each Party shall nominate an arbitrator and the two arbitrators shall nominate a third arbitrator who shall act as the Chairperson. Should the two arbitrators disagree on a third nomination, the President (for the time being) of the Commercial Arbitration Centre, Harare shall nominate the Chairperson. The Chairperson shall determine how the costs of arbitration shall be shared between the parties. The decision of the arbitrators shall be final and binding upon the Parties and shall be registered as an award in a court of competent jurisdiction.
CORE CLAUSES
notes: NEC4 structural heading.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall provide training to the Employer's maintenance staff on the operation of installed plant and equipment.
notes: Training obligation; standard on M&E and infrastructure contracts.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall be liable for consequential, indirect and economic loss suffered by the Employer arising out of any breach, delay or defect, without financial cap.
notes: Uncapped consequential loss liability is commercially unacceptable and uninsurable.

text: It is hereby agreed that all payments under this Contract shall be made in United States Dollars; any payment made in RTGS or ZiG currency shall not discharge the Contractor's payment obligation.
notes: USD-only payment in reverse; in context where contractor is employer, this shifts forex risk onto subcontractor.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall be entitled to apply for interim payment on a monthly basis throughout the Contract Period.
notes: Monthly interim payment right; standard cash flow provision.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Architect shall issue Interim Payment Certificates at the intervals stated in the Contract Particulars, and the Employer shall pay within 14 days of the due date.
notes: Clear payment timeline; HGCRA compliant.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer may reduce the scope of Works by up to 75% without giving reasons and without any adjustment to the Contract Price for preliminaries or overheads.
notes: 75% omission right without overhead recovery; contractor's fixed costs entirely at employer's risk.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Employer provides the Site and the means of access to and from the Site to the Contractor on time.
notes: Employer's access obligation; breach is a CE.

text: The Sub-Contractor hereby accepts full and exclusive responsibility for compliance by the Sub-Contractor and his employees, with the provisions of the OHSACT / PHACT.
notes: Clause creates significant risk for subcontractor; one-sided in favour of contractor/employer.

text: 'Compensation Event' means an event which entitles the Contractor to changes to the Prices and Completion Date.
notes: Standard NEC4 compensation event definition.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor's liability shall not be limited under any circumstances; any contractual cap on liability is expressly excluded.
notes: Removal of any liability cap; contractor faces unlimited financial exposure.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall maintain the Accepted Programme; failure to maintain an accepted programme for more than 4 weeks results in a reduction in the PWDD of 15%.
notes: 15% PWDD reduction for programme lapse; punitive financial sanction for administrative failure.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, any extension of time application by the Contractor must be made within 7 days of the occurrence of the relevant event, failing which the Contractor shall be deemed to have waived its entitlement.
notes: Extremely short notice period; strict waiver provision with no discretion for Engineer/Architect.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall not be entitled to a compensation event where the Contractor failed to give early warning of the matter which caused the event.
notes: Loss of CE entitlement for failure to give early warning; strict application has harsh financial effect.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, where the Project Manager gives an instruction changing the Scope after the Contract Date, all additional Defined Cost and time is recoverable as a compensation event.
notes: Full cost and time recovery on scope change CE; standard NEC4 protection.

text: It is hereby agreed that where the Contractor submits a payment application that the Employer considers to be exaggerated, the Employer may reduce all future applications by 20% as a penalty.
notes: Punitive 20% reduction on future applications; disproportionate response to disputed valuations.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, where the exchange rate moves by more than 5% against the Contractor between the Base Date and the payment date, the Contract Price shall be adjusted by the difference.
notes: Exchange rate fluctuation protection; strong safeguard for international contracts in Zimbabwe.

text: 'Key Date' means the date by which the Contractor is to meet a Condition stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Standard NEC4 Key Date definition.

text: No failure, delay, relaxation or indulgence on the part of any party in terms of this agreement shall operate as a waiver of such power or right, nor preclude any other or further exercises thereof, or the exercise of any other power or right under this agreement, or any such securities or documents.
Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall ensure the Works are carried out in a good and workmanlike manner using materials of the quality specified.
notes: Standard workmanship obligation; fundamental but balanced.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Project Manager may instruct the Contractor to stop or not start any work without giving a reason, and no compensation event arises from such instruction.
notes: Removal of compensation event for stop/start instructions; contractor bears cost of downtime.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Employer's Representative may attend any site operations, tests or inspections at any time without prior notice.
notes: Access right for employer's representative; standard.

text: 'Base Date' means the date 7 days before the latest date for submission of the Tender.
notes: Standard FIDIC Base Date definition; triggers change-in-law risk allocation.

text: SECTION 3: SCOPE OF WORKS
notes: Scope section heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, any variation instructed by the Employer shall be valued by the Employer's Quantity Surveyor, whose valuation shall be final and binding on the Contractor.
notes: Removes contractor's right to challenge variation valuations through dispute resolution.

text: 'Defined Cost' means the amounts calculated by applying the Schedule of Cost Components or Shorter Schedule of Cost Components.
notes: Standard NEC4 cost definition.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor is entitled to additional Cost if it encounters physical conditions which could not have been foreseen by an experienced contractor.
notes: Unforeseeable physical conditions relief; standard FIDIC 4.12 protection.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor warrants that it has independently investigated all site conditions, surrounding infrastructure and sub-surface geology, and accepts all risk therefrom without any entitlement to additional payment.

text: 'Practical Completion' means the stage at which the Works are practically complete as certified by the Architect.
notes: Standard definition; pivotal trigger for retention release and LAD cessation.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, good faith negotiation shall be a condition precedent to arbitration; parties shall negotiate for 30 days before initiating formal proceedings.
notes: Negotiation condition precedent; promotes amicable resolution before formal proceedings.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Defects Liability Period for roofing works shall be 5 years from Practical Completion.
notes: Extended DLP for specialist trade; common on roofing and waterproofing.

notes: Contract section heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor's share of savings achieved below the Target Cost shall be calculated at 50% of the underspend.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor is to provide the Project Manager with information which the Project Manager needs in order to issue a certificate.

notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: SECTION 3 – CONTROL OF THE WORKS
notes: Contract section heading.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall pay liquidated and ascertained damages at the rate stated in the Contract Particulars for every week or part week during which Practical Completion is delayed beyond the Completion Date, without limit as to time or amount.
notes: Uncapped LADs with no causal link required; significant financial exposure for contractor.

text: The risk register is issued to the Contractor at the starting date.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Employer may terminate this Contract at any time without cause or notice, and the Contractor shall have no claim for loss of profit, overhead recovery or any other damages arising from such termination.

text: In this Contract, the following terms have the meanings given to them below unless the context otherwise requires.

notes: Governing law clause; standard for UK-based JCT contract.

notes: Standard FIDIC Base Date definition; triggers change-in-law risk allocation.

notes: Contractor bears full prolongation and disruption costs even where caused by employer actions.

text: CLAUSE 1 – GENERAL PROVISIONS

notes: Price control risk on employer; government intervention risk passed back to employer.

notes: Standard DLP definition.

notes: Site tidiness obligation; standard.

notes: Extended DLP for specialist trade; common on roofing and waterproofing.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor accepts no warranty liability for defects arising from the Employer's design or specification.

notes: Payment section heading.

text: The Contractor agrees and binds itself to indemnify XYZ of whatever injuries or damages caused or occasioned or contributed to by the failure, negligence or conduct of the Contractor and/or its employees, sub-contractors, agent and supplier or consultants arising out of or in connection with or on the occasion of the performance of the Contract.

notes: Extended DLP for specialist trade; common on roofing and waterproofing.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall repay all amounts received under interim certificates if the final account shows the total certified was overpaid, with interest at 8% above base rate.

notes: Contract section heading.

notes: Contract heading.

text: 'Works Information' means information which specifies and describes the works or states any constraint on how the Contractor Provides the Works.

notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Project Manager is to act as stated in this Contract and in a spirit of mutual trust and co-operation.
notes: PM duty of mutual trust; mirrors contractor obligation.

text: The Engineer shall issue Payment Certificates within 7 days of receiving the Contractor's Statement.
notes: 28-day certification period; standard FIDIC payment cycle.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall repay all amounts received under interim certificates if the final account shows the total certified was overpaid, with interest at 8% above base rate.
notes: Retroactive overpayment recovery with punitive interest; creates cash flow uncertainty throughout project.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer shall pay the Contractor a mobilisation advance of 15% of the Contract Sum, secured by an advance payment guarantee, within 30 days of contract execution.
notes: Mobilisation advance at 15%; strong cash flow support for contractor on project start.

text: 'Practical Completion' means the stage at which the Works are sufficiently complete to be used for their intended purpose, as certified by the Employer's Representative.
notes: Standard practical completion definition for Zimbabwe contracts.

text: 10.1 The Contractor shall be provided with Zimplats' BMS policy and the SHEQ policies and procedures before commencement of work. Any breach by the Contractor, its employees, agents and/or sub-Contractors, of the Employer's BMS and/or any of the Employer's SHEQ policies and procedures, or any acts or omissions of the Contractor, its employees, agents and/or sub-Contractors, which result in the following occurrences/incidences due to negligence shall attract the following penalties for each occurrence/incidence (expressed as a percentage of the Monthly Certificate Value): (1) Medical Treatment Case: 0.5% of the average monthly certificate value; (2) Lost Time Injury: 3% of the average monthly certificate value; (3) Fatality: 5% of the average monthly certificate value; (4) Subject to a cap of 5% of the total contract value.
notes: Clause creates significant risk for subcontractor; one-sided in favour of contractor/employer.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor and Project Manager attend each early warning meeting.
notes: Risk register and EW meeting attendance; collaborative and standard.

text: The Contractor shall not suspend work for non-payment unless it has given 48 hours' prior written notice and obtained a court order confirming the sum is due.
notes: Effectively removes contractor's statutory suspension right under HGCRA by imposing impractical preconditions.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, provisional sums included in the Contract Bills shall only be expended on the written instruction of the Employer's Representative.
notes: Provisional sum control mechanism; standard QS practice.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall rectify all defects notified during the Defects Liability Period at its own cost, including defects caused by fair wear and tear, acts of God or third parties.
notes: Warranty extends to fair wear and tear and force majeure damage; far beyond standard contractor warranty obligations.

text: 'Defined Cost' means the amounts calculated by applying the Schedule of Cost Components or Shorter Schedule of Cost Components.
notes: Standard NEC4 cost definition.

text: It is hereby agreed that both Parties shall endeavour to avoid disputes by prompt and amicable resolution of issues.
notes: Dispute avoidance obligation; aspirational and balanced.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall take out and maintain Contractors All Risks insurance in the joint names of the Employer and Contractor for the full reinstatement value of the Works.
notes: Standard insurance requirement; risk allocation is balanced.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer's right to terminate shall not arise where the alleged breach is caused by the Employer's own act or omission.
notes: Employer's concurrent breach prevents termination; protects contractor where employer contributes to the breach.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall bear the cost of all power, water, telecommunications and site services throughout the Contract Period; no allowance has been made in the Contract Sum.
notes: Undisclosed service costs to be borne by contractor; creates hidden financial risk.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall be entitled to a pre-agreed sum for demobilisation costs payable on Practical Completion.
notes: Express demobilisation payment; reduces contractor's post-completion financial exposure.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall be entitled to a daywork rate premium of 35% on direct cost for all instructed dayworks, together with a percentage addition for profit and overhead.
notes: Above-market daywork premium; improves contractor's margin on time-and-materials work.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor provides forecasts of the total Defined Cost at intervals stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Cost forecasting obligation; administrative burden but provides transparency.

text: The parties agree that the Client may terminate the Contractor's obligation to Provide the Works at any time.
notes: Termination for convenience right; contractor receives defined cost plus profit entitlement under NEC4.

text: The parties agree that where the Employer fails to provide the Site by the agreed date, the Contractor is entitled to an extension of time and Cost plus profit.
notes: Full recovery (time + cost + profit) for employer's failure to provide site; strong contractor protection.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall submit a detailed construction programme within 14 days of the Date of Possession and shall update it monthly or following any significant change.
notes: Programme obligation is standard; failure to comply may affect EOT entitlement.

text: In accordance with industry practice, any payment certified by the Quantity Surveyor shall be subject to the Employer's approval before becoming due; the Employer may reduce the certified amount without limitation.
notes: Employer veto over QS certification; undermines independence of certification process.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall provide a Performance Security equal to 15% of the Accepted Contract Amount.
notes: 15% performance security is above FIDIC standard 10%; significant liquidity cost.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall pay liquidated and ascertained damages at the rate stated in the Contract Particulars for every week or part week during which Practical Completion is delayed beyond the Completion Date, without limit as to time or amount.
notes: Uncapped LADs with no causal link required; significant financial exposure for contractor.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Employer may suspend the Works for any reason for up to 60 days without this constituting a termination; during suspension the Contractor shall stand down without payment.
notes: Unpaid suspension right; contractor bears standing costs during employer-requested suspension.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall retain title to all temporary works and equipment on site until paid in full; the Employer acknowledges no lien or right of retention over such items.
notes: Romalpa-style retention of title clause; protects contractor's plant and equipment on employer insolvency.

notes: Document heading; modifications to general conditions.

text: SECTION 6 – INJURY, DAMAGE AND INSURANCE
notes: Contract section heading.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the seat of arbitration shall be Harare, Zimbabwe; the arbitrator shall have authority to grant interim relief.

text: It is hereby agreed that where practical completion is delayed, the Employer may call upon the performance bond in full without proving loss.

notes: 8-week notification bar is strict; permanent loss of entitlement is a significant risk.

text: The Contractor shall give notice to the Engineer within 48 hours after the Contractor became aware, or should have become aware, of the event or circumstance giving rise to the claim; failing which the Contractor shall be entitled to no additional payment.

text: The Employer may terminate the Contract for convenience by giving 48 hours' notice; the Contractor shall be entitled only to Cost incurred plus 3% of the Cost as profit.
notes: Termination for convenience with limited profit entitlement; 3% profit capped regardless of anticipated margin.

notes: Standard precedence clause; provides clarity in case of conflict.

text: Where the Contract Sum is denominated in USD, all payments shall be made in USD or at the parallel market rate, whichever is more favourable to the Contractor.
notes: Parallel market rate protection; significant financial benefit for contractor in multi-currency Zimbabwe environment.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor provides services and facilities listed in the Scope at the times and for the periods stated.

text: In accordance with industry practice, if the Contractor desires to make any variation to the design or specification, it shall submit a formal request to the Employer's Agent, who may consent or refuse at their reasonable discretion.

text: The rate of delay damages shall be 10% per day of the Accepted Contract Amount, uncapped.
notes: 0.5% per day uncapped LADs; extremely high rate on large contracts.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Employer may withhold payment of any certified sum provided it issues a Pay Less Notice not later than the prescribed period before the final date for payment.
notes: HGCRA-compliant pay less notice mechanism; contractor has clear right to challenge.

text: The Subcontractor shall, on a monthly basis, submit a monthly progress report to the Contractor. The first report shall cover the period up to the end of the first calendar month following the Commencement Date. Each report shall be submitted within five days after the last day of the period to which it relates, with the final report to be submitted for the month in which the Subcontractor completes the Subcontract Works. Each report shall include: 1) charts and detailed description of actual progress as measured against planned progress; 2) photographs providing details as regards the status of manufacture and the status of progress of the Subcontract Services and/or Works; 3) copies of quality assurance documents, test results, and certificates relating to materials and goods provided by the Subcontractor; 4) details of any claims made in terms of the Agreement; 5) an analysis of circumstances or potential circumstances which may jeopardise the completion of the Subcontract Services and/or Works, and recommendations as regards the management of these particular circumstances; 6) safety statistics, including details of any hazardous incidents and activities relating to environmental aspects and public relations.
It is hereby agreed that the Project Manager may assess a compensation event if the Contractor has not submitted a required quotation within the time allowed.
notes: PM assessment on contractor delay; some risk but provides programme certainty.

text: For purposes of this Agreement, Force Majeure means an event beyond the control of the parties to this Agreement and does not involve either party's fault or negligence and is not foreseeable. If, at any time during the existence of the Agreement, either party is unable to perform in whole or part any obligation under this Agreement because of such events which include, but are not restricted to acts of God, acts of Government in its sovereign capacity, war, revolutions, hostility, civil commotions, strikes, fires, floods, epidemics, quarantine restrictions, freight embargoes, explosions, then the date of fulfilment of this Agreement shall be postponed during the period when such circumstances are operative. Neither party shall be held liable to the other party for non-performance or delay in performance of any of its obligations under the Agreement due to Force Majeure event/s, imposition of liquidated damages or termination if and to the extent that its delay in performance or other failure to perform its obligations under the Agreement is the result of Force Majeure. Upon the occurrence of such an event of force majeure the affected party shall immediately notify the other party with as much detail as possible of the force majeure. For the avoidance of doubt, the affected Party shall notify the other party of the force majeure within a maximum period of forty-eight (48 hours). Immediately after the cause is removed, the affected party shall perform such obligations with reasonable speed. If such inability to perform continues for a period of more than 1 month, each party shall have the right to be released from further performance of the Agreement, in which case, neither party shall have the right to claim damages from the other.
The Contractor shall commence the Works on the Date of Possession and shall proceed regularly and diligently.
