text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Employer may instruct the Contractor to accelerate the programme; the Contractor shall comply and any claim for acceleration costs shall be submitted within 7 days of the instruction.
notes: 7-day acceleration claim window is impractical; employer can demand acceleration without pre-agreeing cost.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor's liability shall not be limited under any circumstances; any contractual cap on liability is expressly excluded.
notes: Removal of any liability cap; contractor faces unlimited financial exposure.

text: It is hereby agreed that on termination by the Contractor for Employer default, the Contractor shall be entitled to recover its costs, loss of profit on the remaining Works, and reasonable demobilisation costs.
notes: Full termination entitlement for contractor on employer default; standard JCT 8.9 protection.

text: The parties agree that where practical completion is delayed, the Employer may call upon the performance bond in full without proving loss.
notes: On-demand bond call without proof of loss; contractor's bond is vulnerable to abuse.

text: SECTION 3 – TIME
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Employer may terminate the Contractor's employment at will and without cause upon giving 7 days' written notice, whereupon the Contractor shall be entitled only to payment for work properly executed.
notes: At-will termination without compensation for loss of anticipated profit or demobilisation costs; highly one-sided.

text: Dispute shall first be referred to mediation; if not resolved within 14 days either party may refer the dispute to adjudication in accordance with the Scheme for Construction Contracts.
notes: Tiered dispute resolution with adjudication backstop; balanced and HGCRA compliant.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall provide collateral warranties to the Employer's funders and tenants in the form annexed to this Contract.
notes: Collateral warranty obligation; standard on commercial developments.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall be entitled to claim direct loss and expense for any act, omission or default of the Employer or Employer's Agent that causes disruption to the Works.
notes: Broad disruption claim entitlement covering employer defaults; protects contractor's commercial position.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor is to give an early warning of any matter which could increase the total Defined Cost, delay Completion or impair performance of the works in use.
notes: EW obligation is core NEC4 requirement; failure to warn may reduce CE entitlement.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor may suspend performance of the whole or any part of the Works where a payment is overdue, by giving 7 days' written notice to the Employer.
notes: HGCRA suspension right expressly preserved; contractor has clear mechanism to address non-payment.

text: 'Key Date' means the date by which the Contractor is to meet a Condition stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Standard NEC4 Key Date definition.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor is to assess the compensation event using the Shorter Schedule of Cost Components where the work has not been done.
notes: SSCC use for prospective CEs; requires forecast which introduces uncertainty.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall submit an Environmental Management Plan in accordance with EMA regulations within 30 days of Contract Date.
notes: EMA compliance requirement; mandatory for infrastructure projects in Zimbabwe.

text: The parties agree that where the Engineer fails to respond within the period for reply, the Contractor may treat the absence of response as approval of the communication.
notes: Deemed approval on engineer inaction; contractor can proceed on basis of non-response.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall maintain and operate a complaints handling procedure for affected community members throughout the Contract Period.
notes: Community engagement obligation; increasingly common on Zimbabwe infrastructure contracts.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer's Representative shall have authority to issue instructions on behalf of the Employer in all matters relating to the administration of this Contract.
notes: Defines employer's representative authority; standard.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall take out and maintain Contractors All Risks insurance in the joint names of the Employer and Contractor for the full reinstatement value of the Works.
notes: Standard insurance requirement; risk allocation is balanced.

text: It is hereby agreed that where changes in Laws after the Base Date increase the Contractor's Costs, the Contract Price shall be adjusted accordingly.
notes: Change-in-law risk on employer; contractor protected from sovereign risk after Base Date.

text: Any claim not submitted within 14 days of the cause of action arising is time-barred and shall not be considered.
notes: 14-day claim bar is impractically short; causes loss of legitimate claims on complex projects.

text: All documents produced by the Sub-contractor in connection with the Project which are made available to persons other than the Contractor shall bear the name of the Contractor.

For the avoidance of doubt, on termination of the Contract by the Employer for convenience, the Contractor shall be entitled to the full value of work executed, loss of profit on remaining work, and demobilisation costs.
notes: Full termination-for-convenience entitlement; contractor recovers profit on unexecuted work.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Architect shall issue Interim Payment Certificates at the intervals stated in the Contract Particulars, and the Employer shall pay within 14 days of the due date.
notes: Clear payment timeline; HGCRA compliant.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor Provides the Works in accordance with the Scope and this Contract.
notes: Fundamental contractor obligation; standard.

text: SECTION 10: TERMINATION
notes: Termination section heading.

text: The Employer shall provide the Contractor with a Letter of Credit from an approved bank as security for payment of amounts exceeding USD 75,000.
notes: Payment security mechanism; protects contractor against employer insolvency on large contracts.

text: In accordance with industry practice, retention shall be held by the Employer in its general account and shall not attract interest; no obligation to place retention in a separate trust account arises.
notes: Retention is not ring-fenced; contractor has no protection if employer becomes insolvent.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall correct all Defects within 7 days of notification regardless of their nature or complexity, failing which the Employer may employ others and recover the cost plus 50%.
notes: Impossibly short defect correction window with punitive 50% premium recovery.

text: ANNEXURE B: CONSTRUCTION PROGRAMME
notes: Programme annexure heading.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Defects Liability Period for roofing works shall be 5 years from Practical Completion.
notes: Extended DLP for specialist trade; common on roofing and waterproofing.

text: The Contractor shall rectify any defect notified during the Defects Liability Period within 3 days of notification, failing which the Employer may engage others and recover costs at a premium rate of 150% of the cost incurred.
notes: Impossibly short rectification window; premium rate recovery is punitive and unjustifiable.

text: The Employer's Representative may attend any site operations, tests or inspections at any time without prior notice.
notes: Access right for employer's representative; standard.

text: The Contractor may terminate if the Employer fails to pay a certified sum within 14 days of the final date for payment after 14 days' notice.
notes: Contractor termination right for employer non-payment; standard protection.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall execute the Works in accordance with the Contract and the Engineer's instructions.
notes: Fundamental contractor obligation; standard.

text: Where the Employer fails to take possession of the completed Works within 14 days of Practical Completion, the Contractor shall be entitled to storage charges at a rate of 12.5% of the Contract Sum per week.
notes: Storage charges on employer's failure to take possession; monetises employer-caused delay post-completion.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall complete the Works and all snagging within the Defects Liability Period without any entitlement to prolongation costs or disruption claims.
notes: Contractor bears full prolongation and disruption costs even where caused by employer actions.

text: The Engineer's determination of any claim shall be final and binding unless challenged by a DAAB decision within 42 days.
notes: Short DAAB challenge window; practical difficulty in convening DAAB within 28 days in Zimbabwe.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Employer's obligation to provide information on time is a condition precedent to the Contractor's obligation to maintain the programme; late information shall entitle the Contractor to an extension of time.
notes: Information provision as condition precedent; strong protection where employer delays drawings or instructions.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, where the Employer fails to give possession of the Site on the Date of Possession, such failure shall be a Relevant Matter entitling the Contractor to loss and expense.
notes: Contractor protected for employer's failure to give possession.

text: 'Contract Price' means the sum stated in the Letter of Acceptance, subject to adjustment in accordance with this Contract.
notes: Standard contract price definition.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall submit a final account within 90 days of Practical Completion.
notes: Final account submission obligation; standard.

text: SECTION 2: DEFINITIONS
notes: Definitions section heading.

text: The parties agree that payment applications shall be submitted by the Contractor on the 25th of each month; the Employer's Quantity Surveyor shall certify within 14 days and payment shall be made within 30 days of certification.
notes: Payment cycle of approximately 44 days; acceptable but on the longer side for Zimbabwe market.

text: SECTION 7 – ASSIGNMENT, THIRD PARTY RIGHTS AND COLLATERAL WARRANTIES
notes: Contract section heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, government-imposed price controls that prevent the Contractor from procuring materials at market prices shall entitle the Contractor to an adjustment of the Contract Price.
notes: Price control risk on employer; government intervention risk passed back to employer.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, arbitration shall be conducted under the Zimbabwe Arbitration Act [Chapter 7:15] and the rules of the Arbitration Foundation of Southern Africa (AFSA).
notes: Zimbabwe arbitration framework; appropriate institutional rules for regional commercial disputes.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall be entitled to an extension of time for completion of the Works where completion is or is likely to be delayed by a Relevant Event.
notes: Standard EOT entitlement clause; contractor protected for specified relief events.

text: 'Defects Notification Period' means the period for notifying defects in the Works or a Section as stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Standard FIDIC DNP definition.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Project Manager may change the Scope to accept a Defect.
notes: Scope change to accept defect with compensation adjustment; balanced.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, payment applications must be submitted in the prescribed format using the Employer's software system; applications in any other format shall not constitute valid applications.
notes: Format condition precedent on payment applications; employer can reject valid applications on technical grounds.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, certificates issued under this Contract shall not be taken as evidence that the Works have been completed in accordance with the Contract.
notes: Standard certification disclaimer.

text: SECTION 7 – TITLE
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: 'Architect' means the person named as such in the Contract Particulars or any replacement appointed by the Employer.
notes: Standard definition; identifies key contract administrator.

text: d) Validity of Contract
notes: Heading or procedural clause; no material risk allocation.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, certificates issued under this Contract shall not be taken as evidence that the Works have been completed in accordance with the Contract.
notes: Standard certification disclaimer.

text: 7.3 The Contractor shall deploy to the Employer only properly qualified, trained and skilled employees to undertake any obligations in terms of this Agreement. By deploying any such employees, the Contractor represents and warrants that its personnel are so skilled and qualified.
The Employer pays the amount due within 3 weeks of each assessment date.
notes: Standard NEC4 payment period; clear employer obligation.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor obtains required approvals from Others and notifies the Project Manager before starting work which needs such approval.
notes: Third party approval obligation; standard.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the losing party shall bear all costs of arbitration including the other party's legal fees assessed on an indemnity basis.
notes: Indemnity costs order by arbitrator; increases stakes of losing a dispute.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor is to prepare the first programme to show the information stated in this Contract within the period stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Programme submission obligation; standard NEC4 requirement.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall indemnify and hold harmless the Employer against all claims, losses and liabilities arising out of or in connection with the Works, whether or not caused by the Contractor's negligence.
notes: Indemnity without negligence requirement effectively makes contractor an insurer for all site events.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall not assign this Contract or any part of it without the prior written consent of the Employer, which shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed.
notes: Anti-assignment clause with reasonableness qualifier; balanced provision.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor provides services and facilities listed in the Scope at the times and for the periods stated.
notes: Service provision obligation; scope-dependent risk.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall submit a final account within 90 days of Practical Completion.
notes: Final account submission obligation; standard.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, certificates issued under this Contract shall not be taken as evidence that the Works have been completed in accordance with the Contract.
notes: Standard certification disclaimer.

text: SECTION 1 – GENERAL
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall maintain public roads used by construction traffic in a clean and tidy condition at all times.
notes: Road maintenance obligation; standard and reasonable.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, on termination by the Contractor for Employer default, the Contractor shall be entitled to recover its costs, loss of profit on the remaining Works, and reasonable demobilisation costs.
notes: Full termination entitlement for contractor on employer default; standard JCT 8.9 protection.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, where the Employer fails to issue a payment notice or pay less notice by the prescribed date, the sum stated in the Contractor's payment application shall become the sum due.
notes: Default payment mechanism under HGCRA; contractor's application becomes notified sum if employer fails to serve notice.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall erect and maintain a Project Sign Board in accordance with the Employer's specifications within 7 days of Commencement.
notes: Signage obligation; administrative, low risk.

text: The parties agree that either Party may terminate the Contractor's obligation to Provide the Works if the other Party has committed a reason stated in this Contract.
notes: Standard mutual termination for cause; balanced.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall erect and maintain a Project Sign Board in accordance with the Employer's specifications within 7 days of Commencement.
notes: Signage obligation; administrative, low risk.

text: The Contractor shall indemnify the Employer against all costs, claims and expenses arising from the Contractor's presence on site, including claims by third parties, whether or not caused by the Contractor's negligence.
notes: Negligence-free indemnity; contractor becomes insurer for all site-related third party claims.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall maintain contingency resources and plans to mitigate force majeure events to the extent reasonably practicable.
notes: FM mitigation obligation; reasonable and standard.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Architect's decision on any matter concerning the quality or quantity of the Works shall be final and binding and shall not be subject to review in any dispute resolution proceedings.

text: SECTION 1: PARTIES TO THE CONTRACT
notes: Contract parties section.

text: In accordance with industry practice, if the Contractor fails to comply with a Time for Completion, the Employer may after notice reduce the Performance Security proportionally.
notes: Bond reduction on delay; some risk but subject to notice and proportionality.

text: CLAUSE 1 – GENERAL PROVISIONS
notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: The parties agree that escalation shall not apply to any element of the Contract Sum; the Contractor accepts all risks of price escalation in materials, labour and equipment for the full duration of the Contract.
notes: Full escalation risk on contractor in Zimbabwe's volatile inflation environment; commercially dangerous on long contracts.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Project Manager may instruct a change to the Scope and the change is a compensation event.
notes: Standard CE for scope change; balanced provision.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall not be entitled to any uplift on the rates and prices in the Contract Bills for any variation, regardless of the nature, complexity or volume of the varied work.
notes: Fixed-rate variations with no uplift for changed conditions; erodes contractor margin on complex variations.

text: It is hereby agreed that a compensation event is not notified if the Employer or the Contractor did not give an early warning of the event when they could have done so.
notes: Mutual early warning obligation; applies to both parties but in practice limits contractor CEs.

text: In accordance with industry practice, interest shall accrue on overdue payments at a rate of 5% above Bank of England Base Rate from the final date for payment.
notes: Standard interest provision; rate is adequate but not punitive.

text: NOW, THEREFORE, it is agreed as follows:
Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall provide and maintain adequate lighting on the Site during working hours.
notes: Standard site obligation; low risk.

text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF the parties have signed this Contract on the date first written above.
notes: Execution clause.

text: The Employer shall provide the Contractor with a Letter of Credit from an approved bank as security for payment of amounts exceeding USD 75,000.
notes: Payment security mechanism; protects contractor against employer insolvency on large contracts.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, any amounts due to the Contractor may be set off against any claim the Employer may have against the Contractor under any other contract between the parties.
notes: Cross-contract set-off right; allows employer to use disputes on other contracts to withhold current payments.

notes: Document heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall provide training to the Employer's maintenance staff on the operation of installed plant and equipment.
notes: Training obligation; standard on M&E and infrastructure contracts.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, cOVID-19 and any variant thereof shall not constitute a force majeure event for the purposes of this Contract.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor warrants that it has independently verified all information provided by the Employer and accepts full responsibility for any errors or discrepancies in the Contract Documents.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor is entitled to Exceptional Event relief for epidemics, natural catastrophes, wars, embargoes and acts of government.

text: GENERAL CONDITIONS OF CONTRACT

text: SECTION 9: DISPUTE RESOLUTION
notes: Dispute resolution section heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor accepts all risk for errors in the Scope or Works Information provided by the Employer and shall not be entitled to any adjustment of the Prices or Completion Date.
notes: Employer's scope errors become contractor's risk; contrary to CE 60.1(1).

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall not be entitled to additional cost arising from a force majeure event; its sole entitlement is an extension of time.
notes: Time-only relief for force majeure; contractor bears standing cost during FM period.

text: The Employer may terminate the Contractor's employment for any reason and the Contractor shall be entitled only to an amount equal to the Defined Cost incurred to date less a 7.5% reduction.
notes: Termination for convenience with punitive 10% reduction; contractor has no entitlement to profit or pipeline contribution.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, any defect that recurs within 12 months of rectification shall be treated as a new defect with a fresh 12-month rectification warranty.
notes: Rolling warranty on recurring defects; creates perpetual exposure for systemic defects.

text: The parties agree that the Engineer may issue a Taking-Over Certificate when the Works are substantially complete.
notes: Taking-over certification; milestone trigger for risk transfer.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall provide a Performance Security equal to 15% of the Accepted Contract Amount.

text: Any defect that recurs within 12 months of rectification shall be treated as a new defect with a fresh 12-month rectification warranty.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall be entitled to an extension of time and additional cost for delays caused by unavailability of foreign currency for imported materials, provided such unavailability is documented by a bank.

text: The Contractor shall give notice to the Engineer within 48 hours after the Contractor became aware, or should have become aware, of the event or circumstance giving rise to the claim; failing which the Contractor shall be entitled to no additional payment.

CORE CLAUSES
notes: NEC4 structural heading.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor maintains the Site as free from rubbish and the Contractor's equipment as the Project Manager instructs.
notes: Site tidiness obligation; standard.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Employer's obligation to provide information on time is a condition precedent to the Contractor's obligation to maintain the programme; late information shall entitle the Contractor to an extension of time.
notes: Information provision as condition precedent; strong protection where employer delays drawings or instructions.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall indemnify the Employer against all third party claims arising from the Contractor's operations, including claims resulting from the Employer's acts or omissions.
notes: Indemnity extends to employer's own acts; removes employer's responsibility for its own negligence.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, all risk of ground conditions at the Site remains with the Contractor regardless of the Site Information provided; no compensation event arises from physical conditions.
notes: Full ground risk on contractor even where site information proves inaccurate; contrary to Z60.1 CE.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall employ a minimum of 70% local Zimbabwean labour in the execution of the Works.
notes: Local content labour requirement; common condition on government-funded Zimbabwe projects.

text: The Contractor shall provide and maintain adequate lighting on the Site during working hours.
notes: Standard site obligation; low risk.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor accepts no warranty liability for defects arising from the Employer's design or specification.
notes: Design warranty carve-out for employer-provided design; fair on contractor.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, variations shall be valued using the rates and prices in the Contract Bills where applicable, and where not applicable, at fair rates and prices to be agreed.
notes: Bilateral agreement mechanism for variation valuation; balanced but subject to dispute.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor and Project Manager attend each early warning meeting.
notes: Risk register and EW meeting attendance; collaborative and standard.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall rectify any defect notified during the Defects Liability Period within 3 days of notification, failing which the Employer may engage others and recover costs at a premium rate of 150% of the cost incurred.
notes: Impossibly short rectification window; premium rate recovery is punitive and unjustifiable.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall provide a performance guarantee of 10% of the Contract Sum from an acceptable financial institution within 14 days of Contract execution.
notes: 10% performance guarantee; market norm in Zimbabwe but creates liquidity demand.

text: It is hereby agreed that where the Contract Documents are inconsistent, the order of precedence shall be: the Agreement, then the Conditions, then the Employer's Requirements, then the Contractor's Proposals.
notes: Standard precedence clause; provides clarity in case of conflict.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall provide copies of all required insurances to the Employer within 7 days of request.
notes: Standard insurance evidence obligation; reasonable timescale.

text: The Contractor shall provide a retention bond and a performance bond each equal to 15% of the Contract Price, totalling 20% of the Contract Price, within 7 days of award.
notes: Excessive bonding requirement of 20% combined; significant liquidity burden on contractor.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Project Manager may instruct the Contractor to use a different method of working without it being a compensation event if the change is necessary for safety.
notes: Safety-based method change without CE; employer defines 'necessary' unilaterally.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, all disputes shall be finally resolved by a panel of three arbitrators appointed by the ICC; the Contractor waives the right to challenge any ICC appointment.
notes: ICC arbitration with waiver of appointment challenge; reduces contractor's procedural rights.

text: It is hereby agreed that where the Employer fails to certify payment within the prescribed period, the Contractor shall be entitled to suspend the Works after giving 7 days' written notice.
notes: Suspension right on late certification; strengthens contractor's cash flow protection in Zimbabwe's difficult payment environment.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, all risk of ground conditions at the Site remains with the Contractor regardless of the Site Information provided; no compensation event arises from physical conditions.
notes: Full ground risk on contractor even where site information proves inaccurate; contrary to Z60.1 CE.

text: The Contractor shall not suspend work for non-payment unless it has given 42 days' prior written notice and obtained a court order confirming the sum is due.
notes: Effectively removes contractor's statutory suspension right under HGCRA by imposing impractical preconditions.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, if a force majeure event continues for more than 90 days, either party may terminate the Contract by giving 14 days' notice; the Contractor shall be paid for work completed.
notes: Bilateral termination right on prolonged force majeure; contractor recovers value of work done.

text: 'Accepted Contract Amount' means the amount accepted in the Letter of Acceptance for the execution and completion of the Works and the remedying of any defects.
notes: Standard FIDIC definition.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor warrants that all rates in the Schedule of Rates are adequate and shall not be entitled to any adjustment for unforeseeable increases in cost.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall ensure that all waste generated during the Works is disposed of in accordance with EMA regulations.
notes: Waste disposal obligation; EMA compliance standard.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the final account shall be settled within 180 days of the issue of the Defects Certificate; any disputed items shall be referred to adjudication.
notes: 180-day final account settlement with adjudication backstop; reasonable overall.

text: 'Defects Liability Period' means the period of 12 months following Practical Completion during which the Contractor is liable to rectify defects.
notes: Standard DLP definition.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall not be entitled to any additional payment for compliance with any new law, regulation or standard enacted after the Contract Date.
notes: Full post-contract regulatory risk on contractor; particularly dangerous in Zimbabwe's evolving regulatory environment.

text: 'Defined Cost' means the amounts calculated by applying the Schedule of Cost Components or Shorter Schedule of Cost Components.
notes: Standard NEC4 cost definition.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer shall have the right to vary the scope of Works by up to 50% without any adjustment to the unit rates or time for completion.
notes: 50% scope variation without rate adjustment; contractor's preliminaries and profit margin severely eroded on major omissions.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Engineer shall respond to the Contractor's claim within 42 days of receiving the fully detailed claim; failure to respond shall be deemed a rejection.
notes: FIDIC 2017 Clause 20.2.5 response period; deemed rejection protects contractor's programme certainty.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, where the Employer terminates for convenience, the Contractor is entitled to Defined Cost of work done, profit on work done, and a fee for breaking subcontracts.
notes: Termination for convenience with full cost recovery including profit; standard NEC4 R21 entitlement.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer shall pay the Contract Price in the currency or currencies stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Currency of payment clause; basic obligation.

text: SPECIAL CONDITIONS OF CONTRACT
notes: Document heading; modifications to general conditions.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Engineer shall act neutrally between the parties in all matters under this Contract.
notes: FIDIC 2017 engineer neutrality obligation; fundamental balanced provision.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contract shall be governed by the law of Zimbabwe and any disputes shall be subject to the jurisdiction of Zimbabwean courts.
notes: Governing law and jurisdiction; standard for Zimbabwe contracts.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall be entitled to claim direct loss and expense for any act, omission or default of the Employer or Employer's Agent that causes disruption to the Works.
notes: Broad disruption claim entitlement covering employer defaults; protects contractor's commercial position.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall keep a daily site diary recording weather conditions, labour strength, materials received and work carried out.
notes: Site record-keeping obligation; standard practice.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor is entitled to additional Cost if it encounters physical conditions which could not have been foreseen by an experienced contractor.
notes: Unforeseeable physical conditions relief; standard FIDIC 4.12 protection.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall provide and maintain adequate lighting on the Site during working hours.
notes: Standard site obligation; low risk.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall comply with all applicable laws and regulations in carrying out the Works.
notes: General compliance obligation; standard and uncontroversial.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall correct all Defects within 7 days of notification regardless of their nature or complexity, failing which the Employer may employ others and recover the cost plus 50%.
notes: Impossibly short defect correction window with punitive 50% premium recovery.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor accepts the risk of any event which the Contractor could have foreseen at the Contract Date and which affects the Contractor's ability to Provide the Works.
notes: Ouster of compensation events for foreseeable risk; broad contractor risk assumption.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, if the Contractor becomes insolvent, the Employer shall have the right to complete the Works using the Contractor's workforce and equipment without further payment.
notes: Employer's step-in right using contractor's resources without additional payment; commercially punitive.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, a Defect is a part of the works which is not in accordance with the Scope.
notes: Standard definition of defect.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall maintain and operate a complaints handling procedure for affected community members throughout the Contract Period.
notes: Community engagement obligation; increasingly common on Zimbabwe infrastructure contracts.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer may withhold any amount due to the Contractor pending settlement of any claim by the Employer, whether or not such claim is disputed or related to the current Works.
notes: Unrestricted withholding right; allows employer to use unrelated claims to delay payment indefinitely.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall be entitled to an extension of time and additional cost for delays caused by unavailability of foreign currency for imported materials, provided such unavailability is documented by a bank.
notes: Forex-induced delay recognised as relief event; critical protection in Zimbabwe's USD-constrained environment.

text: SECTION 6: VARIATIONS
notes: Variations section heading.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, provisional sums included in the Contract Bills shall only be expended on the written instruction of the Employer's Representative.
notes: Provisional sum control mechanism; standard QS practice.

text: It is hereby agreed that force majeure shall not excuse the Contractor from any obligation under this Contract, including payment of liquidated damages during periods of force majeure.
notes: Force majeure excluded as a relief mechanism; contractor remains liable for LADs during force majeure events.

text: CLAUSE 8 – COMMENCEMENT, DELAYS AND SUSPENSION
notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: The parties shall attempt to resolve any dispute by negotiation within 7 days of written notice of the dispute.

text: In accordance with industry practice, where the Project Manager gives an instruction changing the Scope after the Contract Date, all additional Defined Cost and time is recoverable as a compensation event.
notes: Full cost and time recovery on scope change CE; standard NEC4 protection.

notes: Standard practical completion definition for Zimbabwe contracts.

notes: Cross-reference definition; no risk allocation.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall repay all amounts received under interim certificates if the final account shows the total certified was overpaid, with interest at 8% above base rate.

notes: 14-day claim bar is impractically short; causes loss of legitimate claims on complex projects.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer's Representative may attend any site operations, tests or inspections at any time without prior notice.
notes: Access right for employer's representative; standard.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Project Manager may assess a compensation event if the Contractor has not submitted a required quotation within the time allowed.
notes: PM assessment on contractor delay; some risk but provides programme certainty.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, where a Relevant Event also causes the Contractor to incur additional costs, the Contractor shall be entitled to both extension of time and reimbursement of loss and expense.
notes: Dual entitlement for time and money on relevant matters; balanced and contractor-friendly.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, cOVID-19 and any variant thereof shall not constitute a force majeure event for the purposes of this Contract.
notes: Pandemic exclusion from force majeure; leaves contractor exposed to pandemic disruption costs.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Project Manager may assess a compensation event at any time and the Contractor shall have no right to submit its own quotation.
notes: Removal of contractor's right to quote for CEs; PM-only assessment is contrary to NEC4 spirit.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall maintain a site diary recording daily weather conditions, labour deployed and progress of the Works.
notes: Record-keeping obligation; protects both parties in claims situations.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, where the Employer fails to issue a payment notice or pay less notice by the prescribed date, the sum stated in the Contractor's payment application shall become the sum due.
notes: Default payment mechanism under HGCRA; contractor's application becomes notified sum if employer fails to serve notice.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall maintain contingency resources and plans to mitigate force majeure events to the extent reasonably practicable.
notes: FM mitigation obligation; reasonable and standard.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer shall nominate and pay directly any nominated subcontractors; the Contractor shall be responsible for coordinating nominated subcontractors but not for their defaults.
notes: Nominated subcontractor mechanism; limits contractor's liability for employer-selected specialists.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Project Manager may instruct the Contractor to use a different method of working without it being a compensation event if the change is necessary for safety.
notes: Safety-based method change without CE; employer defines 'necessary' unilaterally.

text: 'Time for Completion' means the time for completing the Works or a Section as stated in the Contract Data, adjusted under Sub-Clause 8.5.
notes: Standard FIDIC completion time definition.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall permit the Auditor General or any authorised representative to inspect all financial records relating to this Contract.
notes: Government audit access right; standard on public sector Zimbabwe contracts.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer may reduce the scope of Works by up to 75% without giving reasons and without any adjustment to the Contract Price for preliminaries or overheads.
notes: 75% omission right without overhead recovery; contractor's fixed costs entirely at employer's risk.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor provides a 10-year structural warranty for all reinforced concrete works in accordance with the relevant British Standards.
notes: Long-form structural warranty; common on residential and commercial developments.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor accepts the risk of legal changes to the law of any country applicable to the Contractor's work and no compensation event arises from change in law.
notes: Change-in-law risk transferred to contractor; contrary to standard NEC4 CE 60.1(18).

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, price escalation shall be calculated monthly using the CPIH index published by ZIMSTAT; all increases shall be paid in the same currency as the Contract Sum.
notes: Escalation clause with ZIMSTAT index; full price protection for contractor in inflationary environment.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Engineer may issue a Taking-Over Certificate when the Works are substantially complete.
notes: Taking-over certification; milestone trigger for risk transfer.

notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor is to prepare the first programme to show the information stated in this Contract within the period stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Programme submission obligation; standard NEC4 requirement.

text: 'Contract Price' means the sum stated in the Letter of Acceptance, subject to adjustment in accordance with this Contract.
notes: Standard contract price definition.

text: 'Accepted Contract Amount' means the amount accepted in the Letter of Acceptance for the execution and completion of the Works and the remedying of any defects.
notes: Standard FIDIC definition.

text: In accordance with industry practice, where the Employer fails to issue a payment notice or pay less notice by the prescribed date, the sum stated in the Contractor's payment application shall become the sum due.
notes: Default payment mechanism under HGCRA; contractor's application becomes notified sum if employer fails to serve notice.

text: SECTION 8 – TERMINATION
notes: Contract section heading.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Employer may terminate the Contractor's employment at will and without cause upon giving 7 days' written notice, whereupon the Contractor shall be entitled only to payment for work properly executed.
notes: At-will termination without compensation for loss of anticipated profit or demobilisation costs; highly one-sided.

text: The Employer shall make an Advance Payment of 20% of the Accepted Contract Amount within 21 days of receipt of the Performance Security.
notes: FIDIC advance payment at 10%; standard and contractor-supportive.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor warrants that it has independently investigated all site conditions, surrounding infrastructure and sub-surface geology, and accepts all risk therefrom without any entitlement to additional payment.
notes: Full unforeseeable ground risk on contractor; removes employer's implied obligation to disclose site information.

text: The parties agree that no interim payment shall be due to the Contractor until the Employer has received and verified the Contractor's application; the Employer shall have 60 days to make such verification.
notes: Verification period far exceeds HGCRA requirements; effectively extends payment cycle to 75+ days.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Project Manager gives access to the Site to the Contractor on the access date stated in Contract Data Part 1.
notes: Standard access provision; employer obligation.

text: 'The Fee' means the tendered fee percentage applied to Defined Cost.
notes: Standard NEC4 fee definition.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor provides forecasts of the total Defined Cost at intervals stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Cost forecasting obligation; administrative burden but provides transparency.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor Provides the Works in accordance with the Scope and this Contract.
notes: Fundamental contractor obligation; standard.

The parties agree that the Contractor shall not carry out any blasting operations without prior written approval from SADC Explosives Board and notification to the Employer's Representative.

text: SECTION 2: DEFINITIONS
notes: Definitions section heading.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer shall certify and pay the sum of all undisputed items in the Contractor's application pending resolution of disputed items.
notes: Undisputed sum payment obligation; protects contractor's cash flow during valuation disputes.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, no payment shall be made for materials off-site unless the Contractor provides proof of insurance, vesting certificate and list of materials.
notes: Off-site materials payment conditions; reasonable certification requirements.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the parties confirm that this Contract constitutes the entire agreement between them in respect of the Works and supersedes all prior negotiations.
notes: Entire agreement clause; standard contract formation provision.

text: In accordance with industry practice, disputes shall first be referred to a technical expert for determination within 30 days; expert determination shall be binding unless overturned by arbitration.
notes: Expert determination as first-tier; efficient mechanism for technical disputes.

text: SECTION 7 – TITLE
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor may refer a dispute to the DAAB at any time; the DAAB shall give its decision within 84 days of referral.
notes: DAAB mechanism; structured independent dispute resolution.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall provide and maintain adequate lighting on the Site during working hours.
notes: Standard site obligation; low risk.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall be entitled to an extension of time for completion of the Works where completion is or is likely to be delayed by a Relevant Event.
notes: Standard EOT entitlement clause; contractor protected for specified relief events.

text: SECTION 5: PROGRAMME AND COMPLETION
notes: Programme section heading.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall be entitled to a pre-agreed sum for demobilisation costs payable on Practical Completion.
notes: Express demobilisation payment; reduces contractor's post-completion financial exposure.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer and the Contractor shall act in a spirit of mutual trust and co-operation.
notes: Core NEC4 collaboration obligation; aspirational but fundamental.

text: The Contractor shall maintain a 15% retention throughout the Works, reducing to 5% on Practical Completion; the balance shall be released at the end of the 12-month Defects Liability Period.
notes: Standard retention regime for Zimbabwe; rates are at market norm.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall bear all costs of independent testing regardless of the test results; no cost recovery applies where tests confirm compliance.
notes: All testing costs on contractor; creates financial disincentive to test and verify compliance.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, either party may give notice of adjudication at any time; the adjudicator shall be appointed by the RICS within 5 days of the notice.
notes: Standard RICS adjudicator nomination; balanced and practical.

text: The parties agree that all risk of ground conditions at the Site remains with the Contractor regardless of the Site Information provided; no compensation event arises from physical conditions.
notes: Full ground risk on contractor even where site information proves inaccurate; contrary to Z60.1 CE.

text: SECTION 7: INSURANCE
notes: Insurance section heading.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall indemnify the Employer against all third party claims arising from the Contractor's operations, including claims resulting from the Employer's acts or omissions.
notes: Indemnity extends to employer's own acts; removes employer's responsibility for its own negligence.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Architect shall issue Interim Payment Certificates at the intervals stated in the Contract Particulars, and the Employer shall pay within 14 days of the due date.
notes: Clear payment timeline; HGCRA compliant.

text: SECTION 7 – TITLE
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Project Manager certifies a payment in the amount due within one week of each assessment date.
notes: Payment certification timeline; standard NEC4.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall be entitled to payment of interest at a rate of 5% above the RBZ overnight lending rate on all overdue amounts calculated from the due date.
notes: Interest on late payment tied to RBZ rate; commercially reasonable and common on Zimbabwe contracts.

text: SECTION 2 – THE CONTRACTOR'S MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: ANNEXURE C: PERFORMANCE BOND FORM
notes: Performance bond form heading.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor maintains the Site as free from rubbish and the Contractor's equipment as the Project Manager instructs.
notes: Site tidiness obligation; standard.

text: 'Base Date' means the date 42 days before the latest date for submission of the Tender.
notes: Standard FIDIC Base Date definition; triggers change-in-law risk allocation.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall be liable for liquidated damages at the rate of USD 5,000 per day for every day of delay beyond the Completion Date, without any limit on the aggregate amount recoverable.
notes: Uncapped daily LADs in USD; creates severe and uninsurable financial exposure for Zimbabwean contractors.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor repays to the Employer the amount of any overpayment on the next payment date.
notes: Overpayment recovery; balanced and expected.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, all disputes shall be resolved by arbitration in terms of the Arbitration Act [Chapter 7:15] of Zimbabwe; the arbitrator shall be appointed solely by the Employer.
notes: Employer-appointed arbitrator lacks independence; contrary to fair arbitration principles.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall take out and maintain Contractors All Risks insurance in the joint names of the Employer and Contractor for the full reinstatement value of the Works.
notes: Standard insurance requirement; risk allocation is balanced.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall be entitled to an extension of time and additional cost for delays caused by unavailability of foreign currency for imported materials, provided such unavailability is documented by a bank.
notes: Forex-induced delay recognised as relief event; critical protection in Zimbabwe's USD-constrained environment.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall be entitled to a daywork rate premium of 35% on direct cost for all instructed dayworks, together with a percentage addition for profit and overhead.
notes: Above-market daywork premium; improves contractor's margin on time-and-materials work.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall provide collateral warranties to the Employer's funders and tenants in the form annexed to this Contract.
notes: Collateral warranty obligation; standard on commercial developments.

text: The Contractor shall provide a performance bond in the sum of 20% of the Contract Sum within 14 days of execution of the Contract.
notes: Performance bond at 10% is at the higher end; bond cost is a contractor risk.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall pay liquidated and ascertained damages at the rate stated in the Contract Particulars for every week or part week during which Practical Completion is delayed beyond the Completion Date, without limit as to time or amount.
notes: Uncapped LADs with no causal link required; significant financial exposure for contractor.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall attend progress meetings at such intervals as the Employer's Agent may reasonably require.
notes: Standard meeting obligation; reasonable frequency qualifier protects contractor.

text: The Contractor shall not suspend work for non-payment unless it has given 21 days' prior written notice and obtained a court order confirming the sum is due.
notes: Effectively removes contractor's statutory suspension right under HGCRA by imposing impractical preconditions.

text: The Contractor is entitled to a compensation event where the Employer or Others do not work within the times shown in the Accepted Programme.
notes: CE for Others' delay; contractor protected where employer-managed parties cause delay.

text: It is hereby agreed that all variations must be supported by a Variation Order signed by the Employer's Representative before work commences, except in cases of urgency where verbal instruction may be confirmed in writing within 48 hours.
notes: VO requirement with urgency exception; balanced but requires contractor diligence.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Project Manager may assess a compensation event at any time and the Contractor shall have no right to submit its own quotation.
notes: Removal of contractor's right to quote for CEs; PM-only assessment is contrary to NEC4 spirit.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, where the Employer fails to issue a payment notice or pay less notice by the prescribed date, the sum stated in the Contractor's payment application shall become the sum due.
notes: Default payment mechanism under HGCRA; contractor's application becomes notified sum if employer fails to serve notice.

text: SECTION 1: PARTIES TO THE CONTRACT
notes: Contract parties section.

text: It is hereby agreed that payment of the final account shall only become due when the Employer has received all as-built drawings, O&M manuals, warranties and training certificates in a form satisfactory to the Employer.
notes: Final payment held hostage to administrative deliverables, giving employer disproportionate leverage after practical completion.

text: It is hereby agreed that either party shall give 14 days' written notice of any change in address for service of notices.

notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: The Contractor shall, in accordance with the provisions of the Principal Contract, for the benefit of himself and the Sub-Contractor at all material times insure the Works including the Sub-Contract Works and materials and property on site for use in the Works but excluding the Sub-Contractor's plant, tools, equipment and other property or procure the insurance of the Works as aforesaid by the Employer and keep it or have it kept so insured.

The Sub-Contractor is referred to Sections of the OHSACT which outlines the Sub-Contractor's particular duties as an employer, and the General Safety Regulations which relate to the Sub-Contractor's particular field(s) of expertise, and the Construction Regulations.

Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall repay all amounts received under interim certificates if the final account shows the total certified was overpaid, with interest at 8% above base rate.

text: All costs incurred by the Contractor, in so doing, shall be claimable as against the Sub-Contractor, as damages, irrespective of whether these costs and damages flowed directly from the breach event, or where consequential to the breach event.

text: 7. SECRECY AND CONFIDENTIALITY
notes: Heading or procedural clause; no material risk allocation.

text: It is hereby agreed that where a nominated subcontractor fails to perform, the Employer shall bear the resulting delay costs and shall grant an extension of time to the Contractor.
notes: Employer's risk for nominated subcontractor failure; contractor protected from third-party defaults imposed by employer.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, sub-contracting of portions of the Works exceeding 25% of the Contract Sum requires prior written consent from the Employer's Agent.
notes: Sub-contracting threshold consent requirement is commercially acceptable.

text: Force majeure events include epidemic, pandemic, earthquake, flood, fire, war, civil commotion, sanctions, government action and any other event beyond the reasonable control of the affected party.
notes: Broad force majeure definition including government action; well-suited to Zimbabwe's political risk environment.

text: The Employer may terminate the Contractor's employment for any reason and the Contractor shall be entitled only to an amount equal to the Defined Cost incurred to date less a 12.5% reduction.
notes: Termination for convenience with punitive 10% reduction; contractor has no entitlement to profit or pipeline contribution.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall not be entitled to any additional payment for compliance with any new law, regulation or standard enacted after the Contract Date.
notes: Full post-contract regulatory risk on contractor; particularly dangerous in Zimbabwe's evolving regulatory environment.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer may terminate the Contractor's employment at will and without cause upon giving 7 days' written notice, whereupon the Contractor shall be entitled only to payment for work properly executed.
notes: At-will termination without compensation for loss of anticipated profit or demobilisation costs; highly one-sided.

text: Extensions of Time shall be granted for Exceptional Events and other matters set out in Sub-Clause 8.5.
notes: Standard FIDIC EOT provision covering listed relief events.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, unavailability of foreign currency shall constitute a force majeure event under this Contract.
notes: Forex unavailability as FM event; critical protection for Zimbabwe contracts.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall not be entitled to a compensation event for delay caused by any action or inaction of a subcontractor or supplier.

notes: Contract section heading.

text: The parties agree that no payment shall be made for materials off-site unless the Contractor provides proof of insurance, vesting certificate and list of materials.

notes: FIDIC particular conditions heading.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall continue to work diligently pending resolution of any dispute and shall not suspend work merely because a dispute exists.
notes: Work continuation obligation during disputes; limits contractor's leverage but maintains project progress.

notes: Standard EOT entitlement clause; contractor protected for specified relief events.

text: SECTION 4 – PAYMENT
notes: Contract section heading.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall give notice of a force majeure event within 14 days of its commencement; failure to give notice within this period shall disentitle the Contractor from any relief.
notes: 14-day FM notice as condition precedent; strict but proportionate.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor is to obtain the Project Manager's approval before starting the design of each part of the works.
notes: Design approval obligation; standard for ECC Option A with contractor design.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall not be entitled to loss of profit on omitted works where variations reduce the scope of the Contract.
notes: Exclusion of loss of profit on omissions is common but limits contractor's commercial protection.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Project Manager certifies a payment in the amount due within one week of each assessment date.
notes: Payment certification timeline; standard NEC4.

text: Dispute shall first be referred to mediation; if not resolved within 72 hours either party may refer the dispute to adjudication in accordance with the Scheme for Construction Contracts.
notes: Tiered dispute resolution with adjudication backstop; balanced and HGCRA compliant.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, any claim not submitted within 14 days of the cause of action arising is time-barred and shall not be considered.
notes: 14-day claim bar is impractically short; causes loss of legitimate claims on complex projects.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, no interim payment shall be due to the Contractor until the Employer has received and verified the Contractor's application; the Employer shall have 60 days to make such verification.
notes: Verification period far exceeds HGCRA requirements; effectively extends payment cycle to 75+ days.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall maintain the Accepted Programme; failure to maintain an accepted programme for more than 4 weeks results in a reduction in the PWDD of 15%.
notes: 15% PWDD reduction for programme lapse; punitive financial sanction for administrative failure.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall submit a construction programme in bar chart form within 21 days of the Commencement Date and update it monthly.
notes: Programme obligation; standard practice in Zimbabwe.

notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor is entitled to a compensation event where a physical condition encountered is one which an experienced contractor would have judged at the Contract Date to have a small chance of occurring.

text: The Contractor shall maintain the Accepted Programme; failure to maintain an accepted programme for more than 4 weeks results in a reduction in the PWDD of 15%.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor is entitled to a compensation event where a physical condition encountered is one which an experienced contractor would have judged at the Contract Date to have a small chance of occurring.

notes: 180-day final account settlement with adjudication backstop; reasonable overall.

notes: Full unforeseeable ground risk passed to contractor without any site investigation warranty from employer.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall comply with the Employer's Health, Safety and Environment policy as issued and updated from time to time.
notes: HSE policy compliance obligation; standard.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor is entitled to Exceptional Event relief for epidemics, natural catastrophes, wars, embargoes and acts of government.
notes: Broad exceptional event relief including government action; relevant for Zimbabwe sovereign risk.

text: The Contractor shall provide a retention bond and a performance bond each equal to 15% of the Contract Price, totalling 20% of the Contract Price, within 7 days of award.
notes: Excessive bonding requirement of 20% combined; significant liquidity burden on contractor.

notes: PI insurance obligation post-completion; standard on design-and-build contracts.
