text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall submit a construction programme in bar chart form within 21 days of the Commencement Date and update it monthly.
notes: Programme obligation; standard practice in Zimbabwe.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, if a force majeure event continues for more than 90 days, either party may terminate the Contract by giving 14 days' notice; the Contractor shall be paid for work completed.
notes: Bilateral termination right on prolonged force majeure; contractor recovers value of work done.

text: Dispute shall first be referred to mediation; if not resolved within 14 days either party may refer the dispute to adjudication in accordance with the Scheme for Construction Contracts.
notes: Tiered dispute resolution with adjudication backstop; balanced and HGCRA compliant.

text: The parties agree that hyperinflationary conditions that render the Contract commercially impossible to perform shall constitute a force majeure event.
notes: Hyperinflation as FM event; Zimbabwe-specific protection for contractor.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor repays to the Employer the amount of any overpayment on the next payment date.
notes: Overpayment recovery; balanced and expected.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall not be entitled to additional cost arising from a force majeure event; its sole entitlement is an extension of time.
notes: Time-only relief for force majeure; contractor bears standing cost during FM period.

text: CLAUSE 3 – THE ENGINEER
notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall submit all CE quotations within 21 days; late submission means the PM's own assessment is binding on the Contractor without review.
notes: PM's assessment binding on late quotation; removes contractor's ability to challenge PM's figures.

text: Words importing the singular include the plural and vice versa; words importing any gender include all genders.
notes: Standard interpretation provision.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer may suspend the Works for any reason for up to 60 days without this constituting a termination; during suspension the Contractor shall stand down without payment.
notes: Unpaid suspension right; contractor bears standing costs during employer-requested suspension.

text: The Contractor shall provide a performance bond in the sum of 7.5% of the Contract Sum within 14 days of execution of the Contract.
notes: Performance bond at 10% is at the higher end; bond cost is a contractor risk.

text: SECTION 9 – SETTLEMENT OF DISPUTES
notes: Contract section heading.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall provide product warranties from all specialist suppliers in the Employer's name for a minimum of 10 years.
notes: Third-party warranty assignment obligation; requires supplier co-operation.

text: In accordance with industry practice, either party may terminate if a force majeure event continues for more than 180 days, with the Contractor entitled to all costs incurred to the termination date.
notes: FM termination after 180 days with full cost recovery; balanced and reasonable.

text: Any amounts due to the Contractor may be set off against any claim the Employer may have against the Contractor under any other contract between the parties.
notes: Cross-contract set-off right; allows employer to use disputes on other contracts to withhold current payments.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall be responsible for setting out the Works from the reference points provided and shall verify all dimensions before commencing construction.
notes: Contractor's setting-out responsibility; standard obligation.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor is to obtain the Project Manager's approval before starting the design of each part of the works.
notes: Design approval obligation; standard for ECC Option A with contractor design.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall be responsible for the security of the entire Site, including all Employer-stored materials, and shall indemnify the Employer for any theft or loss on Site.
notes: Security indemnity for employer's materials; imposes insurance cost for employer's own property.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall complete the Works within the time stated even if access, materials or instructions are delayed; no extension of time shall be granted for any cause whatsoever.
notes: Absolute completion obligation with no EOT; imposes strict liability for delays not within contractor's control.

text: The parties agree that if the Project Manager does not reply to a communication from the Contractor within the period for reply, the Contractor may notify the Project Manager that the period has expired.
notes: Inactivity CE mechanism; protects contractor from PM delay in responding.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall indemnify the Employer against all third party claims arising from the Contractor's operations, including claims resulting from the Employer's acts or omissions.
notes: Indemnity extends to employer's own acts; removes employer's responsibility for its own negligence.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall not be entitled to a compensation event where the Contractor failed to give early warning of the matter which caused the event.
notes: Loss of CE entitlement for failure to give early warning; strict application has harsh financial effect.

text: It is hereby agreed that government-imposed price controls that prevent the Contractor from procuring materials at market prices shall entitle the Contractor to an adjustment of the Contract Price.
notes: Price control risk on employer; government intervention risk passed back to employer.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall be entitled to an extension of time and additional cost for delays caused by unavailability of foreign currency for imported materials, provided such unavailability is documented by a bank.
notes: Forex-induced delay recognised as relief event; critical protection in Zimbabwe's USD-constrained environment.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall retain the right to remove from Site any equipment, materials and temporary works paid for by the Contractor at any time.
notes: Retention of title and removal right; protects contractor's assets against employer default.

text: The Employer shall pay the final balance of the Contract Sum within 14 days of the issue of the Final Certificate.
notes: Clear final payment timeline; balanced obligation.

text: Either Party may terminate the Contractor's obligation to Provide the Works if the other Party has committed a reason stated in this Contract.
notes: Standard mutual termination for cause; balanced.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer may instruct the Contractor to submit a revised quotation if the Project Manager considers the original quotation unreasonable, and the Project Manager's assessment shall be final.
notes: PM's assessment of CE is final; removes contractor's ability to challenge valuation.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall erect and maintain a Project Sign Board in accordance with the Employer's specifications within 7 days of Commencement.
notes: Signage obligation; administrative, low risk.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Employer may instruct the Contractor to submit a revised quotation if the Project Manager considers the original quotation unreasonable, and the Project Manager's assessment shall be final.
notes: PM's assessment of CE is final; removes contractor's ability to challenge valuation.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall provide a Performance Security equal to 15% of the Accepted Contract Amount.
notes: 15% performance security is above FIDIC standard 10%; significant liquidity cost.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall be liable for consequential, indirect and economic loss suffered by the Employer arising out of any breach, delay or defect, without financial cap.
notes: Uncapped consequential loss liability is commercially unacceptable and uninsurable.

text: 'Commencement Date' means the date stated in the Letter of Acceptance from which the Contract Period is calculated.
notes: Standard definition; administrative.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Client may terminate the Contractor's obligation to Provide the Works at any time.
notes: Termination for convenience right; contractor receives defined cost plus profit entitlement under NEC4.

text: 'Key Date' means the date by which the Contractor is to meet a Condition stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Standard NEC4 Key Date definition.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall be entitled to a daywork rate premium of 35% on direct cost for all instructed dayworks, together with a percentage addition for profit and overhead.
notes: Above-market daywork premium; improves contractor's margin on time-and-materials work.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Employer may terminate this Contract at any time without cause or notice, and the Contractor shall have no claim for loss of profit, overhead recovery or any other damages arising from such termination.
notes: At-will termination without compensation; contractor loses all anticipated returns on investment.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall pay all employees a minimum wage not less than the applicable ZITS/ZCEA minimum wage for the construction sector.
notes: Labour law compliance; Zimbabwe Construction Industry Federation wage requirement.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall provide and maintain adequate lighting on the Site during working hours.
notes: Standard site obligation; low risk.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall attend progress meetings at such intervals as the Employer's Agent may reasonably require.
notes: Standard meeting obligation; reasonable frequency qualifier protects contractor.

text: It is hereby agreed that payment applications shall be submitted by the Contractor on the 25th of each month; the Employer's Quantity Surveyor shall certify within 14 days and payment shall be made within 30 days of certification.
notes: Payment cycle of approximately 44 days; acceptable but on the longer side for Zimbabwe market.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall maintain the Accepted Programme; failure to maintain an accepted programme for more than 4 weeks results in a reduction in the PWDD of 15%.
notes: 15% PWDD reduction for programme lapse; punitive financial sanction for administrative failure.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall provide product warranties from all specialist suppliers in the Employer's name for a minimum of 10 years.
notes: Third-party warranty assignment obligation; requires supplier co-operation.

text: The parties agree that the Employer shall provide such information as the Contractor may reasonably require to carry out the Works.
notes: Mutual information obligation; protects contractor's right to information.

text: The Contractor's share of any cost overrun shall be 100% for the first 7.5% of overrun and 50% thereafter; the Employer's share of savings is 70%.
notes: Asymmetric pain/gain share highly unfavourable to contractor; 100% pain on first 10% with only 30% gain.

text: SECTION 1 – GENERAL
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, all notices under this Contract shall be in writing and may be served by email, fax, hand delivery or first class post.
notes: Clear service of notice provision; balanced and practical.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer shall provide such information as the Contractor may reasonably require to carry out the Works.
notes: Mutual information obligation; protects contractor's right to information.

text: The parties agree that where a force majeure event affects both parties, each shall bear its own costs and no claim shall be made against the other.
notes: Bilateral cost-bearing on shared FM events; balanced loss allocation.

text: In accordance with industry practice, if the Project Manager does not assess a compensation event within the time allowed, the Contractor's quotation is treated as accepted.
notes: Deemed acceptance of contractor's CE quotation on PM inaction; contractor-favourable mechanism.

text: It is hereby agreed that all payments shall be subject to deduction of withholding tax at the rate applicable under Zimbabwe Revenue Authority regulations.
notes: ZIMRA withholding tax deduction; statutory requirement reducing contractor's net receipts.

text: It is hereby agreed that any amounts due to the Contractor may be set off against any claim the Employer may have against the Contractor under any other contract between the parties.
notes: Cross-contract set-off right; allows employer to use disputes on other contracts to withhold current payments.

text: WHEREAS, XYZ, in its Invitation to Bid no. called for tenders for the Construction of Super Diversion Canal and Related Structures and Supply and installation of Gates for Casecnan Multipurpose Irrigation and Power Project- Irrigation Component, in accordance with the Bid Documents.
SIGNED for and on behalf of THE CONTRACTOR by a duly authorised representative:
notes: Execution signature block.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, where the Employer fails to give possession of the Site on the Date of Possession, such failure shall be a Relevant Matter entitling the Contractor to loss and expense.
notes: Contractor protected for employer's failure to give possession.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor is to assess the compensation event using the Shorter Schedule of Cost Components where the work has not been done.
notes: SSCC use for prospective CEs; requires forecast which introduces uncertainty.

text: CLAUSE 1 – GENERAL PROVISIONS
notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall keep a daily site diary recording weather conditions, labour strength, materials received and work carried out.
notes: Site record-keeping obligation; standard practice.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall ensure that all waste generated during the Works is disposed of in accordance with EMA regulations.
notes: Waste disposal obligation; EMA compliance standard.

text: The parties agree that the Project Manager may instruct a change to the Scope and the change is a compensation event.
notes: Standard CE for scope change; balanced provision.

text: 'Employer's Representative' means the person appointed by the Employer to administer the Contract on its behalf.
notes: Standard ER definition.

text: In accordance with industry practice, no CE shall arise from any instruction given orally by the Project Manager; all instructions must be in writing and logged in the Risk Register.
notes: Oral instruction exclusion; contractors regularly act on PM oral instructions on live sites.

text: The Engineer shall issue Payment Certificates within 48 hours of receiving the Contractor's Statement.
notes: 28-day certification period; standard FIDIC payment cycle.

text: SCHEDULE OF RATES AND QUANTITIES
notes: Pricing document heading.

text: 'Completion Date' means the date stated in the Contract Particulars for completion of the Works, as adjusted by any extension of time granted under this Contract.
notes: Standard definition.

text: 'Commencement Date' means the date stated in the Letter of Acceptance from which the Contract Period is calculated.

notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: All variations must be supported by a Variation Order signed by the Employer's Representative before work commences, except in cases of urgency where verbal instruction may be confirmed in writing within 48 hours.
notes: VO requirement with urgency exception; balanced but requires contractor diligence.

text: This contract is a NEC4 Engineering and Construction Contract.
notes: Contract identification clause.

text: SECTION 8: FORCE MAJEURE
notes: Force majeure section heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor is to refer a dispute to the Dispute Avoidance Board within 4 weeks of becoming aware of the dispute.
notes: DAB referral timeframe; standard NEC4 W3 procedure.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, if the Contractor desires to make any variation to the design or specification, it shall submit a formal request to the Employer's Agent, who may consent or refuse at their reasonable discretion.
notes: Variation approval subject to reasonable discretion; some restriction on contractor flexibility.

text: SECTION 9: DISPUTE RESOLUTION
notes: Dispute resolution section heading.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, payment applications must be submitted in the prescribed format using the Employer's software system; applications in any other format shall not constitute valid applications.
notes: Format condition precedent on payment applications; employer can reject valid applications on technical grounds.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall pay liquidated and ascertained damages at the rate stated in the Contract Particulars for every week or part week during which Practical Completion is delayed beyond the Completion Date, without limit as to time or amount.
notes: Uncapped LADs with no causal link required; significant financial exposure for contractor.

text: The Contractor and the Subcontractor shall deliver to each other, within five days of receipt of same, accurate and complete copies of all reports on tests carried out by or on behalf of either Party, save to the extent that such reports are prepared specifically for the purpose of dispute resolution proceedings.
The parties agree that the seat of arbitration shall be Harare, Zimbabwe; the arbitrator shall have authority to grant interim relief.
notes: Harare-seated arbitration; appropriate for Zimbabwe construction disputes.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall be liable for liquidated damages at the rate of USD 5,000 per day for every day of delay beyond the Completion Date, without any limit on the aggregate amount recoverable.
notes: Uncapped daily LADs in USD; creates severe and uninsurable financial exposure for Zimbabwean contractors.

notes: FIDIC particular conditions heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, any extension of time application by the Contractor must be made within 7 days of the occurrence of the relevant event, failing which the Contractor shall be deemed to have waived its entitlement.
notes: Extremely short notice period; strict waiver provision with no discretion for Engineer/Architect.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Project Manager may instruct a change to the Scope and the change is a compensation event.
notes: Standard CE for scope change; balanced provision.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall indemnify and hold harmless the Employer against all claims, losses and liabilities arising out of or in connection with the Works, whether or not caused by the Contractor's negligence.
notes: Indemnity without negligence requirement effectively makes contractor an insurer for all site events.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Architect shall issue Interim Payment Certificates at the intervals stated in the Contract Particulars, and the Employer shall pay within 14 days of the due date.
notes: Clear payment timeline; HGCRA compliant.

text: The Employer shall provide the Contractor with a Letter of Credit from an approved bank as security for payment of amounts exceeding USD 150,000.
notes: Payment security mechanism; protects contractor against employer insolvency on large contracts.

text: 'Defects Liability Period' means the period of 12 months following Practical Completion during which the Contractor is liable to rectify defects.
notes: Standard DLP definition.

text: In accordance with industry practice, payment applications must be submitted in the prescribed format using the Employer's software system; applications in any other format shall not constitute valid applications.

The Employer may arrange for necessary medical assistance for the affected Contractor employees and the costs for such medical services shall be borne by the Contractor.

notes: Definitions section heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall keep and maintain proper books of account relating to the Works and shall make them available for inspection by the Employer's Quantity Surveyor on 48 hours' notice.

text: CONTRACT DATA PART 1 – DATA PROVIDED BY THE EMPLOYER
notes: Standard NEC4 document heading.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall provide a Performance Security equal to 15% of the Accepted Contract Amount.
notes: 15% performance security is above FIDIC standard 10%; significant liquidity cost.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor Provides the Works in accordance with the Scope and this Contract.
notes: Fundamental contractor obligation; standard.

text: 'Contract Price' means the sum stated in the Letter of Acceptance, subject to adjustment in accordance with this Contract.
notes: Standard contract price definition.

text: ANNEXURE B: CONSTRUCTION PROGRAMME
notes: Programme annexure heading.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall be entitled to payment of interest at a rate of 5% above the RBZ overnight lending rate on all overdue amounts calculated from the due date.
notes: Interest on late payment tied to RBZ rate; commercially reasonable and common on Zimbabwe contracts.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, force majeure events include epidemic, pandemic, earthquake, flood, fire, war, civil commotion, sanctions, government action and any other event beyond the reasonable control of the affected party.
notes: Broad force majeure definition including government action; well-suited to Zimbabwe's political risk environment.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, any claim not submitted within 14 days of the cause of action arising is time-barred and shall not be considered.
notes: 14-day claim bar is impractically short; causes loss of legitimate claims on complex projects.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Engineer shall issue Payment Certificates within 28 days of receiving the Contractor's Statement.
notes: 28-day certification period; standard FIDIC payment cycle.

text: It is expressly agreed and understood that in case of any irreconcilable conflict between this Contract and the provisions of any of the Contract Documents, the former shall be the controlling.
notes: Clause creates significant risk for subcontractor; one-sided in favour of contractor/employer.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the parties confirm that this Contract constitutes the entire agreement between them in respect of the Works and supersedes all prior negotiations.
notes: Entire agreement clause; standard contract formation provision.

text: The Employer shall make an Advance Payment of 5% of the Accepted Contract Amount within 21 days of receipt of the Performance Security.
notes: FIDIC advance payment at 10%; standard and contractor-supportive.

text: SECTION 4 – PAYMENT
notes: Contract section heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor is to give an early warning of any matter which could increase the total Defined Cost, delay Completion or impair performance of the works in use.
notes: EW obligation is core NEC4 requirement; failure to warn may reduce CE entitlement.

text: 'Defects Notification Period' means the period for notifying defects in the Works or a Section as stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Standard FIDIC DNP definition.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor repays to the Employer the amount of any overpayment on the next payment date.
notes: Overpayment recovery; balanced and expected.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor's liability shall not be limited under any circumstances; any contractual cap on liability is expressly excluded.
notes: Removal of any liability cap; contractor faces unlimited financial exposure.

text: 'Practical Completion' means the stage at which the Works are sufficiently complete to be used for their intended purpose, as certified by the Employer's Representative.
notes: Standard practical completion definition for Zimbabwe contracts.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, practical Completion shall not be certified unless all snagging items and outstanding works have been fully completed to the Employer's Agent's absolute satisfaction.
notes: Subjective practical completion test prevents contractor triggering LAD stop; unlimited retention period.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, where the Employer fails to provide something they are to provide by the date stated, the Contractor is entitled to a compensation event for delay and additional cost.
notes: Employer supply chain risk passed back to employer; contractor protected for late employer-furnished items.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, after the Taking-Over Certificate is issued, risk of loss or damage to the Works shall pass to the Employer.
notes: Risk transfer on taking-over; standard FIDIC risk allocation.

text: SECTION 5 – PAYMENT
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall complete the Works within the time stated even if access, materials or instructions are delayed; no extension of time shall be granted for any cause whatsoever.
notes: Absolute completion obligation with no EOT; imposes strict liability for delays not within contractor's control.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor warrants that the completed Works will achieve the energy performance rating specified in the Employer's Requirements.
notes: Performance warranty; creates ongoing liability if building underperforms.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, an amount due is calculated at each assessment date by the Project Manager and Contractor jointly, or by the Project Manager alone if the Contractor does not attend.
notes: Joint assessment preferred; PM can proceed alone if contractor absent.

text: 'Defects Liability Period' means the period of 12 months following Practical Completion during which the Contractor is liable to rectify defects.
notes: Standard DLP definition.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, all variations must be supported by a Variation Order signed by the Employer's Representative before work commences, except in cases of urgency where verbal instruction may be confirmed in writing within 48 hours.
notes: VO requirement with urgency exception; balanced but requires contractor diligence.

text: ANNEXURE B: CONSTRUCTION PROGRAMME
notes: Programme annexure heading.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, in the event of any dispute, the Employer's decision shall be final and binding; the Contractor shall continue to perform the Works pending any subsequent arbitration.
notes: Employer as sole arbiter of disputes; no independent decision-making; contractor must perform under adverse decisions.

text: It is hereby agreed that all Contractor's equipment, materials and temporary works brought onto Site shall be deemed to be the property of the Employer for the duration of the Contract and shall not be removed without Employer's written consent.
notes: Deemed vesting of contractor's property; exposes contractor to loss if employer becomes insolvent or hostile.

text: SECTION 8: FORCE MAJEURE
notes: Force majeure section heading.

text: The parties shall attempt to resolve any dispute by negotiation within 48 hours of written notice of the dispute.
notes: Negotiation first step is standard and commercially sensible.

text: The parties agree that extension of time shall be granted where the Contractor demonstrates on the balance of probabilities that a Relevant Event caused critical delay to Practical Completion.
notes: Causal link and balance of probabilities test is standard but requires robust programming evidence.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Project Manager may assess a compensation event at any time and the Contractor shall have no right to submit its own quotation.
notes: Removal of contractor's right to quote for CEs; PM-only assessment is contrary to NEC4 spirit.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall ensure that all waste generated during the Works is disposed of in accordance with EMA regulations.
notes: Waste disposal obligation; EMA compliance standard.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor's share of savings achieved below the Target Cost shall be calculated at 50% of the underspend.
notes: 50% gainshare on Option C target; significant upside for efficient contractor.

text: The Employer shall provide the Contractor with a Letter of Credit from an approved bank as security for payment of amounts exceeding £250,000.
notes: Payment security mechanism; protects contractor against employer insolvency on large contracts.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall repay all amounts received under interim certificates if the final account shows the total certified was overpaid, with interest at 8% above base rate.
notes: Retroactive overpayment recovery with punitive interest; creates cash flow uncertainty throughout project.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Works shall be carried out in accordance with the drawings, specifications and Bill of Quantities forming part of this Contract.
notes: Fundamental obligation to comply with contract documents; standard.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor accepts no warranty liability for defects arising from the Employer's design or specification.
notes: Design warranty carve-out for employer-provided design; fair on contractor.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall be responsible for the security of the entire Site, including all Employer-stored materials, and shall indemnify the Employer for any theft or loss on Site.
notes: Security indemnity for employer's materials; imposes insurance cost for employer's own property.

text: It is hereby agreed that either party shall give 14 days' written notice of any change in address for service of notices.
notes: Notice address update obligation; administrative.

text: PROGRAMME
notes: Heading or procedural clause; no material risk allocation.

text: In accordance with industry practice, where the Employer fails to issue a payment notice or pay less notice by the prescribed date, the sum stated in the Contractor's payment application shall become the sum due.
notes: Default payment mechanism under HGCRA; contractor's application becomes notified sum if employer fails to serve notice.

text: SECTION 10: TERMINATION
notes: Termination section heading.

text: SECTION 8 – TERMINATION
notes: Contract section heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, retention shall be at the rate of 5% until Practical Completion, reducing to 2.5% thereafter, and shall be released at the end of the Defects Liability Period.
notes: Standard retention regime; rates are at the higher end of market practice.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall complete the Works and all snagging within the Defects Liability Period without any entitlement to prolongation costs or disruption claims.
notes: Contractor bears full prolongation and disruption costs even where caused by employer actions.

text: It is hereby agreed that all payments under this Contract shall be made in United States Dollars; any payment made in RTGS or ZiG currency shall not discharge the Contractor's payment obligation.
notes: USD-only payment in reverse; in context where contractor is employer, this shifts forex risk onto subcontractor.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Employer shall pay the Contractor within 56 days of the Engineer issuing a Payment Certificate.
notes: 56-day payment period; on the longer side under FIDIC Red Book.

text: It is hereby agreed that arbitration shall be conducted under the Zimbabwe Arbitration Act [Chapter 7:15] and the rules of the Arbitration Foundation of Southern Africa (AFSA).
notes: Zimbabwe arbitration framework; appropriate institutional rules for regional commercial disputes.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer shall have the right to vary the scope of Works by up to 50% without any adjustment to the unit rates or time for completion.
notes: 50% scope variation without rate adjustment; contractor's preliminaries and profit margin severely eroded on major omissions.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, where changes in Laws after the Base Date increase the Contractor's Costs, the Contract Price shall be adjusted accordingly.
notes: Change-in-law risk on employer; contractor protected from sovereign risk after Base Date.

text: SECTION 1 – DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION
notes: Contract heading/section marker.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, retention shall be held by the Employer in its general account and shall not attract interest; no obligation to place retention in a separate trust account arises.
notes: Retention is not ring-fenced; contractor has no protection if employer becomes insolvent.

text: Payments shall be made in United States Dollars.

Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, an amount due is calculated at each assessment date by the Project Manager and Contractor jointly, or by the Project Manager alone if the Contractor does not attend.
notes: Joint assessment preferred; PM can proceed alone if contractor absent.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall not be entitled to any additional payment or extension of time arising from any ground conditions, howsoever arising, whether foreseeable or unforeseeable.
notes: Full unforeseeable ground risk passed to contractor without any site investigation warranty from employer.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer may instruct the Contractor to accelerate the programme; the Contractor shall comply and any claim for acceleration costs shall be submitted within 7 days of the instruction.
notes: 7-day acceleration claim window is impractical; employer can demand acceleration without pre-agreeing cost.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor is to submit the first assessment of the amount due no later than the first assessment date.
notes: Application submission obligation; standard NEC4.

text: 'Architect' means the person named as such in the Contract Particulars or any replacement appointed by the Employer.
notes: Standard definition; identifies key contract administrator.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall retain title to all temporary works and equipment on site until paid in full; the Employer acknowledges no lien or right of retention over such items.
notes: Romalpa-style retention of title clause; protects contractor's plant and equipment on employer insolvency.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall maintain public roads used by construction traffic in a clean and tidy condition at all times.
notes: Road maintenance obligation; standard and reasonable.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, hyperinflationary conditions that render the Contract commercially impossible to perform shall constitute a force majeure event.
notes: Hyperinflation as FM event; Zimbabwe-specific protection for contractor.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall pay liquidated and ascertained damages at the rate stated in the Contract Particulars for every week or part week during which Practical Completion is delayed beyond the Completion Date, without limit as to time or amount.
notes: Uncapped LADs with no causal link required; significant financial exposure for contractor.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer may instruct the Contractor to accelerate the programme; the Contractor shall comply and any claim for acceleration costs shall be submitted within 7 days of the instruction.
notes: 7-day acceleration claim window is impractical; employer can demand acceleration without pre-agreeing cost.

text: It is hereby agreed that all disputes shall be resolved by arbitration in terms of the Arbitration Act [Chapter 7:15] of Zimbabwe; the arbitrator shall be appointed solely by the Employer.
notes: Employer-appointed arbitrator lacks independence; contrary to fair arbitration principles.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Employer shall provide such information as the Contractor may reasonably require to carry out the Works.
notes: Mutual information obligation; protects contractor's right to information.

text: SCHEDULE OF RATES AND QUANTITIES
notes: Pricing document heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall keep a daily site diary recording weather conditions, labour strength, materials received and work carried out.
notes: Site record-keeping obligation; standard practice.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall be entitled to an extension of time and additional cost for delays caused by unavailability of foreign currency for imported materials, provided such unavailability is documented by a bank.
notes: Forex-induced delay recognised as relief event; critical protection in Zimbabwe's USD-constrained environment.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Employer may terminate the Contractor's employment at will and without cause upon giving 7 days' written notice, whereupon the Contractor shall be entitled only to payment for work properly executed.
notes: At-will termination without compensation for loss of anticipated profit or demobilisation costs; highly one-sided.

text: The Contractor may terminate if the Employer fails to pay a certified sum within 28 days of the final date for payment after 14 days' notice.
notes: Contractor termination right for employer non-payment; standard protection.

text: The parties agree that an amount due is calculated at each assessment date by the Project Manager and Contractor jointly, or by the Project Manager alone if the Contractor does not attend.
notes: Joint assessment preferred; PM can proceed alone if contractor absent.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall provide training to the Employer's maintenance staff on the operation of installed plant and equipment.
notes: Training obligation; standard on M&E and infrastructure contracts.

text: The parties agree that the Defects Liability Period for roofing works shall be 5 years from Practical Completion.
notes: Extended DLP for specialist trade; common on roofing and waterproofing.

text: The Engineer's determination of any claim shall be final and binding unless challenged by a DAAB decision within 42 days.
notes: Short DAAB challenge window; practical difficulty in convening DAAB within 28 days in Zimbabwe.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall retain title to all temporary works and equipment on site until paid in full; the Employer acknowledges no lien or right of retention over such items.
notes: Romalpa-style retention of title clause; protects contractor's plant and equipment on employer insolvency.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor is to assess the compensation event using the Shorter Schedule of Cost Components where the work has not been done.
notes: SSCC use for prospective CEs; requires forecast which introduces uncertainty.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Employer's Agent may issue instructions requiring the Contractor to open up work for inspection; where the work is found to be in accordance with the Contract, the cost shall be added to the Contract Sum.
notes: Cost recovery mechanism for compliant open-up is fair; risk of disruption remains.

text: The Employer shall provide the Contractor with a Letter of Credit from an approved bank as security for payment of amounts exceeding USD 50,000.
notes: Payment security mechanism; protects contractor against employer insolvency on large contracts.

text: In this Contract, the following terms have the meanings given to them below unless the context otherwise requires.
notes: Standard definitions introduction.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, all risk of ground conditions at the Site remains with the Contractor regardless of the Site Information provided; no compensation event arises from physical conditions.
notes: Full ground risk on contractor even where site information proves inaccurate; contrary to Z60.1 CE.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall comply with all applicable laws, regulations and permit conditions throughout the Contract.
notes: General regulatory compliance; standard obligation.

text: SECTION 6: VARIATIONS
notes: Variations section heading.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall indemnify the Employer against all costs, claims and expenses arising from the Contractor's presence on site, including claims by third parties, whether or not caused by the Contractor's negligence.
notes: Negligence-free indemnity; contractor becomes insurer for all site-related third party claims.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, any amounts due to the Contractor may be set off against any claim the Employer may have against the Contractor under any other contract between the parties.
notes: Cross-contract set-off right; allows employer to use disputes on other contracts to withhold current payments.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Project Manager may change the Scope to accept a Defect.
notes: Scope change to accept defect with compensation adjustment; balanced.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, payment shall only be made quarterly; no interim payments shall be due during the Contract Period regardless of the value of work executed.
notes: Quarterly payment cycle; creates severe cash flow hardship for contractor, particularly in Zimbabwe's inflation environment.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Defects Liability Period shall be 12 months from the date of Practical Completion during which the Contractor shall rectify defects notified by the Employer within 21 days.
notes: Standard DLP provision; 21-day notice-to-rectify window is reasonable.

text: SECTION 9 – TERMINATION
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: The Project Manager gives access to the Site to the Contractor on the access date stated in Contract Data Part 1.
notes: Standard access provision; employer obligation.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, a Defect is a part of the works which is not in accordance with the Scope.
notes: Standard definition of defect.

text: It is hereby agreed that where the final account value exceeds the Contract Sum by more than 15%, the Contractor shall be entitled to re-price preliminaries and time-related items.
notes: Preliminaries re-pricing right on substantial scope increase; protects contractor on poorly scoped projects.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall not be entitled to any additional payment for compliance with any new law, regulation or standard enacted after the Contract Date.
notes: Full post-contract regulatory risk on contractor; particularly dangerous in Zimbabwe's evolving regulatory environment.

text: The Subcontractor shall provide all necessary assistance, labour, materials, testing equipment and instruments for the purpose of such tests to be performed by himself, or if so instructed by the Contractor, for the purpose of tests to be performed by others.
notes: Clause creates significant risk for subcontractor; one-sided in favour of contractor/employer.

text: In accordance with industry practice, all payments under this Contract shall be made in United States Dollars; any payment made in RTGS or ZiG currency shall not discharge the Contractor's payment obligation.
notes: USD-only payment in reverse; in context where contractor is employer, this shifts forex risk onto subcontractor.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor is to provide the Project Manager with a first programme showing the information required by this Contract.
notes: Programme provision obligation; standard.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall be entitled to a pre-agreed sum for demobilisation costs payable on Practical Completion.
notes: Express demobilisation payment; reduces contractor's post-completion financial exposure.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor and Project Manager attend each early warning meeting.
notes: Risk register and EW meeting attendance; collaborative and standard.

text: The Contractor repays to the Employer the amount of any overpayment on the next payment date.
notes: Overpayment recovery; balanced and expected.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall continue to work diligently pending resolution of any dispute and shall not suspend work merely because a dispute exists.
notes: Work continuation obligation during disputes; limits contractor's leverage but maintains project progress.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor is to provide the Project Manager with information which the Project Manager needs in order to issue a certificate.
notes: Information provision obligation; standard.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Project Manager may instruct the Contractor to use a different method of working without it being a compensation event if the change is necessary for safety.
notes: Safety-based method change without CE; employer defines 'necessary' unilaterally.

text: CLAUSE 20 – EMPLOYER'S AND CONTRACTOR'S CLAIMS
notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: The Contractor may terminate if the Employer fails to pay a certified sum within 72 hours of the final date for payment after 14 days' notice.
notes: Contractor termination right for employer non-payment; standard protection.

text: It is hereby agreed that sub-contracting of portions of the Works exceeding 25% of the Contract Sum requires prior written consent from the Employer's Agent.
notes: Sub-contracting threshold consent requirement is commercially acceptable.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, no interim payment shall be due to the Contractor until the Employer has received and verified the Contractor's application; the Employer shall have 60 days to make such verification.
notes: Verification period far exceeds HGCRA requirements; effectively extends payment cycle to 75+ days.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Project Manager certifies a payment in the amount due within one week of each assessment date.
notes: Payment certification timeline; standard NEC4.

text: The parties agree that the Project Manager may instruct a change to the Scope and the change is a compensation event.
notes: Standard CE for scope change; balanced provision.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall provide adequate sanitary facilities for all site workers throughout the Contract Period.
notes: Site welfare obligation; standard and required by NSSA.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall be liable for delay damages at the stated rate from the scheduled Completion Date, whether or not the delay was caused partly by the Employer.
notes: LADs run even where employer is partly responsible; no concurrent delay apportionment.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall comply with all ZESA regulations regarding electrical installations and shall obtain ZESA certification before energising any electrical systems.
notes: Standard regulatory compliance obligation; Zimbabwe ZESA requirement.

text: The parties agree that a Defect is a part of the works which is not in accordance with the Scope.
notes: Standard definition of defect.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, provisional sums included in the Contract Bills shall only be expended on the written instruction of the Employer's Representative.
notes: Provisional sum control mechanism; standard QS practice.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Project Manager may instruct the Contractor to stop or not start any work without giving a reason, and no compensation event arises from such instruction.
notes: Removal of compensation event for stop/start instructions; contractor bears cost of downtime.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall comply with all CDM 2015 requirements as Principal Contractor and shall bear the cost of such compliance unless expressly stated otherwise.
notes: CDM compliance is a statutory duty; cost allocation is standard practice.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall be entitled to an extension of time and additional cost for delays caused by unavailability of foreign currency for imported materials, provided such unavailability is documented by a bank.
notes: Forex-induced delay recognised as relief event; critical protection in Zimbabwe's USD-constrained environment.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall not be entitled to additional cost arising from a force majeure event; its sole entitlement is an extension of time.
notes: Time-only relief for force majeure; contractor bears standing cost during FM period.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer shall provide the Contractor with access to borehole water on site at no charge for construction use.
notes: Employer-provided site water; reduces contractor's site overhead.

text: The Contractor shall give notice to the Engineer within 48 hours after the Contractor became aware, or should have become aware, of the event or circumstance giving rise to the claim; failing which the Contractor shall be entitled to no additional payment.
notes: FIDIC 2017 Red Book Clause 20.2.1 time bar; strict 28-day notice condition precedent with permanent loss of entitlement.

text: The parties agree that variations shall be valued using the rates and prices in the Contract Bills where applicable, and where not applicable, at fair rates and prices to be agreed.
notes: Bilateral agreement mechanism for variation valuation; balanced but subject to dispute.

text: The parties agree that the Employer shall certify and pay the sum of all undisputed items in the Contractor's application pending resolution of disputed items.
notes: Undisputed sum payment obligation; protects contractor's cash flow during valuation disputes.

text: The parties agree that all payments shall be subject to deduction of withholding tax at the rate applicable under Zimbabwe Revenue Authority regulations.
notes: ZIMRA withholding tax deduction; statutory requirement reducing contractor's net receipts.

text: SECTION 2 – CONTRACTOR'S OBLIGATIONS
notes: Contract section heading.

text: It is hereby agreed that force majeure events include epidemic, pandemic, earthquake, flood, fire, war, civil commotion, sanctions, government action and any other event beyond the reasonable control of the affected party.
notes: Broad force majeure definition including government action; well-suited to Zimbabwe's political risk environment.

text: 'Employer's Representative' means the person appointed by the Employer to administer the Contract on its behalf.
notes: Standard ER definition.

text: With regard to setting out the subcontract works: 1) The Contractor shall establish the basic reference pegs and benchmarks on the Site, and give to the Subcontractor the particulars thereof in sufficient time to enable the Subcontractor to meet his approved programme;

Notwithstanding any other provision, government-imposed price controls that prevent the Contractor from procuring materials at market prices shall entitle the Contractor to an adjustment of the Contract Price.
notes: Price control risk on employer; government intervention risk passed back to employer.

text: The Employer shall provide the Contractor with a Letter of Credit from an approved bank as security for payment of amounts exceeding £500,000.
notes: Payment security mechanism; protects contractor against employer insolvency on large contracts.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, either party may apply to the High Court of Zimbabwe for urgent interim relief pending arbitration.
notes: Preservation of urgent court relief; balanced dispute mechanism.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall comply with all applicable laws, regulations and permit conditions throughout the Contract.
notes: General regulatory compliance; standard obligation.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, where the Employer fails to issue a payment notice or pay less notice by the prescribed date, the sum stated in the Contractor's payment application shall become the sum due.
notes: Default payment mechanism under HGCRA; contractor's application becomes notified sum if employer fails to serve notice.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall submit a construction programme in bar chart form within 21 days of the Commencement Date and update it monthly.
notes: Programme obligation; standard practice in Zimbabwe.

text: AND WHEREAS the Contractor has given the Sub-Contractor reasonable opportunity of inspecting the provisions of the Principal Contract except the detailed prices of the Contractor included in the Schedules and Bills of Quantities.
notes: Heading or procedural clause; no material risk allocation.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Architect's decision on any matter concerning the quality or quantity of the Works shall be final and binding and shall not be subject to review in any dispute resolution proceedings.
notes: Ousts contractor's right to challenge Architect's decisions; contrary to HGCRA principles.

text: SECTION 9 – TERMINATION
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: It is hereby agreed that any extension of time application by the Contractor must be made within 7 days of the occurrence of the relevant event, failing which the Contractor shall be deemed to have waived its entitlement.
notes: Extremely short notice period; strict waiver provision with no discretion for Engineer/Architect.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, where the Employer fails to issue a payment notice or pay less notice by the prescribed date, the sum stated in the Contractor's payment application shall become the sum due.
notes: Default payment mechanism under HGCRA; contractor's application becomes notified sum if employer fails to serve notice.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, an amount due is calculated at each assessment date by the Project Manager and Contractor jointly, or by the Project Manager alone if the Contractor does not attend.
notes: Joint assessment preferred; PM can proceed alone if contractor absent.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer may at any time instruct the removal of the Contractor's key personnel, and the Contractor shall have no right to object or seek compensation for the disruption caused.
notes: Employer can disrupt contractor's team without justification or compensation.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer may withhold payment of any amount due pending resolution of any dispute, without serving a pay less notice.
notes: Payment withholding without pay less notice undermines NEC4's payment transparency mechanisms.

text: It is hereby agreed that either party may apply to the High Court of Zimbabwe for urgent interim relief pending arbitration.
notes: Preservation of urgent court relief; balanced dispute mechanism.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Employer may terminate the Contract if the Contractor is in material breach and fails to remedy the breach within 14 days of written notice.
notes: Standard termination for cause with 14-day cure period; balanced provision.

text: 'Works Information' means information which specifies and describes the works or states any constraint on how the Contractor Provides the Works.
notes: Standard NEC4 definition.

text: The Contractor shall give notice to the Engineer within 14 days after the Contractor became aware, or should have become aware, of the event or circumstance giving rise to the claim; failing which the Contractor shall be entitled to no additional payment.
notes: FIDIC 2017 Red Book Clause 20.2.1 time bar; strict 28-day notice condition precedent with permanent loss of entitlement.

text: The Engineer shall issue Payment Certificates within 14 days of receiving the Contractor's Statement.
notes: 28-day certification period; standard FIDIC payment cycle.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall keep and maintain proper books of account relating to the Works and shall make them available for inspection by the Employer's Quantity Surveyor on 48 hours' notice.
notes: Open-book accounting obligation; some administrative burden but not unusual.

text: GENERAL CONDITIONS OF CONTRACT
notes: Document heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall comply with all local authority requirements, including those of the City of Harare/Bulawayo City Council, for excavation in public areas.
notes: Local authority compliance obligation; standard but requires advance planning.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, where the final account value exceeds the Contract Sum by more than 15%, the Contractor shall be entitled to re-price preliminaries and time-related items.
notes: Preliminaries re-pricing right on substantial scope increase; protects contractor on poorly scoped projects.

text: In accordance with industry practice, price escalation shall be calculated monthly using the CPIH index published by ZIMSTAT; all increases shall be paid in the same currency as the Contract Sum.
notes: Escalation clause with ZIMSTAT index; full price protection for contractor in inflationary environment.

text: The parties agree that government-imposed price controls that prevent the Contractor from procuring materials at market prices shall entitle the Contractor to an adjustment of the Contract Price.
notes: Price control risk on employer; government intervention risk passed back to employer.

text: The parties agree that the Completion Date is the date in the Accepted Programme for Completion, adjusted for compensation events.
notes: Completion Date definition; programme-linked.

text: The parties shall attempt to resolve any dispute by negotiation within 7 days of written notice of the dispute.
notes: Negotiation first step is standard and commercially sensible.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall submit all CE quotations within 21 days; late submission means the PM's own assessment is binding on the Contractor without review.
