text: Notwithstanding any other provision, retention shall be held by the Employer in its general account and shall not attract interest; no obligation to place retention in a separate trust account arises.
notes: Retention is not ring-fenced; contractor has no protection if employer becomes insolvent.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall submit a final account within 90 days of Practical Completion.
notes: Final account submission obligation; standard.

text: SECTION 3 – CONTROL OF THE WORKS
notes: Contract section heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer provides the Site and the means of access to and from the Site to the Contractor on time.
notes: Employer's access obligation; breach is a CE.

text: The Contractor shall provide a performance bond in the sum of 7.5% of the Contract Sum within 14 days of execution of the Contract.
notes: Performance bond at 10% is at the higher end; bond cost is a contractor risk.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Project Manager certifies a payment in the amount due within one week of each assessment date.
notes: Payment certification timeline; standard NEC4.

text: SPECIAL CONDITIONS OF CONTRACT
notes: Document heading; modifications to general conditions.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Architect shall issue Interim Payment Certificates at the intervals stated in the Contract Particulars, and the Employer shall pay within 14 days of the due date.
notes: Clear payment timeline; HGCRA compliant.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall be liable for liquidated damages at the rate of USD 5,000 per day for every day of delay beyond the Completion Date, without any limit on the aggregate amount recoverable.
notes: Uncapped daily LADs in USD; creates severe and uninsurable financial exposure for Zimbabwean contractors.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Project Manager gives access to the Site to the Contractor on the access date stated in Contract Data Part 1.
notes: Standard access provision; employer obligation.

text: SECTION 4 – TESTING AND DEFECTS
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, hyperinflationary conditions that render the Contract commercially impossible to perform shall constitute a force majeure event.
notes: Hyperinflation as FM event; Zimbabwe-specific protection for contractor.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer may withhold payment of any amount due pending resolution of any dispute, without serving a pay less notice.
notes: Payment withholding without pay less notice undermines NEC4's payment transparency mechanisms.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, price escalation shall be calculated monthly using the CPIH index published by ZIMSTAT; all increases shall be paid in the same currency as the Contract Sum.
notes: Escalation clause with ZIMSTAT index; full price protection for contractor in inflationary environment.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer shall appoint an Employer's Agent to act on its behalf in administering the Contract.
notes: Standard appointment provision; defines contract administration structure.

text: 'Time for Completion' means the time for completing the Works or a Section as stated in the Contract Data, adjusted under Sub-Clause 8.5.
notes: Standard FIDIC completion time definition.

text: The parties agree that the final account shall be settled within 180 days of the issue of the Defects Certificate; any disputed items shall be referred to adjudication.
notes: 180-day final account settlement with adjudication backstop; reasonable overall.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Defects Liability Period for roofing works shall be 5 years from Practical Completion.
notes: Extended DLP for specialist trade; common on roofing and waterproofing.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall be entitled to recover finance charges and legal costs incurred in pursuing overdue payments as a debt due under this Contract.
notes: Express right to recover finance costs and legal fees; strengthens contractor's cash flow protection.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor warrants that it has independently investigated all site conditions, surrounding infrastructure and sub-surface geology, and accepts all risk therefrom without any entitlement to additional payment.
notes: Full unforeseeable ground risk on contractor; removes employer's implied obligation to disclose site information.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall not be entitled to loss of profit on omitted works where variations reduce the scope of the Contract.
notes: Exclusion of loss of profit on omissions is common but limits contractor's commercial protection.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall submit all CE quotations within 21 days; late submission means the PM's own assessment is binding on the Contractor without review.
notes: PM's assessment binding on late quotation; removes contractor's ability to challenge PM's figures.

text: The parties agree that certificates issued under this Contract shall not be taken as evidence that the Works have been completed in accordance with the Contract.
notes: Standard certification disclaimer.

text: SECTION 8 – RISKS AND INSURANCE
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, sub-contracting of portions of the Works exceeding 25% of the Contract Sum requires prior written consent from the Employer's Agent.
notes: Sub-contracting threshold consent requirement is commercially acceptable.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall register the project with the Zimbabwe Revenue Authority and comply with all ZIMRA withholding tax obligations.
notes: Tax compliance obligation; standard for Zimbabwe construction projects.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, interest shall accrue on certified but unpaid sums at LIBOR plus 2% from the due date for payment.
notes: Interest on overdue sums; LIBOR-linked rate is market standard.

text: SECTION 7 – TITLE
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Sub-Contractor shall not be entitled to payment for variations unless a signed Variation Order is produced prior to commencing the varied work; verbal instructions shall not create payment entitlement.
notes: Pre-variation-order condition precedent; practical impossibility on live construction sites denies contractors legitimate claims.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall correct all Defects within 7 days of notification regardless of their nature or complexity, failing which the Employer may employ others and recover the cost plus 50%.
notes: Impossibly short defect correction window with punitive 50% premium recovery.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Employer provides the Site and the means of access to and from the Site to the Contractor on time.
notes: Employer's access obligation; breach is a CE.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Completion Date is the date in the Accepted Programme for Completion, adjusted for compensation events.
notes: Completion Date definition; programme-linked.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer may withhold payment of any amount due pending resolution of any dispute, without serving a pay less notice.
notes: Payment withholding without pay less notice undermines NEC4's payment transparency mechanisms.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Architect shall issue Interim Payment Certificates at the intervals stated in the Contract Particulars, and the Employer shall pay within 14 days of the due date.
notes: Clear payment timeline; HGCRA compliant.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, any extension of time application by the Contractor must be made within 7 days of the occurrence of the relevant event, failing which the Contractor shall be deemed to have waived its entitlement.
notes: Extremely short notice period; strict waiver provision with no discretion for Engineer/Architect.

text: SECTION 5 – PAYMENT
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall repay all amounts received under interim certificates if the final account shows the total certified was overpaid, with interest at 8% above base rate.
notes: Retroactive overpayment recovery with punitive interest; creates cash flow uncertainty throughout project.

text: CORE CLAUSES
notes: NEC4 structural heading.

text: The parties agree that the Employer shall appoint an Employer's Agent to act on its behalf in administering the Contract.
notes: Standard appointment provision; defines contract administration structure.

text: It is hereby agreed that where the exchange rate moves by more than 5% against the Contractor between the Base Date and the payment date, the Contract Price shall be adjusted by the difference.
notes: Exchange rate fluctuation protection; strong safeguard for international contracts in Zimbabwe.

text: SECTION 4: CONTRACT PRICE AND PAYMENT
notes: Payment section heading.

text: The Sub-Contractor shall keep a copy of the OHSACT / PH Act on site at all times.
For the avoidance of doubt, where the Employer fails to give possession of the Site on the Date of Possession, such failure shall be a Relevant Matter entitling the Contractor to loss and expense.
notes: Contractor protected for employer's failure to give possession.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor is entitled to a compensation event where a physical condition encountered is one which an experienced contractor would have judged at the Contract Date to have a small chance of occurring.
notes: Unforeseeable ground conditions CE; standard NEC4 60.1(12) protection.

text: PART II – PARTICULAR CONDITIONS
notes: FIDIC particular conditions heading.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall maintain public roads used by construction traffic in a clean and tidy condition at all times.
notes: Road maintenance obligation; standard and reasonable.

text: In accordance with industry practice, all Contractor's equipment, materials and temporary works brought onto Site shall be deemed to be the property of the Employer for the duration of the Contract and shall not be removed without Employer's written consent.
notes: Deemed vesting of contractor's property; exposes contractor to loss if employer becomes insolvent or hostile.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor maintains the Site as free from rubbish and the Contractor's equipment as the Project Manager instructs.
notes: Site tidiness obligation; standard.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall be entitled to a pre-agreed sum for demobilisation costs payable on Practical Completion.
notes: Express demobilisation payment; reduces contractor's post-completion financial exposure.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall submit a final account within 90 days of Practical Completion.
notes: Final account submission obligation; standard.

text: In accordance with industry practice, payment applications must be submitted in the prescribed format using the Employer's software system; applications in any other format shall not constitute valid applications.
notes: Format condition precedent on payment applications; employer can reject valid applications on technical grounds.

text: In accordance with industry practice, where the Contract Sum is denominated in USD, all payments shall be made in USD or at the parallel market rate, whichever is more favourable to the Contractor.
notes: Parallel market rate protection; significant financial benefit for contractor in multi-currency Zimbabwe environment.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall indemnify the Employer against all third party claims arising from the Contractor's operations, including claims resulting from the Employer's acts or omissions.
notes: Indemnity extends to employer's own acts; removes employer's responsibility for its own negligence.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, disputes shall first be referred to a technical expert for determination within 30 days; expert determination shall be binding unless overturned by arbitration.
notes: Expert determination as first-tier; efficient mechanism for technical disputes.

text: ANNEXURE A: CONTRACT DATA
notes: Contract data annexure heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Defects Liability Period shall be 12 months from the date of Practical Completion during which the Contractor shall rectify defects notified by the Employer within 21 days.
notes: Standard DLP provision; 21-day notice-to-rectify window is reasonable.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, all variations must be supported by a Variation Order signed by the Employer's Representative before work commences, except in cases of urgency where verbal instruction may be confirmed in writing within 48 hours.
notes: VO requirement with urgency exception; balanced but requires contractor diligence.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall indemnify the Employer against all costs, claims and expenses arising from the Contractor's presence on site, including claims by third parties, whether or not caused by the Contractor's negligence.
notes: Negligence-free indemnity; contractor becomes insurer for all site-related third party claims.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, if the Project Manager does not assess a compensation event within the time allowed, the Contractor's quotation is treated as accepted.
notes: Deemed acceptance of contractor's CE quotation on PM inaction; contractor-favourable mechanism.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer shall provide survey benchmarks and reference points for the Contractor's use in setting out the Works.
notes: Employer's setting-out support obligation; standard.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall be entitled to an extension of time and additional cost for delays caused by unavailability of foreign currency for imported materials, provided such unavailability is documented by a bank.
notes: Forex-induced delay recognised as relief event; critical protection in Zimbabwe's USD-constrained environment.

text: In accordance with industry practice, where the Employer fails to provide something they are to provide by the date stated, the Contractor is entitled to a compensation event for delay and additional cost.
notes: Employer supply chain risk passed back to employer; contractor protected for late employer-furnished items.

text: CLAUSE 1 – GENERAL PROVISIONS
notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: If the Sub-Contractor's estate is sequestered or if, being a company, it is placed in voluntary or compulsory liquidation or under judicial management or if the Sub-Contractor effects a compromise or compound with his general body of creditors or if any of his assets are attached pursuant to warrant or execution issued by any Court of Law, or if he commits an act of insolvency.
notes: Clause creates significant risk for subcontractor; one-sided in favour of contractor/employer.

text: SECTION 2 – CONTRACTOR'S OBLIGATIONS
notes: Contract section heading.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Commencement Date shall be the date stated in the Letter of Acceptance, and the Contractor shall commence Works within 7 days thereof.
notes: Standard commencement obligation.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer's right to terminate shall not arise where the alleged breach is caused by the Employer's own act or omission.
notes: Employer's concurrent breach prevents termination; protects contractor where employer contributes to the breach.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall be entitled to apply for interim payment on a monthly basis throughout the Contract Period.
notes: Monthly interim payment right; standard cash flow provision.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor is entitled to a compensation event where the Project Manager gives an instruction to stop work, and the stop lasts more than 13 weeks, and no further instruction is given.
notes: Prolonged stop-work triggers CE; protects contractor's standing costs.

text: The Contractor will be charged for material wastage.
notes: Clause creates significant risk for subcontractor; one-sided in favour of contractor/employer.

text: 'Time for Completion' means the time for completing the Works or a Section as stated in the Contract Data, adjusted under Sub-Clause 8.5.
notes: Standard FIDIC completion time definition.

text: The Employer shall pay the final balance of the Contract Sum within 48 hours of the issue of the Final Certificate.
notes: Clear final payment timeline; balanced obligation.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall keep and maintain proper books of account relating to the Works and shall make them available for inspection by the Employer's Quantity Surveyor on 48 hours' notice.
notes: Open-book accounting obligation; some administrative burden but not unusual.

text: SECTION 10: TERMINATION
notes: Termination section heading.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall not commence any variation until the value has been pre-agreed in writing; no post-hoc valuation claims shall be entertained.
notes: Pre-agreed VO pricing condition precedent; prevents urgent variations proceeding without agreed cost.

text: It is hereby agreed that any variation instructed by the Employer shall be valued by the Employer's Quantity Surveyor, whose valuation shall be final and binding on the Contractor.
notes: Removes contractor's right to challenge variation valuations through dispute resolution.

text: SECTION 6 – INJURY, DAMAGE AND INSURANCE
notes: Contract section heading.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer may instruct the Contractor to accelerate the programme; the Contractor shall comply and any claim for acceleration costs shall be submitted within 7 days of the instruction.
notes: 7-day acceleration claim window is impractical; employer can demand acceleration without pre-agreeing cost.

text: The parties agree that the Defects Liability Period for roofing works shall be 5 years from Practical Completion.
notes: Extended DLP for specialist trade; common on roofing and waterproofing.

text: The parties agree that the Employer shall give the Contractor possession of the Site on the Date of Possession stated in the Contract Particulars.
notes: Standard possession provision; balanced obligation on employer.

text: In accordance with industry practice, where the Employer fails to provide the Site by the agreed date, the Contractor is entitled to an extension of time and Cost plus profit.
notes: Full recovery (time + cost + profit) for employer's failure to provide site; strong contractor protection.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall not terminate the Contract for any reason without first obtaining a court order declaring the Employer to be in breach.
notes: Court order condition precedent to contractor termination; practically removes contractor's self-help termination right.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall be responsible for obtaining all statutory approvals, permits and licences required for the Works at its own cost, and delays arising from the permitting process shall not entitle the Contractor to any extension of time.
notes: Contractor bears permitting risk in a jurisdiction with unpredictable regulatory timelines.

text: The Contractor shall maintain a site diary recording daily weather conditions, labour deployed and progress of the Works.
notes: Record-keeping obligation; protects both parties in claims situations.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall provide training to the Employer's maintenance staff on the operation of installed plant and equipment.
notes: Training obligation; standard on M&E and infrastructure contracts.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Defects Liability Period shall be 12 months from the date of Practical Completion during which the Contractor shall rectify defects notified by the Employer within 21 days.
notes: Standard DLP provision; 21-day notice-to-rectify window is reasonable.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, all payments shall be subject to deduction of withholding tax at the rate applicable under Zimbabwe Revenue Authority regulations.
notes: ZIMRA withholding tax deduction; statutory requirement reducing contractor's net receipts.

text: The Contract shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the law of England and Wales.
notes: Governing law clause; standard for UK-based JCT contract.

text: CLAUSE 1 – GENERAL PROVISIONS
notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, either party may terminate if a force majeure event continues for more than 180 days, with the Contractor entitled to all costs incurred to the termination date.
notes: FM termination after 180 days with full cost recovery; balanced and reasonable.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall complete the Works within the time stated even if access, materials or instructions are delayed; no extension of time shall be granted for any cause whatsoever.
notes: Absolute completion obligation with no EOT; imposes strict liability for delays not within contractor's control.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall be liable for consequential, indirect and economic loss suffered by the Employer arising out of any breach, delay or defect, without financial cap.
notes: Uncapped consequential loss liability is commercially unacceptable and uninsurable.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer may deduct the cost of rectifying any defect from amounts otherwise due to the Contractor without first notifying the Contractor or giving it an opportunity to rectify.
notes: Deduction without notice or opportunity to cure; denies contractor natural justice.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor is entitled to Exceptional Event relief for epidemics, natural catastrophes, wars, embargoes and acts of government.
notes: Broad exceptional event relief including government action; relevant for Zimbabwe sovereign risk.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, all variations must be supported by a Variation Order signed by the Employer's Representative before work commences, except in cases of urgency where verbal instruction may be confirmed in writing within 48 hours.
notes: VO requirement with urgency exception; balanced but requires contractor diligence.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall be liable for the full cost of any re-testing or re-inspection required whether or not the works are found to be compliant upon re-inspection.
notes: Contractor pays re-inspection costs even when subsequent test passes; creates financial deterrent to exercising inspection rights.

text: SECTION 3 – TIME
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall comply with all applicable laws, regulations and permit conditions throughout the Contract.
notes: General regulatory compliance; standard obligation.

text: The Contractor shall provide a performance guarantee of 7.5% of the Contract Sum from an acceptable financial institution within 14 days of Contract execution.
notes: 10% performance guarantee; market norm in Zimbabwe but creates liquidity demand.

text: The Contractor provides a 10-year structural warranty for all reinforced concrete works in accordance with the relevant British Standards.
notes: Long-form structural warranty; common on residential and commercial developments.

text: SECTION 4 – TESTING AND DEFECTS
notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, where the Engineer fails to respond within the period for reply, the Contractor may treat the absence of response as approval of the communication.
notes: Deemed approval on engineer inaction; contractor can proceed on basis of non-response.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Employer pays the amount due within 3 weeks of each assessment date.
notes: Standard NEC4 payment period; clear employer obligation.

text: 'Base Date' means the date 42 days before the latest date for submission of the Tender.
notes: Standard FIDIC Base Date definition; triggers change-in-law risk allocation.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer shall nominate and pay directly any nominated subcontractors; the Contractor shall be responsible for coordinating nominated subcontractors but not for their defaults.
notes: Nominated subcontractor mechanism; limits contractor's liability for employer-selected specialists.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Employer's Representative may attend any site operations, tests or inspections at any time without prior notice.
notes: Access right for employer's representative; standard.

text: SECTION 8: FORCE MAJEURE
notes: Force majeure section heading.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, any variation instructed by the Employer shall be valued by the Employer's Quantity Surveyor, whose valuation shall be final and binding on the Contractor.
notes: Removes contractor's right to challenge variation valuations through dispute resolution.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, where the Employer issues an omission instruction, the Contractor shall be entitled to recover its preliminaries and time-related costs attributable to the omitted work.
notes: Preliminaries recovery on omissions; protects contractor's fixed cost recovery on scope reductions.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall take full responsibility for the adequacy, stability and safety of all site operations.
notes: Safety responsibility; fundamental contractor duty.

text: SECTION 2: DEFINITIONS
notes: Definitions section heading.

text: ANNEXURE C: PERFORMANCE BOND FORM
notes: Performance bond form heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Employer shall pay the Contractor within 56 days of the Engineer issuing a Payment Certificate.
notes: 56-day payment period; on the longer side under FIDIC Red Book.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Commencement Date shall be the date stated in the Letter of Acceptance, and the Contractor shall commence Works within 7 days thereof.
notes: Standard commencement obligation.

notes: Standard definition; administrative.

notes: Standard ER definition.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor's liability for latent defects shall survive the Defects Certificate and continue for 6 years from the date of Practical Completion.
notes: Latent defect liability period; standard under Limitation Act 1980.

text: It is hereby agreed that no payment shall be made for materials off-site unless the Contractor provides proof of insurance, vesting certificate and list of materials.
notes: Off-site materials payment conditions; reasonable certification requirements.

text: The Contractor shall provide training to the Employer's maintenance staff on the operation of installed plant and equipment.
notes: Training obligation; standard on M&E and infrastructure contracts.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall provide training to the Employer's maintenance staff on the operation of installed plant and equipment.
notes: Training obligation; standard on M&E and infrastructure contracts.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor is to assess the compensation event using the Shorter Schedule of Cost Components where the work has not been done.
notes: SSCC use for prospective CEs; requires forecast which introduces uncertainty.

notes: Force majeure definition includes Zimbabwe-specific risks (forex, civil unrest); balanced provision.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall be liable for consequential, indirect and economic loss suffered by the Employer arising out of any breach, delay or defect, without financial cap.
notes: Uncapped consequential loss liability is commercially unacceptable and uninsurable.

For the avoidance of doubt, force majeure events include epidemic, pandemic, earthquake, flood, fire, war, civil commotion, sanctions, government action and any other event beyond the reasonable control of the affected party.
notes: Broad force majeure definition including government action; well-suited to Zimbabwe's political risk environment.

text: The parties agree that the Employer's obligation to provide information on time is a condition precedent to the Contractor's obligation to maintain the programme; late information shall entitle the Contractor to an extension of time.
notes: Information provision as condition precedent; strong protection where employer delays drawings or instructions.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall be responsible for the security of the entire Site, including all Employer-stored materials, and shall indemnify the Employer for any theft or loss on Site.
notes: Security indemnity for employer's materials; imposes insurance cost for employer's own property.

text: CLAUSE 3 – THE ENGINEER
notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, cOVID-19 and any variant thereof shall not constitute a force majeure event for the purposes of this Contract.
notes: Pandemic exclusion from force majeure; leaves contractor exposed to pandemic disruption costs.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Employer's Representative may attend any site operations, tests or inspections at any time without prior notice.
notes: Access right for employer's representative; standard.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall comply with all applicable laws and regulations in carrying out the Works.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor is to give an early warning of any matter which could increase the total Defined Cost, delay Completion or impair performance of the works in use.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall not be entitled to any additional payment or extension of time arising from any ground conditions, howsoever arising, whether foreseeable or unforeseeable.
notes: Full unforeseeable ground risk passed to contractor without any site investigation warranty from employer.

text: WHEREAS the Contractor has been awarded a contract (hereinafter referred to as "the Principal Contract") by ZIMPLATS, SMELTER CONCENTRATOR (hereinafter referred to as "the Employer") for the Construction of works for the SMC Concentrator TSF Phase 2A Footprint Civil and Earthworks Project and WHEREAS the Contractor desires to have executed the Works of which particulars are set out in Annexure "A" hereto (hereinafter referred to as "the Sub-Contract Works") and which form part of the Works comprised in and to be executed in accordance with the Principal Contract and any authorised variations of the Sub-Contract Works.
notes: Heading or procedural clause; no material risk allocation.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall not make any claim for loss and expense arising from delay to the Works unless a detailed programme showing critical path analysis is submitted with the claim.
notes: Critical path programme as condition precedent to loss and expense; places impossible evidential burden on contractor.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall submit a construction programme in bar chart form within 21 days of the Commencement Date and update it monthly.
notes: Programme obligation; standard practice in Zimbabwe.

text: It is hereby agreed that where the Employer fails to give possession of the Site on the Date of Possession, such failure shall be a Relevant Matter entitling the Contractor to loss and expense.
notes: Contractor protected for employer's failure to give possession.

text: 'Defects Notification Period' means the period for notifying defects in the Works or a Section as stated in the Contract Data.
notes: Standard FIDIC DNP definition.

text: It is hereby agreed that in the event of any dispute, the Employer's decision shall be final and binding; the Contractor shall continue to perform the Works pending any subsequent arbitration.
notes: Employer as sole arbiter of disputes; no independent decision-making; contractor must perform under adverse decisions.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall pay liquidated and ascertained damages at the rate stated in the Contract Particulars for every week or part week during which Practical Completion is delayed beyond the Completion Date, without limit as to time or amount.
notes: Uncapped LADs with no causal link required; significant financial exposure for contractor.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall rectify all defects notified during the Defects Liability Period at its own cost, including defects caused by fair wear and tear, acts of God or third parties.
notes: Warranty extends to fair wear and tear and force majeure damage; far beyond standard contractor warranty obligations.

text: The Contractor shall submit as-built drawings within 14 days of Practical Completion.
notes: As-built record submission obligation; standard completion deliverable.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor warrants that it has independently investigated all site conditions, surrounding infrastructure and sub-surface geology, and accepts all risk therefrom without any entitlement to additional payment.
notes: Full unforeseeable ground risk on contractor; removes employer's implied obligation to disclose site information.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, provisional sums included in the Contract Bills shall only be expended on the written instruction of the Employer's Representative.
notes: Provisional sum control mechanism; standard QS practice.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor warrants that all rates in the Schedule of Rates are adequate and shall not be entitled to any adjustment for unforeseeable increases in cost.
notes: Fixed-rate warranty removes all cost risk adjustment mechanisms; dangerous in Zimbabwe's volatile material cost environment.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer's right to terminate shall not arise where the alleged breach is caused by the Employer's own act or omission.
notes: Employer's concurrent breach prevents termination; protects contractor where employer contributes to the breach.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall not be entitled to a compensation event for delay caused by any action or inaction of a subcontractor or supplier.
notes: Sub-contractor delay risk entirely on contractor with no pass-through to employer.

text: The parties agree that provisional sums included in the Contract Bills shall only be expended on the written instruction of the Employer's Representative.
notes: Provisional sum control mechanism; standard QS practice.

text: The Contract shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the law of England and Wales.
notes: Governing law clause; standard for UK-based JCT contract.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, cOVID-19 and any variant thereof shall not constitute a force majeure event for the purposes of this Contract.
notes: Pandemic exclusion from force majeure; leaves contractor exposed to pandemic disruption costs.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall comply with all applicable laws and regulations in carrying out the Works.
notes: General compliance obligation; standard and uncontroversial.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, where the final account value exceeds the Contract Sum by more than 15%, the Contractor shall be entitled to re-price preliminaries and time-related items.
notes: Preliminaries re-pricing right on substantial scope increase; protects contractor on poorly scoped projects.

text: 21. ELECTRONIC SIGNATURE
notes: Heading or procedural clause; no material risk allocation.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall provide adequate sanitary facilities for all site workers throughout the Contract Period.
notes: Site welfare obligation; standard and required by NSSA.

text: 'The Fee' means the tendered fee percentage applied to Defined Cost.
notes: Standard NEC4 fee definition.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, on termination of the Contract by the Employer for convenience, the Contractor shall be entitled to the full value of work executed, loss of profit on remaining work, and demobilisation costs.
notes: Full termination-for-convenience entitlement; contractor recovers profit on unexecuted work.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, where the Employer fails to give possession of the Site on the Date of Possession, such failure shall be a Relevant Matter entitling the Contractor to loss and expense.
notes: Contractor protected for employer's failure to give possession.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor warrants that all subcontractors and suppliers will perform their obligations; the Contractor shall be liable for all defaults of subcontractors as if they were the Contractor's own defaults.
notes: Full vicarious liability for subcontractor defaults; removes contractor's ability to claim against employer for nominated sub failures.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor is entitled to additional Cost if it encounters physical conditions which could not have been foreseen by an experienced contractor.
notes: Unforeseeable physical conditions relief; standard FIDIC 4.12 protection.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall maintain public roads used by construction traffic in a clean and tidy condition at all times.
notes: Road maintenance obligation; standard and reasonable.

text: SCHEDULE OF RATES AND QUANTITIES
notes: Pricing document heading.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer shall pay the Contractor a mobilisation advance of 15% of the Contract Sum, secured by an advance payment guarantee, within 30 days of contract execution.
notes: Mobilisation advance at 15%; strong cash flow support for contractor on project start.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall give written notice to the Architect within 28 days of the occurrence of a Relevant Event and shall provide particulars within a further 14 days.
notes: Standard EOT notice provisions; strict but not unreasonable.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor's liability shall not be limited under any circumstances; any contractual cap on liability is expressly excluded.
notes: Removal of any liability cap; contractor faces unlimited financial exposure.

text: The parties agree that price escalation shall be calculated monthly using the CPIH index published by ZIMSTAT; all increases shall be paid in the same currency as the Contract Sum.
notes: Escalation clause with ZIMSTAT index; full price protection for contractor in inflationary environment.

text: The parties agree that sub-contracting of portions of the Works exceeding 25% of the Contract Sum requires prior written consent from the Employer's Agent.
notes: Sub-contracting threshold consent requirement is commercially acceptable.

text: The Contractor shall provide a minimum of one team for each stage of construction.

Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor accepts all risk of currency fluctuation throughout the Contract Period; no adjustment mechanism applies to the Contract Sum in respect of exchange rate movements.
notes: Full currency risk transferred to contractor in long-duration international projects.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, on termination by the Contractor for Employer default, the Contractor shall be entitled to recover its costs, loss of profit on the remaining Works, and reasonable demobilisation costs.
notes: Full termination entitlement for contractor on employer default; standard JCT 8.9 protection.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor is to give an early warning of any matter which could increase the total Defined Cost, delay Completion or impair performance of the works in use.
notes: EW obligation is core NEC4 requirement; failure to warn may reduce CE entitlement.

text: The Contractor shall not be entitled to any uplift on the rates and prices in the Contract Bills for any variation, regardless of the nature, complexity or volume of the varied work.
notes: Fixed-rate variations with no uplift for changed conditions; erodes contractor margin on complex variations.

text: 'Practical Completion' means the stage at which the Works are practically complete as certified by the Architect.
notes: Standard definition; pivotal trigger for retention release and LAD cessation.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor provides a 10-year structural warranty for all reinforced concrete works in accordance with the relevant British Standards.
notes: Long-form structural warranty; common on residential and commercial developments.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, any payment certified by the Quantity Surveyor shall be subject to the Employer's approval before becoming due; the Employer may reduce the certified amount without limitation.
notes: Employer veto over QS certification; undermines independence of certification process.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer shall give the Contractor possession of the Site on the Date of Possession stated in the Contract Particulars.
notes: Standard possession provision; balanced obligation on employer.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Defects Liability Period shall be extended by the period of any defect rectification; a single recurring defect may extend the DLP indefinitely.
notes: Rolling DLP extension creates perpetual warranty exposure; no limit on total DLP duration.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Sub-Contractor shall not be entitled to payment for variations unless a signed Variation Order is produced prior to commencing the varied work; verbal instructions shall not create payment entitlement.
notes: Pre-variation-order condition precedent; practical impossibility on live construction sites denies contractors legitimate claims.

text: The parties agree that the Engineer shall respond to the Contractor's claim within 42 days of receiving the fully detailed claim; failure to respond shall be deemed a rejection.
notes: FIDIC 2017 Clause 20.2.5 response period; deemed rejection protects contractor's programme certainty.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall not terminate the Contract for any reason without first obtaining a court order declaring the Employer to be in breach.
notes: Court order condition precedent to contractor termination; practically removes contractor's self-help termination right.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor is entitled to a compensation event where the Employer or Others do not work within the times shown in the Accepted Programme.
notes: CE for Others' delay; contractor protected where employer-managed parties cause delay.

text: Any extension of time application by the Contractor must be made within 7 days of the occurrence of the relevant event, failing which the Contractor shall be deemed to have waived its entitlement.
notes: Extremely short notice period; strict waiver provision with no discretion for Engineer/Architect.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor is to provide the Project Manager with information which the Project Manager needs in order to issue a certificate.
notes: Information provision obligation; standard.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, where the Employer fails to provide something they are to provide by the date stated, the Contractor is entitled to a compensation event for delay and additional cost.
notes: Employer supply chain risk passed back to employer; contractor protected for late employer-furnished items.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, all Contractor's Equipment brought to Site shall be deemed to be exclusively intended for use on the Works and shall not be removed without the Engineer's consent.
notes: Deemed vesting of contractor's equipment; exposes contractor to loss on employer insolvency.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the losing party shall bear all costs of arbitration including the other party's legal fees assessed on an indemnity basis.
notes: Indemnity costs order by arbitrator; increases stakes of losing a dispute.

text: In this Contract, the following terms have the meanings given to them below unless the context otherwise requires.

text: CORE CLAUSES
notes: NEC4 structural heading.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Employer's Agent may issue instructions requiring the Contractor to open up work for inspection; where the work is found to be in accordance with the Contract, the cost shall be added to the Contract Sum.
notes: Cost recovery mechanism for compliant open-up is fair; risk of disruption remains.

text: SIGNED for and on behalf of THE EMPLOYER by a duly authorised representative:

text: It is hereby agreed that all variations must be supported by a Variation Order signed by the Employer's Representative before work commences, except in cases of urgency where verbal instruction may be confirmed in writing within 48 hours.
notes: VO requirement with urgency exception; balanced but requires contractor diligence.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, retention shall be held by the Employer in its general account and shall not attract interest; no obligation to place retention in a separate trust account arises.
notes: Retention is not ring-fenced; contractor has no protection if employer becomes insolvent.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall notify the Employer within 14 days of becoming aware of any circumstance that may give rise to a compensation event under clause 60.
notes: Notice obligation is standard; failure may reduce entitlement in practice.

text: It is hereby agreed that no payment shall be made for materials off-site unless the Contractor provides proof of insurance, vesting certificate and list of materials.
notes: Off-site materials payment conditions; reasonable certification requirements.

text: The parties agree that any defect that recurs within 12 months of rectification shall be treated as a new defect with a fresh 12-month rectification warranty.
notes: Rolling warranty on recurring defects; creates perpetual exposure for systemic defects.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, either Party may terminate the Contractor's obligation to Provide the Works if the other Party has committed a reason stated in this Contract.
notes: Standard mutual termination for cause; balanced.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor warrants that all rates in the Schedule of Rates are adequate and shall not be entitled to any adjustment for unforeseeable increases in cost.
notes: Fixed-rate warranty removes all cost risk adjustment mechanisms; dangerous in Zimbabwe's volatile material cost environment.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Contractor shall ensure the Works are carried out in a good and workmanlike manner using materials of the quality specified.
notes: Standard workmanship obligation; fundamental but balanced.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor warrants that all rates in the Schedule of Rates are adequate and shall not be entitled to any adjustment for unforeseeable increases in cost.
notes: Fixed-rate warranty removes all cost risk adjustment mechanisms; dangerous in Zimbabwe's volatile material cost environment.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall not be entitled to a compensation event for delay caused by any action or inaction of a subcontractor or supplier.
notes: Sub-contractor delay risk entirely on contractor with no pass-through to employer.

notes: Payment section heading.

text: 'Compensation Event' means an event which entitles the Contractor to changes to the Prices and Completion Date.
notes: Standard NEC4 compensation event definition.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall comply with all local authority requirements, including those of the City of Harare/Bulawayo City Council, for excavation in public areas.
notes: Local authority compliance obligation; standard but requires advance planning.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall not be entitled to any uplift on the rates and prices in the Contract Bills for any variation, regardless of the nature, complexity or volume of the varied work.

text: CLAUSE 20 – EMPLOYER'S AND CONTRACTOR'S CLAIMS
notes: FIDIC section heading.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Project Manager issues a Completion Certificate when the Contractor has done all the work necessary to complete the works.
notes: Completion certification; standard milestone.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall submit all CE quotations within 21 days; late submission means the PM's own assessment is binding on the Contractor without review.
notes: PM's assessment binding on late quotation; removes contractor's ability to challenge PM's figures.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall not be entitled to additional cost arising from a force majeure event; its sole entitlement is an extension of time.
notes: Time-only relief for force majeure; contractor bears standing cost during FM period.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer's Agent may issue instructions requiring the Contractor to open up work for inspection; where the work is found to be in accordance with the Contract, the cost shall be added to the Contract Sum.
notes: Cost recovery mechanism for compliant open-up is fair; risk of disruption remains.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor shall be liable for delay damages at the stated rate from the scheduled Completion Date, whether or not the delay was caused partly by the Employer.
notes: LADs run even where employer is partly responsible; no concurrent delay apportionment.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall not be entitled to any additional payment for compliance with any new law, regulation or standard enacted after the Contract Date.

text: The Contractor shall commence the Works on the Date of Possession and shall proceed regularly and diligently.

text: The parties agree that the Employer's Representative may attend any site operations, tests or inspections at any time without prior notice.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall be entitled to apply for interim payment on a monthly basis throughout the Contract Period.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor shall submit a final account within 90 days of Practical Completion.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor is entitled to additional Cost if it encounters physical conditions which could not have been foreseen by an experienced contractor.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, where the Project Manager gives an instruction changing the Scope after the Contract Date, all additional Defined Cost and time is recoverable as a compensation event.

text: The parties agree that all payments shall be subject to deduction of withholding tax at the rate applicable under Zimbabwe Revenue Authority regulations.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, where changes in Laws after the Base Date increase the Contractor's Costs, the Contract Price shall be adjusted accordingly.

text: This SUB-CONTRACT is made and entered into between Masimba Holdings Limited of 44 Tilbury Road, Willowvale, Harare, Zimbabwe, (hereinafter referred to as "the Main Contractor") and RAM Mining Solutions of 7 Watermeyer Road, Harare, Zimbabwe, hereinafter referred to as "the Sub-Contractor").

notes: NEC4 section heading.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer shall have the right to vary the scope of Works by up to 50% without any adjustment to the unit rates or time for completion.

notes: Preliminaries recovery on omissions; protects contractor's fixed cost recovery on scope reductions.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, where the Engineer fails to respond within the period for reply, the Contractor may treat the absence of response as approval of the communication.
notes: Deemed approval on engineer inaction; contractor can proceed on basis of non-response.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Employer may suspend the Works for any reason for up to 60 days without this constituting a termination; during suspension the Contractor shall stand down without payment.
notes: Unpaid suspension right; contractor bears standing costs during employer-requested suspension.

notes: Sub-contracting threshold consent requirement is commercially acceptable.

notes: Quarterly payment cycle; creates severe cash flow hardship for contractor, particularly in Zimbabwe's inflation environment.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, an amount due is calculated at each assessment date by the Project Manager and Contractor jointly, or by the Project Manager alone if the Contractor does not attend.
notes: Joint assessment preferred; PM can proceed alone if contractor absent.

notes: Information provision as condition precedent; strong protection where employer delays drawings or instructions.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, if a force majeure event continues for more than 90 days, either party may terminate the Contract by giving 14 days' notice; the Contractor shall be paid for work completed.
notes: Bilateral termination right on prolonged force majeure; contractor recovers value of work done.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor accepts the risk of any event which the Contractor could have foreseen at the Contract Date and which affects the Contractor's ability to Provide the Works.
notes: Ouster of compensation events for foreseeable risk; broad contractor risk assumption.

notes: Full unforeseeable ground risk passed to contractor without any site investigation warranty from employer.

notes: Governing law clause; standard for UK-based JCT contract.

notes: Full termination-for-convenience entitlement; contractor recovers profit on unexecuted work.

notes: Change-in-law risk on employer; contractor protected from sovereign risk after Base Date.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall be responsible for obtaining all statutory approvals, permits and licences required for the Works at its own cost, and delays arising from the permitting process shall not entitle the Contractor to any extension of time.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, all Intellectual Property created during the Works shall vest exclusively in the Employer upon creation without further payment to the Contractor.
notes: IP assignment without compensation; contractor loses rights to proprietary methods and designs.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer may at any time instruct the removal of the Contractor's key personnel, and the Contractor shall have no right to object or seek compensation for the disruption caused.
notes: Employer can disrupt contractor's team without justification or compensation.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, if the Contractor fails to comply with a Time for Completion, the Employer may after notice reduce the Performance Security proportionally.
notes: Bond reduction on delay; some risk but subject to notice and proportionality.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall be liable for the full cost of any re-testing or re-inspection required whether or not the works are found to be compliant upon re-inspection.
notes: Contractor pays re-inspection costs even when subsequent test passes; creates financial deterrent to exercising inspection rights.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Defects Liability Period shall be extended by the period of any defect rectification; a single recurring defect may extend the DLP indefinitely.
notes: Rolling DLP extension creates perpetual warranty exposure; no limit on total DLP duration.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Project Manager notifies the Contractor of each Defect as soon as he finds it.
notes: PM's notification obligation; balanced duty.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor obtains required approvals from Others and notifies the Project Manager before starting work which needs such approval.
notes: Third party approval obligation; standard.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Contractor's liability for latent defects shall survive the Defects Certificate and continue for 6 years from the date of Practical Completion.
notes: Latent defect liability period; standard under Limitation Act 1980.

text: The Contract shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the law of England and Wales.
notes: Governing law clause; standard for UK-based JCT contract.

text: The parties agree that the Employer pays the amount due within 3 weeks of each assessment date.
notes: Standard NEC4 payment period; clear employer obligation.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall be entitled to a pre-agreed sum for demobilisation costs payable on Practical Completion.
notes: Express demobilisation payment; reduces contractor's post-completion financial exposure.

text: The parties agree that the Works shall be carried out in accordance with the drawings, specifications and Bill of Quantities forming part of this Contract.
notes: Fundamental obligation to comply with contract documents; standard.

text: In accordance with industry practice, payment applications must be submitted in the prescribed format using the Employer's software system; applications in any other format shall not constitute valid applications.
notes: Format condition precedent on payment applications; employer can reject valid applications on technical grounds.

text: The parties agree that the Employer shall provide the Contractor with access to borehole water on site at no charge for construction use.
notes: Employer-provided site water; reduces contractor's site overhead.

text: The Employer's Agent may issue instructions requiring the Contractor to open up work for inspection; where the work is found to be in accordance with the Contract, the cost shall be added to the Contract Sum.
notes: Cost recovery mechanism for compliant open-up is fair; risk of disruption remains.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Contractor shall maintain contingency resources and plans to mitigate force majeure events to the extent reasonably practicable.
notes: FM mitigation obligation; reasonable and standard.

text: Notwithstanding any other provision, variations shall be valued using the rates and prices in the Contract Bills where applicable, and where not applicable, at fair rates and prices to be agreed.
notes: Bilateral agreement mechanism for variation valuation; balanced but subject to dispute.

text: 'Employer's Representative' means the person appointed by the Employer to administer the Contract on its behalf.
notes: Standard ER definition.

text: In accordance with industry practice, the Contractor shall repay all amounts received under interim certificates if the final account shows the total certified was overpaid, with interest at 8% above base rate.
notes: Retroactive overpayment recovery with punitive interest; creates cash flow uncertainty throughout project.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, sub-contracting of portions of the Works exceeding 25% of the Contract Sum requires prior written consent from the Employer's Agent.
notes: Sub-contracting threshold consent requirement is commercially acceptable.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, all Contractor's equipment, materials and temporary works brought onto Site shall be deemed to be the property of the Employer for the duration of the Contract and shall not be removed without Employer's written consent.
notes: Deemed vesting of contractor's property; exposes contractor to loss if employer becomes insolvent or hostile.

text: It is hereby agreed that where a force majeure event affects both parties, each shall bear its own costs and no claim shall be made against the other.
notes: Bilateral cost-bearing on shared FM events; balanced loss allocation.

text: SECTION 1 – DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION
notes: Contract heading/section marker.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor is to give an early warning of any matter which could increase the total Defined Cost, delay Completion or impair performance of the works in use.
notes: EW obligation is core NEC4 requirement; failure to warn may reduce CE entitlement.

text: Subject to the terms of this Contract, the Employer's Representative shall have authority to issue instructions on behalf of the Employer in all matters relating to the administration of this Contract.
notes: Defines employer's representative authority; standard.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Employer may withhold any amount due to the Contractor pending settlement of any claim by the Employer, whether or not such claim is disputed or related to the current Works.
notes: Unrestricted withholding right; allows employer to use unrelated claims to delay payment indefinitely.

text: The parties agree that the Contractor shall comply with the Employer's site rules and environmental management plan as updated from time to time during the Contract Period.
notes: Compliance with evolving site rules creates some uncertainty but is standard on managed sites.

text: The parties agree that the Project Manager may give an instruction to stop or not start any work for safety or other urgent reason.
notes: Stop work instruction with underlying CE entitlement for contractor.

text: The Contractor shall keep and maintain proper books of account relating to the Works and shall make them available for inspection by the Employer's Quantity Surveyor on 48 hours' notice.
notes: Open-book accounting obligation; some administrative burden but not unusual.

text: It is hereby agreed that where the final account value exceeds the Contract Sum by more than 15%, the Contractor shall be entitled to re-price preliminaries and time-related items.
notes: Preliminaries re-pricing right on substantial scope increase; protects contractor on poorly scoped projects.

text: For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor shall provide training to the Employer's maintenance staff on the operation of installed plant and equipment.
notes: Training obligation; standard on M&E and infrastructure contracts.

text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Contractor accepts the risk of any event which the Contractor could have foreseen at the Contract Date and which affects the Contractor's ability to Provide the Works.
notes: Ouster of compensation events for foreseeable risk; broad contractor risk assumption.

text: In accordance with industry practice, disputes shall first be referred to a technical expert for determination within 30 days; expert determination shall be binding unless overturned by arbitration.
notes: Expert determination as first-tier; efficient mechanism for technical disputes.

text: It is hereby agreed that the Employer may instruct the Contractor to accelerate the programme; the Contractor shall comply and any claim for acceleration costs shall be submitted within 7 days of the instruction.
notes: 7-day acceleration claim window is impractical; employer can demand acceleration without pre-agreeing cost.

text: In accordance with industry practice, all disputes shall be resolved by arbitration in terms of the Arbitration Act [Chapter 7:15] of Zimbabwe; the arbitrator shall be appointed solely by the Employer.
notes: Employer-appointed arbitrator lacks independence; contrary to fair arbitration principles.
